CHAPTER 456 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled" An act to incorporatethe Central
TransferCompany ," approvedApril 16,1884.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled " An act to incorporate the
CentralTransferCompany ," approved April 16, 1884,
be, and the same ishereby, amended as follows: The
saidCentralTransfer Company is hereby authorized,
from time to time, at itsdiscretion, by a vote of a ma-
jorityof itsstockholders,at any regular meeting, or
any meeting called for the purpose, to increaseits
capitalstock to any amount not exceeding ($150,000)
one hundred and fiftythousand dollars. And it may ,
under the terms and upon the conditionsprovided in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 3
e
e. the thirdsectionof the said act of itsincorporation,
borrow money and issue itsbonds for any amount not
exceedingtheamount of itscapitalstock so increased.
§2 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved March 9,1888.

lowaycounty,therighttoappointa deputy.

trict,ofCallowaycounty, Kentucky, shallhave the

thedischargeof the dutiesof constableof saiddis-
trict.
§2.But saidappointment shallbe made only by
theconsentand approvalof the countycourtof Cal-
lowaycounty,in which courtsaiddeputy,beforehe
actsassuch,shalltake the oaths now required to be
administeredto constables .
§3.The principalshallbe responsibleon his bond
forallofficialactsof hisdeputy .
§4. Thisact shallbe in force from and afterits
passage.
Approved March 9, 1888.
CHAPTER 458 .
AN ACT toamendthecharterofthe LexingtonOpera-houseCom-
pany .
WHEREAS , Itisrepresented to the General Assem-
blyof the Commonwealth of Kentucky , that forthe
buildingand completion of its opera-house, at Lex-
ington,the said company , in excess of the capital
stocksubscribedand paid in, have expended about

4 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
fifteenthousand dollars, which sum is now a debt
againstit, and said company are desirous of making
provision for the payment of said indebtedness, by
the issueof preferredstock of said company , redeem-
able as hereinafter provided ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That saidopera-house company be, and it is
hereby, authorizedand empowered to issuepreferred
stock in said company not to exceed fifteenthousand
($15,000) dollars, in shares of one hundred dollars
each , which shall be a preferred debt and a lien upon
the property of the company , the proceeds of which
shall be used to pay the indebtedness aforesaid.
Upon said shares of stock, which shall be denomi-
nated preferredstock,the company shall pay a semi-
annual dividend of three per cent. on the firstday of
April and October. But said company has the right
to redeem saidstock on the firstday of April or Oс-
tober in any year , by paying the par value of same
and the interest that may have accrued thereon to
the date of redemption ; and this right to redeem
said stock, and the time within which it shall be
exercised, shall be indorsed on the certificatesbe-
fore they are issued or delivered.
§ 2. This act shall be in full force from and after
its passage .
Approved March 9, 1888.
CHAPTER 459 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe MethodistProtestantChurch for theTen-
nesseeDistrict,which embracesa partof Kentucky.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the annual conference of the Methodist
Protestant, Tennessee District, which embraces a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 5
part of the State of Kentucky , is hereby incorpo-
rated ; and by that name may sue and be sued,plead
and be impleaded , and hold property, real, personal
or mixed , and that the capitalstock of said confer-
ence shall not exceed fivehundred thousand ($500,000)
dollars .
§ 2. Be itfurther enacted, That the affairsof said
corporation may be managed by the annual confer-
ence of the Methodist Protestant Church , Tennessee
District, or any seven persons said conference may
elect or appoint, said persons to be elected or ap-
pointed annually by said conference, and shall con-
tinue in officeuntil their successorsare appointed,
one of whom shall be electedby said seven persons
president of theirbody .
§ 3. Be itfurther enacted, That the property which
said conference is authorized to hold shall be applied
to said religious, charitable, literaryor missionary
purposes as said annual conference may designate:
Provided , Said applicationof said property or funds
shall not be in violation of the Constitution or laws
of this State or of the United States .
§ 4. Be itfurther enacted, That said corporation
may hold property of any kind in trust for such
religious, charitable, literary or missionary purposes
as the donor or testatormay designate , not inconsist-
ent with the Constitution or laws of this State or of
the United States, and execute said trust.
§ 5. Be itfurther enacted, That said corporation
shallhave the power to appoint a treasurerof said
corporation, eitherof their own body or any other
person, and take bond and securityfor the faithful
performance of his duties, and impose such penalties
as they may deem just, not contrary to the laws of
this Commonwealth .
§ 6. Be itfurther enacted, That a majority of di-
rectors,authorizedto be electedor appointed by said
conference,shallconstitutea quorum to do business;
and saidcorporationshallhave power to sell,by the

6 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
usual mode of conveyance, any property they may
hold in furtheranceof the objectsherein authorized.
§ 7. The present board of directorsconsistsof the
following persons, viz: B. F. Duggan , Jos. Camper ,
J. S. Chandler, M. F. Thompson , J. P. Warner , W.
H. Moore and J. N. Howard .
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 9,1888 .
CHAPTER 460 .
AN ACT toenablethe Newport and Licking,theOld StateRoad, and
theAlexandriaand LickingTurnpike Companies,in Campbellcounty'
to consolidate,or to contractwith each other to authorizethe erection
of a toll-gate and the collectionof tollsfor each other, and to fixthe
rate of toll in certain cases.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Newport and Licking Turnpike Com-
pany ,the Old StateRoad Turnpike Company ,and the
Alexandria and Licking Turnpike Company , be, and
they arehereby, empowered ,by and with the consent
of four-fifths in interest of the stockholders in said
companies respectively, to so consolidate, under the
name of " The Newport , Licking and Alexandria
Turnpike Company ," as to enable the lattercom-
pany to have and control one continuous and un-
broken road from Newport to Alexandria in said
county, the Old State Road Turnpike Company to
retainitspresentname and organization, except such
part thereofas is essentialto and becomes a part of
said continuous road from Newport to Alexandria ; or
said companies,by and with such consent, may buy ,
sell, lease, or otherwise contract to or with each other ,
in such way and to such an extent, in respectof their
severalroads or partsthereof, as, in the judgment of
said four-fifthsinterest, may best promote the inter-
ests of said roads and of the public .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 7
§ 2. That such consolidatedcompany , or said com-
panies respectively, shall have the right, where the
tollis now lessthan one cent, to charge one cent.
§ 3. Said Old State Road Turnpike Company shall
have the right to erect a toll-gate at a point within a
mile of itsintersectionwith the road of the present
Alexandria and Licking Turnpike Company .
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage.
Approved March 9,1888.
CHAPTER 461 .
AN ACT to legalizeand ratifythe subscriptionof the Boyle county

pany .
WHEREAS , At the October term , 1886, of the Boyle

fore the making of said subscription, been fully au-
thorizedand empowered by law to order and make the
same .

Approved March 9,1888.

CHAPTER 462 .
AN ACT toamend thecharterof the ElizavilleCemetery Company ,of
Elizaville,Fleming county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That section four of the charter of the Elizaville
Cemetery Company be, and the same is hereby, so
amended as to read as follows, viz :
§1 . When a burial lot is purchased and payment
made therefor, the trusteesshall cause a certificate,
under the sealof the corporation, to be issued in the
name of the purchaser,the same to be signed by the
chairman and secretary,which shallvestthe purchaser
with title. This titleshall be transferable according
to such rules and regulations as may be prescribed by
the by -laws of the corporation, but in no other man-
ner ; but shall descend or pass by devise as other real
estate. The company shallhave a lienon any lotsold
and not paid for in full for the purchase money or any
unpaid part thereof. Such lots shall never be used
forany other purpose than buriallots;and ,ifapplied
to any other use,the titleshall revert to the corpora-
tion .
Approved March 9,1888.
CHAPTER 463 .
AN ACT to authorizethe countycourtof Pike countyto issuebonds
forthe purpose of building a court-house and officestherein.
WHEREAS , At the October term , 1887, the court
of claims of Pike county, by an order of record,
unanimously requested the Legislature to pass an
act enablingthe county court to issue bonds for the
payment to the buildersfor the building of a new

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 9
court-house and officestherein, for Pike county ;
therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county judge of Pike county is
hereby empowered and directed to issue bonds for
an amount not to exceed twenty -five thousand dol-
lars, with interest-bearing coupons attached, payable
to the bearer or order, at the CatlettsburgNational
Bank or Ashland National Bank , both of Boyd
county, Kentucky ; said bonds to issuein sums not
less than one hundred nor more than one thousand
dollarseach, to be payable in such sums as will de-
fray said indebtedness of twenty -one thousand and
fivehundred dollars within a period of thirty years .
The firstpayment to be made within one year, and
then one to be due for each succeeding year there-
after, until said indebtedness is paid, and so as to
make the payment for each year equal or nearly
equal. Said bonds are each to bear interestat the
rateof six per cent. per annum from their date until
paid, and said interestto be paid at one of said
banks semi -annually ; said bonds shallbe signed by
thecounty judge of Pike county and countersigned
by the clerk of the Pike county court, with his offi-
cialseal attached. The judge may sell said bonds
attheirpar value : Provided , Before he may do so
he shall execute a bond , before the clerk of the
Pike circuitcourt, with good security, in the sum
equal to the amount of the said bonds , or he may
deliver said bonds to the contractor or builder of
thecourt-house , without executing said bond ; said
builderto receive them at their par value ; and if
saidbuilder does receiveand negotiate said bonds ,
itshall be in satisfactionof his present contract
for the building of said court-house .
§2. It shall be the duty of the county court of
levyof Pike county, annuallyto levy a tax of not
exceeding twenty -five cents per annum on the hun

10 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dred dollars on all the tax -payers of Pike county ,
to liquidate said bonds and their interest as they be-
come due . The levy to be made under the general
laws upon that subject.
§3 . It shallbe the duty of the sheriffof Pike
county to collectsaidtaxes which may be thus levied,
and pay the same over to the county judge of Pike
county, and shall be responsiblethereforeon his offi-
cial bonds .
§ 4. It ismade the duty of the county judge to re-
ceive said money from the sheriffand deposit the
same in the bank in which said bonds may be made
payable, as said bonds and interestsmay become due ,
for which money he shall be responsiblefor on his
officialbond .
§5 . The county judge is hereby directedand em -
powered to use the money , when collectedby the
sheriff, collectedfrom the levy of fifteencents on the
hundred dollars' worth of property made at the Oс-
tober term, 1887, of the Pike county court of levy,
forthe purpose of redeeming the firstbond due and
the interest thereon due .
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 9, 1888 .

CHAPTER 468 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Lawrenceburg ColoredCemetery Company .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Thomas Burras , H. C. Harris , Matt . Elli-
sien, Henry Brown , and General Carter, and their
successors, be, and they are hereby , constituted a
body -corporate, under the name and style of the
Lawrenceburg Colored Cemetery Company ; and by
that name they shallhave power to contractand be
contracted with , sue and be sued , use a common seal,
make allnecessary by -laws , rules and regulations, for
itsgovernment not inconsistentwith the law and the
provisionshereof, and to do such other things as are
incident to such a corporation and necessary to effect
the object and purpose thereof, which is to provide a
suitable and permanent cemetery near Lawrenceburg ,
in Anderson county, Kentucky ,for the burial of the
colored dead .
§ 2. Said corporationshallhave the power to take,
acquire and hold, by purchase,giftor devise,for the
purpose aforesaid, any quantity of land in Anderson
county, near Lawrenceburg , Kentucky , not exceeding
twenty acres, and receive a conveyance of the same in
itscorporate name ; which land, when so conveyed ,
shallbe held by said corporationfor a cemetery, and
shallnever be alienatedor used by said corporation
forany other purpose ; and said land and itsappur

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 25
tenances shall be forever free and exempt from all
executions, attachments and other legal process, and
from any charge,liabilityor assignment to or for any
public purpose inconsistentwith its use as a ceme .
tery, to which purpose and use it is hereby forever
dedicated .
§ 3. Said corporationshallcause a plator plan to be
made of the land so acquired by it, dividingsaid land
into avenues ,walks and burial lots, and designating
saidavenues and walks by name , and said buriallots
by number ,which plat or plan shall be recorded on
the books of said corporation. After said land is
thus laid off, the corporationshall have power to sell
saidburiallots,in such manner and on such terms as
may be prescribedby the board of directorsof said
corporation.
§ 4. Each owner of a lotin said cemetery, upon the
productionof a receiptin fullfor the priceof the lot
purchased by him from the actingtreasurerof the cor-
poration, shallbe entitledto a conveyance of said lot,
which conveyance shall be made by a certificateof
the board and countersigned by the secretaryof the
corporation, specifying that such purchaser is the
owner of such lot or lots, which certificateshall be re-
corded in the clerk's office of the Anderson county
court; and such certificateshallinvestthe fee-simple-
rightand titlein and to such lot or lotsin the pur-
chaser, his heirsor assigns,forever,and shallhave the
same effectfor alllegalpurposes as deeds duly exe ·
cutedand recorded; but saidlotsshallbe held by the
purchasers thereof subjectto the by -laws, rules and
regulations,of the corporationand itsboard of direct-
ors; and said lots shall never be used by the pur-
chaser for any other purpose than that of burial lots,
and ifappliedto any otherpurpose,the rightand title
thereof shallrevert to the corporation .
§ 5. Each person receivinga certificateas aforesaid
for one or more of said lots shall thereby become and
be consideredthereafteramember of said corporation,

26 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and he or she shall be entitled to a vote in all meet-
ings of the corporation .
§ 6. The affairsand business of the corporation shall
be managed by a board of fivedirectors, allof whom
shallbe lot-owners in said cemetery. The board of
directorsshallbe electedannually by the members of
the corporation, and shallhold their officefor one year
and untiltheir successorsare electedand qualified.
They shall choose a president out of their number .
They may appoint a secretary and treasurer, who
shall give such bond as they may require for the
faithfulperformance of his duty . All vacancies in
saidboard of directorsshallbe filledby appointment
of the president of the board .
§ 7. The board of directorsshallmake such by -laws,
rules and regulations, as they may deem proper for
callingand conducting their meetings and for the
transactionof their business and duties. They shall
have control of all the business transactions of the
corporation; shall collectand expend for the corpo-
ration all money ; shall have complete control of the
cemetery grounds and other property; shallhave
power to inclose, improve , embellish, and to adorn
the cemetery grounds , and to prescriberules for in-
closing, adorning, and erectingmonuments or vaults
upon the cemetery lotsby purchasers.
§ 8. The persons hereinbeforenamed as incorpora-
tors shall constitute the firstboard of directors of the
corporation .
§ 9. If any person shall willfullyand without law-
fulauthorityviolateany of the graves of the dead , or
deface or remove any of the tombstones , monuments ,
or inclosures,or cut,break,or injure any tree,shrub ,
plant,or otherornament of saidcemetery,he shallbe
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and besides being
liableto the corporation, or to the owner of the lot,
forthe damage or injurydone,he shallbe finednot
less than ten nor more than twenty -five dollarsfor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 27
each offense,upon convictionin any court having ju-
risdiction thereof .
§10.The secretaryshallkeep in a well-bound book ,
kept forthepurpose,a fulland accurateaccountof
allbusinessmeetings of the company - a record of the
salesand transfer of lots.
§ 11.Purchasers of lotsshall be required to pay
cash,or furnishapproved securityfor deferred pay-
ments,beforeany interments are made on their lots.
§12.All money realizedfrom the sale of lots, or
profitsreceivedfrom any source whatever, shallbe
devotedforeverand exclusivelyto the purpose of in-
closing, protecting, and ornamenting the cemetery
grounds,exceptsuch sums as may be appropriated
fornecessarypay of officersand keeper.
§ 13.Thisact shall take effect and be in force from
andafteritspassage.
Approved March 9, 1888.

liquorsfor the term of one year from said date, at

city; and whereas, said licensehas not been used by

liquorson Sumner street, in said cityof Bowling
Green , Kentucky ; therefore,

CHAPTER 491 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the Christian County Driving Park .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Christian County Driving Park , a
corporationcreatedand existingunder and by virtue
of chapter 56 of the General Statutesof Kentucky ,
be, and it is hereby, authorized and empowered to
sell, or to permit to be sold on itsgrounds,combina-
tion, French and other pools, on all trotting, running
or other races that may take place on said grounds
under itssupervision; and itmay alsosellsaidpools,

54 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
at some place to be designated by itsboard of direct-
ors, in the cityof Hopkinsville, during the continu-
ance of said races: Provided , Nothing herein shall
be construedas authorizingthe sellingof such pools
on any racesto be run or trottedelsewhere than upon
its own race -course .
§ 2. That it ishereby made the duty of the county
judge of Christiancounty,upon the written applica-
tion of the board of directors of said Christian County
Driving Park , to appoint a reasonable number of
sober and discreet men to act as policemen during
the continuance of the races on said race-course ;
and it shallbe the duty of saidpolicemento pre-
serve order and to arrestallpersons violatinglaw or
disturbing the peace on the grounds of said driving
park . But said policemen,before acting under such
appointment, shalltake an oath before said judge
to faithfullyand impartiallyperform theirduty as
such ; and the saidcounty judge aforesaidshallcause
an order to be spread upon the recordsof said county
court,showing the names of such policemen, and that
they have taken the oath as required.
§3 . That the compensation of the policemen pro-
vided for in the foregoing section shallbe paid by
the said ChristianCounty Driving Park .
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom and afterits pas-
sage .
Approved March 13,1888.

county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. B. Denham , late sheriffof Metcalfe
county, be, and he is, allowed the furthertime of one
year from the passage of this actto distrainfor and
collecthis uncollectedor delinquent county levy and
revenue taxes and his uncollected fee-bills.
§ 2. That nothing in this act shall be construed to
exempt saidlatesherifffrom the penaltiesprescribed
by law againstsuch officersfor distrainingfor or col-
lectingillegalfee-bills.
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved March 10,1888.

CHAPTER 499 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Cave City, in Barren
county .
Be itenacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That an act, entitled"An act to fix the corporate
boundary of Cave City,in Barren county," approved
March the 4th, 1869, be, and the same is hereby,
amended as follows :
§ 1. The board of trusteesof the town of Cave City,
in Barren county,shallhave power , within the limits
of said town, in additionto the other powers granted
to and vested in them by said act :
1. To prevent,abate and remove nuisances at the
expense of the owners or occupiers, or the parties
upon whose grounds they exist, and to define and
declare by ordinance what shall be a nuisance within
the limitsof said town ; and they may by ordinance
fix the penalty for keeping,causing or committing a
nuisance, which shall be by a finein the Cave City
policecourt not exceeding twenty -fivedollarsforeach
day the same iscontinued afternotice.
2. To appoint, support and regulate policemen,
night watches and patrols, and to prescribetheir
duties and compensation .

66 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
3. To suppress gaming , drunkenness , gambling-
houses, bawdy -houses or houses of prostitution,street
walkers and disorderlyhouses of allkinds,and to pro-
vide for the arrestand punishment of the offenders,
and may provide for the punishment of any person
who shallrent property to prostitutes, or shallsuffer
theirproperty to be used or occupied by lewd or dis-
orderlypersons, and may prescribeby ordinance such
penalty thereforas they may deem proper, not ex-
ceeding a fineof fiftydollars for each offense.
4. To provide for the prevention and extinguish-
ment of fires; to regulate and restrain the erection of
wooden buildings, and to provide for the removal of
the same when built contrary to the ordinances of
said town .
5. To provide for the prevention and suppression of
any riot, rout, breach of the peace,noise, disturbance,
disorderlyor unlawful assembly in the streets, houses
or other places in the town , or any violationof any
town ordinance , and for the arrest and confinement of
the offenders in the work -house of said town or the
jailof Barren county ,and to provide for the trialof
the partiescommitting, engaging in or taking part in
or aidingor abettingthe same . The board of trustees
shallhave power to prescribethe penalty for such
offenses, or for any violationof any of the by -laws or
ordinances of said town ; and the said board of trus-
tees of said town may , by their by -laws and ordi-
nances , prescribe and provide in all prosecutions for
offensesagainstsuch by -laws and ordinances, where
the penalty, or any part thereof, isa fine,that unless
the fine,fees and costsshallbe immediately paid or
replevied, the defendant shall be placed in the work-
house of said town or the jailof Barren county , and
there confined untilthe judgment forsuch fine,fees,
and all costs of the prosecution shall be paid or re-
plevied; but such imprisonment shallnot exceed one
day for each one dollar of the fine, fees and costsof
the prosecution: Provided, That the penalty fixed

by said board of trusteesfor a violationof any of
the by -laws or ordinances of said town shallnot ex-
ceed a fine of one hundred dollars or imprisonment
for fiftydays,or both, and the fees and costs of the
prosecution .
§ 2. All laws and acts in conflictwith this act are
hereby repealed.
§3 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved March 10,1888.
CHAPTER 500 .
AN ACT toamend an actincorporatingthetown of Lewisburg,county
of Logan,Stateof Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the corporatelimitsof the town of Lewis-
burg, Logan county , Kentucky , be, and are hereby,
reduced so as to include the following boundary :
Beginning at the present limits of the town on the
northern boundary at Alum lick; thence west with
the present limitsto the north-west corner of Cornel
Arnold's lot; thence in a straightline to a stone on
the southern boundary near the south -west corner of
the high school lot ; thence with the present limits
east to Alum lick; thence with the eastern boundary
to the beginning .
§2 . That allfinesassessedin pursuance of the pro-
visionsof the charter,or any of itsamendments ,or
under the by -laws of the town of Lewisburg, together
with all fees and costs arising from the prosecution of
the same ,if not paid or replevied, may be discharged
in the work -house or on the streetsor alleysor any
publicworks of the town at the rateof one dollarper
day ; and in caseany feesor cost are thus discharged,
the officersto whom such fees or costs are payable

68 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallbe paid by the town afterdeducting fiftycents
per day for the board of the prisonerwhile working
out said fees or cost .
§ 3. That the justiceof peace residingin or nearest
the corporatelimitsof the town of Lewisburg be em-
powered and hereby authorized to serve as police
judge, with allthe jurisdictionof said judge of said
town of Lewisburg .
§ 4. That the board of trustees be allowed to levy a
tax, not exceeding fortycentsper hundred dollars, to
be used in improving streetsand alleysand otherpub-
lic works of the town .
§ 5. That thisact take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved March 10, 1888 .

CHAPTER 502 .
AN ACT to authorizea specialterm of the courtof claimsin and
for Marshallcounty for the year 1888, and to empower saidcourt
to levy an ad valorem tax to build, re-build or repair the court-
house or clerk'sofficesin said county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That a specialterm of the Marshall county
court of claims may be held at any time during the
year 1888, upon a call of the justicesof the peace
for said county by the county judge of said county .
§2 . That said court of claims, when assembled
under said call, a majority of the justicesof said
county being present, shall have power to re-build,
repairor enlarge the court-house or clerks' offices, or
both, or to build a new court-house or clerks' offices,
or both, in said county .
§3 . For the purpose of erectingnew buildings, or
enlarging or repairingthe old ones, as set forth in
section 2 of this act, said court of claims shall have ,
atsaidspecialterm of said court, power to levy an ad
valorem tax on all the taxable property of Marshall
county, real, personal and mixed , of not exceeding
twenty -five cents on each one hundred dollars' worth
of said property subjectto taxation in said county ;
and said taxes, when collected, shallbe applied only
to the purposes aforesaid.
§ 4. Said court of claims is hereby authorizedand

72 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
empowered to make such dispositionof the old court-
house or clerks' officesas may be deemed best by said.
court .
§5 . The taxes raisedby the levy hereby provided
for shall be collectedby the sheriffor collectorof the
county levy and public dues of said county ,and he
shallbe governed in making said collectionsby the
general laws governing and regulatingthe collection
of the county levy and public dues of said county ,
and shallhave the same powers as given by the gen-
eral law .
§ 6. The sheriffof said county shall, before he pro-
ceeds to collectthe taxes provided for by this act,
execute bond with good security, to be approved by
said court of claims , in the same manner and form as
now requiredby law.
§ 7. The tax and levy herein provided for may be
levied for the years 1888–1889 , but no longer .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved March 13, 1888.

CHAPTER 509 .
AN ACT to establishthe Allen Male and Female College in Scotts-
ville, Allen county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an institutionof learning be, and the
same is hereby, established in Scottsville, Allen
county, Kentucky , by the name and style of " The
Allen Male and Female College," which shall be
under the control of R. L. Brown , S. H. Welch , J.
R. Garrison, F. G. Harlan, W. H. H. Mitchell, J. F.
Pitchford and Wm . H. Read , and theirsuccessors, as
trustees of said institution. If at any time a vacancy
shalloccur by death,removal or resignation,the other
trustees shall name a suitable person to fillthe vacan-
cy. They shallhave the power to name one of their
number presidentof theirboard,and to selectsuch
other officersas they may deem proper. They shall
have power to employ such professorsof learning as
they may desire to teach in said college. They may
hold annual commencements and public examina-
tionsof the pupils. They may prescribethe courses
of instructionfor said college. They may confer the
degrees of Bachelor of Science and Maid of Science,
Bachelor of Arts and Maid of Arts ,and such honorary
degrees and diplomas as is usual in colleges in this
Commonwealth . But no honorary degree shallentitle
the person receivingit to be exempt from the regular
examination as a teacher .
§2 . Be itfurther enacted, That said school shall
receive such students of both sexes, and of such de-
gree of advancement , as it may deem expedient and
proper .
§3 . That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 13,1888 .

replevy, for three months ,any judgment ,fineor for-
feitureof the police court , on execution or other final
process thereon, by giving bond , with good security,
in the same manner as allowed by law in similar
cases within the jurisdiction of other courts of this
Commonwealth .
§ 38. That a return of not found on a capias pro
fine and of no property found on a fierifacias issued
on any judgment of the police court, shall authorize
an equitableproceeding in favor of the board of trus-
tees of Falmouth againstthe estateof the defendant
forthe purpose of enforcingsatisfactionof saidjudg-
ment ,in the same manner that a return of no prop-
ertyauthorizessuch proceedingsin civilcauses.
§ 39. That upon alljudgments forfinesrendered by
saidcourt, and which shallnot be paid, an execution
againstthe estateof the defendant may be issued, or
a capias pro fine may be issued, commanding the
imprisonmentof the defendantin the jailor work-
house of the town or county until the same shall
be paid ; but the imprisonment shall not exceed one
day foreach dollarof such fine: Provided, That said
court, in its discretion, may assign such defendant
so finedto hard labor on the streetsor alleys, or any
works belonging in any manner to said town , or at
some other labor for the benefit of said town , until
he shall pay his fineand the costsadjudged against
him ; allowing for hislabor at the rate of one dollar
per day ;eighthours shallconstitutea day ;and when
not at labor defendant shall be confinedas provided
in thissection. And for the purpose of putting him
in the jailor work -house and taking him out to la-
bor, a certifiedcopy of the order assigninghim to
work shall be sufficient authority to the marshal ,
jaileror keeper of the work -house. All persons
sentenced to imprisonment by said court may be
assigned to labor in like manner as those fined.
When personsimprisoned, as contemplatedby this

section,shallbe confined in the county jail, the jail-
er's fees shallbe paid by said town .
§ 40. That appeals shallbe from the decisionsand
judgments of saidpolicecourt of the Pendleton crim-
inal court in criminal and penal causes, when the fine
exceeds twenty-fivedollarsor the imprisonment ex-
ceeds ten days. But no judgment shallbe suspended
by such appeals unlessthe defendant,within fivedays
after the rendition thereof, cause to be executed , be-
fore the clerk of the court to which the appeal is
taken , a bond with good security, in all respects as
required by section364 of the Criminal Code of Prac-
tice; or, in civilcases, appeals shallbe to the Pendle-
ton quarterlyor circuitcourts, the same as appeals
from judgments of justicesof the peace to said
courts .
§ 41. That the fees of the police judge, whether
acting as judge or clerk or otherwise, shall be the
same in all cases as are now or may hereafter be given
by law to officersperforming like services; and fees
and costs shall be taxed in cases in said court to the
same extent , in the same way , and under the same
regulations, as in other courts having similarjuris-
diction .
General Powers and Duties of the Board of Trustees .
§ 42. That the board of trustees shall hold stated
meetings, and the chairman , or, in his absence, the
clerk, may callmeetings by notice(oralor written) to
each of the members ; a majority of the members
elected as trustees shall constitute a quorum .
§ 43. That the board of trustees shall have power to
make , ordain and publish all such ordinances, by-
laws, police and sanitaryregulations, not contrary to
the Constitutionof this Commonwealth , forthe good
government of said town , and to promote the health,
security, trade and commerce thereof, and that may
be necessary or expedient to carry into effect the
powers vested in the board of trustees, or any officer

of said town ,by thisact, and to amend or repeal the
same , and to enforce the observance of all ordinances ,
by -laws and regulationsmade in pursuance to thisact
by penaltiesnot to exceed one hundred dollars fine
and fiftydays imprisonment for any offenseagainst
the same .
§ 44. That the board of trustees shall be allowed
fiftycents a piece for every regular meeting of the
board that they may attend , as compensation for their
services; but they shallnot be allowed any compensa-
tion for calledmeetings or for any regular meeting
they do not attend; said allowance, when due , shall
be allowed by the board,and an orderentered on their

rant on the treasurer for same , as allother claims are
allowed .

attached to said office, the allowance of which is made
by said board of trustees; nor shall any member of
said board of trustees be allowed to furnish materials
or other things to said board for the benefitof the
town of Falmouth under contract, or in any other
manner , where there is compensation attached to

§ 49. That all taxes or assessments levied or assessed
by the board of trustees under this act shall be a lien
upon the real and personal estate upon which the
same may be imposed , voted or assessed, until the
same is paid ; and no sale or transfer of real estate
shall affect the lien thereon .
§ 50. That the right of redemption in all cases of
sales of real estate for taxes or assessments shall exist
to the owner or his heirs,for a period of three years
from the day of sale, on payment of the amount for
which the same was sold, and twenty -fiveper cent.
added , and allthe taxes accruing subsequent to the
sale; and if the owner , at the time of the sale, be an
infant, married woman , or person of unsound mind ,
said right to redeem shall exist for a like number of
years afterthe removal of such disability. If not re-
deemed , the board of trusteesshall, on presentation
of the certificateof sale,execute a deed to the pur-
chaser,conveying to him the real estateso sold and
unredeemed as aforesaid .
§ 51. That allfinesand forfeitures, taxes and assess-
ments herein contemplated and provided for, shallbe
appropriatedexclusivelyto the use and benefitof said
town , in such a manner as the board of trustees may
direct .
§ 52. That the clerk shallkeep a record of allsales
fortaxesand assessments,which shallbe open forin-
spection at alltimes .

eficialto said town , and by a unanimous vote may
closeany alleyin saidtown .

104 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 515 .
AN ACT directingtheSecretaryofStateto procureand furnishto
Menefee county certainbooks .
WHEREAS , At the formation of Menefee county the
act creatingsaid county required the Secretary of
State to furnish said county and its several officers
with allthe books then allowed by law to the several
counties; and whereas, the Secretary of State failed
to furnish to the clerks of the county and circuit
courts,and to the county judge of said county, one
copy each of volumes one and two of Duvall's Re-
ports, and one copy each of volumes one and two of
Bush's Reports ; therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Secretary of State be , and he is
hereby,directedto procure,by purchase or otherwise,
and furnish to said county, one copy each for the
county court and circuitcourt clerk'soffices, and one
copy for the use of the county judge's officeof the
firstand second volumes of Duvall's Reports and the
firstand second volumes of Bush's Reports ; and for
the cost of which he will draw his warrant on the
Treasurer ; but that in no event shall any one volume
cost more than five dollars per volume .
§ 2. This act to take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
[Became a law withoutapprovalof Governor March 14,1888.]

in any court having jurisdiction.
§ 3. That the said association shall have full power
and authority to make ,ordain and enforce such by-
laws, rulesand regulations, as itmay deem necessary
for the administrationand government of its mem-
bers: Provided , That such by -laws, rules and regu-
lations, shall not be inconsistent with the Constitution
or by -laws of thisStateor of the United States.
§ 4. That the said association shall have power to
purchase and hold allsuch realand personalproperty
as it may deem necessary and proper for the conven-
ient transactionof its business; but the aggregate
value of such property shall not at any time exceed
the sum of twenty thousand dollars.

act are hereby repealed.
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom and afterits
passage .

CHAPTER 539 .
AN ACT to change thejurisdictionand fix the time of holding the
regular civilterms of the Bowling Green city court.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That after the passage of this act the Bowling
Green city court shall have no jurisdictionto hear,
tryor determine any action or prosecution under the
criminalor penal laws of thisCommonwealth , except
prosecutionsforoffensescommitted within the corpо-
rate boundary of the city of Bowling Green ; nor
shallsaid court have jurisdictionto hold an examin-
ing trialin any case where the offenseis committed
elsewhere than in said city.
§2. That said city court shallhave no jurisdiction
tohear, try or determine any civilaction or proceed-
ing, except actionsin which the defendant or some
one of the defendants resideswithin said city,or con-
sentsin writing,signed by him , and indorsed on the
summons , for the action to be returned for trialbefore
saidcity court .
§3 . That the regular civilterms of said citycourt
shallbe held only on the third Monday in January ,
April, July and October : Provided , however, That if
said court may not be able to complete the docket
upon one day , it may adjourn from day to day until
the business of the term is completed .
§ 4. That nothing in thisact shallaffectany action
or prosecution begun in said city court before the
adoption of this act.
§ 5. That allactsand partsof acts in conflictwith
this act are hereby repealed .
§ 6. That this act shall take effect and be in force
from and afteritspassage.

CHAPTER 545 .
AN ACT for the benefitof Carroll county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Carroll county court is hereby author-
ized to levy and have collected an ad valorem tax, not

exceeding ten cents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of taxable property in said county, for the
purpose of paying the present indebtedness of said
county, and to build or repairbridgesand culvertson
the variousroads in said county in the future. Said
tax may be leviedat any time before the firstof Au
gust, 1888, for the year 1888, and the court of claims
forsaid county may levy said tax whenever thought
necessary thereafter.
§ 2. The sheriffof saidcounty shallcollectsaid tax
when levied, and pay the same to the treasurer of

county levy bond shallbe liablefor the collectionand
payment of said taxes as aforesaid.

passage .

CHAPTER 547 .
AN ACT authorizingthe Owen countycourttoissueand sellbonds to
pay off itsindebtedness,and to issuebonds torefunditsoutstanding
bonded indebtedness,and toprovideforthepayment thereof.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Owen county court be , and itishereby ,
authorizedto issue the bonds of said county in any
sum not exceeding twenty thousand dollars,to pay off
itspresent floatingindebtedness for turnpike and
bridge purposes, and in any additionalsum , not ex-
ceeding eighty thousand dollars,for the purpose of
refunding the present bonded indebtedness of said
county . No bond is to be for a less sum than one
hundred dollars .
§ 2. Said bonds shall bear interest at a rate not ex-
ceeding six per centum per annum , and shall be due
and payable in fifteenyears, but redeemable at any
time in the pleasure of the county court aftereight
years from the issualof any of saidbonds .
§ 3. They shallbe signed by the county judge and
attestedby the county clerkof saidcounty,and coun-
tersignedby the county treasurerof said county, and
stamped with the seal of the court. They shallbe
made payable to bearer and pass by delivery, and the
interestthereon shall be paid semi -annually .
§ 4. Said bonds shallbe disposed of by saidcounty
court for the purpose of paying off the present in-
debtedness of the county not already bonded , and
bonds may be issued under this act and substituted

144 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
i
for bonds heretoforeissued,bearing a greater rate of
interest, but the amount of bonds to be limited, as
provided in section 1 of this act.
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the county court judge
and county treasurerto regulateand sellsaid bonds .
The date,amount ,and when due of each bond shallbe
enteredby them upon a registerkept by the county
treasurerfor that purpose ; and the proceeds of said
bonds shallbe paid to the county treasurer, and be by
him applied to the payment of the said indebtedness ,
and he shall be responsibleupon his bond for the
faithful application of such moneys .
§6 . It shall be the duty of the county court to pro-
vide forthe payment of the principaland interestof
saidbonds, and is authorized to levy an ad valorem
tax for not more than fiftycents on each one hundred
dollars' worth of taxable property in the county to en-
ableitto comply with the provisionsof thisact; but
the ad valorem tax in said county shall not at any
time exceed the said sum of fiftycents on each one
hundred dollars'worth of taxableproperty in any one
year .
§ 7. Said tax shall be collected in the same manner
and under the same liabilitythat other county taxes
are collected, and shall pay the same to the order of
the county court .
§ 8. The county court shall have power to make all
orders necessary to carrying into execution the pro-
visions of this act .
§ 9. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 15,1888.

CHAPTER 548 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Ludlow .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . The school board of the city of Ludlow , in
Kenton county,shallhave full and exclusivecontrol
of allthe school money and funds now belonging to,
or that may hereafterin anywise come to belong or
appertain to,the common school fund or to the com-
mon schoolsof saidcity.
§2 . The treasurerof the city of Ludlow shall be
ex officiothe treasurerof the schoolboard, shallgive
bond with two sufficientsuretiesfor double the largest
amount of money which will pass through his hands
in one year, which bond must be approved by the
school board ; he shall submit to the school board of
saidcitya statementof allreceiptsand expenditures
of money belonging to the school fund , and showing
the balance remaining to the creditof said fund once
in each month , and at such other times as said school
board may require; and said treasurershall pay all
certificatesof indebtedness issued by said school
board immediately upon presentation.
§ 3. A majority of all the members of said school
board shall constitute a quorum to transact business .
The expenditure of money ,the fillingof vacanciesin
saidboard ,and the electionof teachersor other em-
ployes,shallrequirethe concurrence of a majority of
all the members of said board ; in the transaction of
allother business, a majority of the members present
shall concur , unless otherwise ordered by said board .
§4 . If any member elected to said school board
shallfail to qualifywithin thirtydays afterthe be-
ginning of his term of office, his seatmay be declared
vacant by a two-thirdvote of the qualifiedmembers .

CHAPTER 549 .
AN ACT for the benefitof districtNo. 114,known as Green Hill Sem-
inary, Barren county .
WHEREAS , There being a greater number of pupil
childrenof school age livingin said districtNo. 114,
known as Green Hill Seminary , Barren county, than
the presentlaw permits money to be drawn upon , and
they being convenient enough to attend the school-
house in said district,more so than to adjoiningdis-
tricts; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That allthe white pupil children of said dis-
trictof school age, at the proper time, be reported in
the census report, and that the districtdraw the pub-
lic school fund each year for all children properly
reported, as in other districtswhere the number does
* not exceed the requirement of the law .
§ 2. The above sectionfirstshallfirstapply to the
census taken in April , 1888 .
§ 3. The board of trustees-elect of the common
school shall also be the trustees of the said Green Hill
Seminary ,districtNo. 114, and they shallhave power
and authorityto appoint a principalor presidentof
the school, and , with the advice of said principal,
such other teachers as may be necessary to instruct
the pupilsof saidschool and seminary in the artsand
sciences, and in all necessaryand useful branches of
a thorough and liberaleducation;the object, aim and
design being not only a common school under the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 147
12
general laws of this Commonwealth for the education
of all its white children within the school age residing
in said district, but also to enable the trustees and
teachers thereof to raise the standard of education ,
and to teach all persons or children that may attend
said school the higher branches of education not re-
quired by the common school law of the State to be
taught in schoolsexclusivelyunder and regulatedby
said school law .
§ 4. The trustees of said school and seminary , to-
gether with the principal teacher thereof, shall have
power and authority to regulate the course of studies,
including allcommon school branches, and grade the
school children in departments and classes as to them
may seem best; and may also confer upon the grad-
uates of said school and seminary such diplomas ,
honors and degrees, as evidence of scholarship, as
are usual in the high schools and collegesin this
Commonwealth .
§ 5. Nothing in sections2, 3 and 4 shall, in any
way , interferewith the regulationsof the common
school as prescribedby the common school law gov-
erning the same .
§ 6. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 15,1888.

CHAPTER 551 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe LouisvilleDemocratic Club .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That B. F. Camp , Thomas H. Sherley, A. S.
Willis, John T. Gathright, W. B. Hoke , F. Hagan ,
LyttletonCooke ,Shackelford Miller,C. G. Hulsewede ,
E. D. Briscoe and Joseph B. Reed , their associates,
successors and assigns, be,and they are hereby , cre-
ated a body -politicand corporate,under the name and
style of the LouisvilleDemocratic Club ; and by that
name shall have perpetual succession, and power to
contractand be contractedwith,sue and be sued, and
do business generally .
§2 . The capital stock of said club shall not exceed
one hundred thousand dollars, divided into shares of
ten dollarseach, and the said stock shallbe held by
members only ; and upon the death, resignation or
expulsion of any member , the stock held by him
shall revert to the club .
§3 . The said club shall be located in the city of
Louisville, Kentucky , and shall have power , and is
hereby authorized , to establish, furnish and maintain
a suitable club -house for the use and benefit of its
members , and for the purpose of socialenjoyment ,
the advancement of scienceof politicaleconomy , and
the propagation of the principlesof Democracy , and
the promotion of socialand friendlyrelationsamong
its members ; and for that purpose may purchase,
build and rent suitableland and buildings in said
city; and the said club may take and hold,by gift,
purchase , contract or devise, personaland real estate
to the value of one hundred thousand dollars, which
shall be used for the benefit of the club .
§ 4. The club may be organizedas soon as fivethou-
sand dollarsof itsstock issubscribedand paid for,by
the appointment of a president, and such other offi

150 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
cersas may be deemed necessary. Its members may
make such by -laws, rulesand regulations, as it may
deem proper, from time to time, for the government
and management of the club ; and itmay ,by itsby-
laws, prescribe the duties and qualificationsof its
members , and the conditionsupon which persons may
become and remain members of the club .
§5 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved March 15, 1888.

AN ACT to amend an act approved on the second day of March ,
1888 ,entitled" An act to amend an act, entitled 'An act to amend
and reduce into one the various acts in regard to Bardstown . "
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act approved March 2, 1888, entitled
" An act to amend an act, entitled 'An act to amend
and reduce into one the various acts in regard to
Bardstown , " be amended by adding to the second
last section of the said act, which was approved on
the second day of March , 1888, the following: Prо-
vided, That all taxes mentioned in the act to which
this is an amendment , shall be paid on or before the
twentiethday of March , 1888, or else the one offering
to vote shall not be entitled to vote in any municipal
electionwhich may be held during the year 1888 ; and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 157
after the year 1888 said taxes shall be paid on or
before March first, as is provided in the act to which
this is an amendment .
§2 This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved March 16, 1888.

CHAPTER 562 .
AN ACT to authorizethe county courtof Rockcastlecounty to levy
and collectan ad valoremtaxforcountypurposes.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Rockcastle county , in
order to reduce the poll-tax to one dollar, shall have
power to levy an ad valorem tax , not to exceed thirty
cents,on each one hundred dollarsof taxable prop-
erty in said county, to be collectedby the sheriffof
said county, under the same pains and penaltiesas
sheriffsare now subject to under existinglaws : Pro-
vided, That before the said court shallbe authorized
to levy the said ad valorem tax, and reduce the poll-
tax as herein provided, the provisionsof thisact shall
be firstsubmitted to a vote of the qualifiedvotersof
said county,at the regular August electionof 1888,
and a majorityof the votersof said county voting at
said election shall have voted in favor of such ad
valorem tax and reduced poll-tax, the vote to be
certifiedby the examining board to the court, and
entered upon the record in the county court clerk's
officeof said county .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 16,1888.
CHAPTER 563 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Mutual Fire Insurance Company of Ken-
tucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Paris C. Brown , Q. O. Maddux , H. Drex-
elius, Sam'l Bigstaff, J. K. Stone, W. P. Dickerson,
C. J. Helm , G. Artsman , H. Buchanan , or such of
them as may act, and theirassociates, successorsand

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 187
assigns, be,and they are hereby, created a body -cor-
porate, under the name and styleof the Mutual Fire
Insurance Company of Kentucky ; with perpetualsuc-
cession, and fullpowers to contractand be contracted
with, sue and be sued ; to acquire, use,hold and dis-
pose of real and personal property; to invest its
capital and funds accumulated in the course of its
business,or any part thereof, in any bonds, stocks,
mortgages , deeds of trustin real estate,in or out of
the State of Kentucky ; to loan its said capitaland
accumulated funds out on such securityas itsboard
of directorsmay determine, not, however , except as
authorizedby the generalinsurancelaws of the State;
to insurehouses, buildings, and other kinds of prop-
erty, in or out of this State, againstloss or dam-
age by fireor from lightning; to cause itselfto be
insured against any lossor riskitmay have incurred
in the course of itsbusiness, and upon the interestit
may have in any property by means of any loan or
loansitmay have on mortgage or other lien, and gen-
erallyto do and perform other matters and things
proper to promote these things ; to enact by -laws not
in conflict with this act or the laws of the State .
That the capitalstock of said company shallbe the
premium noteshereinafterprovided for,and ten thou-
sand dollars, divided intoshares of fiftydollarseach,
which shall be transferableonly on the books of the
company , and shall be paid up only as the directors
of said company shall determine. Any person, his
heirs, executor, administratorand assigns, may , by
saidcompany ,be allowed to become a member thereof
by insuring property therein on the mutual plan.
Persons so insuring shall, in addition to paying in
cash a sum to be determined by the directors, also
execute a note to said company forsuch a sum as the
directorsshalldetermine, which sum shallbe forfifty
dollars , or the multiples thereof. Said note shall be
payable on demand at any time during the lifeof the
insurance forwhich the same may have been given,

188 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and a lien for the amount of same shall exist on the
property insured; notes for any one risk shall not
exceed one thousand dollars. At the expiration of
the term of insurance for which such notes may be
given , they shall be delivered up to the assured ; said
notesshallconstitutepart of the capitalstock of said
company . Whenever said company shall meet any
losswhich they cannot otherwisepay , then they shall
assessallsuch notestheirproportionatepart of such
loss, and may ,if necessary, instituteproper proceed-
ings to collect the same ; no such assessment shall,
however , be made until the ten thousand dollars of
capital stock shall have been exhausted . On the first
of January in each year said company shall ascertain
its net earnings after the payment of all expenses ,
and shall, out of the same , set apart twenty-fiveper
cent. thereof as a surplus fund ; from the residue they
shall pay to the holders of the ten thousand dollars
of capitalstock six per cent.thereon, and the residue
shall be declared a dividend on the said ten thousand
dollarsof capitalstock and allpremium notes that are
sixmonths old,and the same shallbe paid to the hold-
ers of said stock and the makers of said notes in cash .
No such divisionof earnings shall, however , be de-
clared, unless the capital stock of ten thousand dollars
shall be unimpaired,but the whole of the earnings
shall go to make up the impairment of the capital
stock. Privateproperty of the owners of the capital
stock shall be exempt from liabilityfor corporate
debts, except to the extent of unpaid subscriptionto
the stock, and the face of premium notes unpaid .
The affairsof said company shall be managed by a
board of five directors, to be chosen from the owners
of the ten thousand dollars of capitalstock by the
owners thereof, and the makers of premium notes,
who shallbe entitledto participatein the profits, in
person or by proxy ; each share of stock and each
fiftydollars of premium notes shall represent one
vote. Said board may electand employ such officers

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 189
.
and agents as may be necessaryto transactitsbusi-
ness, and may make such by -laws, not in conflictwith
the Constitution and laws of this State or the United
States, as they seeproper. Said company shall, in so
faras applicable, comply with the laws of thisState
regulatingfireinsurancecompanies .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved March 16, 1888 .

CHAPTER 568 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous,vinous or malt liquorswithin
one mile of the turnpike road leading from Yosemite to Dunnville , in
Casey county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That itshall be unlawful forany person within
one mile of the turnpike road leading from Yosemite
to Dunnville, in Casey county, to sell spirituous,
vinousor malt liquorsin any quantity : Provided ,
That this section shall not affect the laws now in
forcein the incorporated towns on said road .
§2. That any person guilty of a violationof the
provisionsof the preceding sectionshall be guiltyof
a misdemeanor,and upon convictionshallbe finedfor
eachoffensenot lessthan ten nor more than fiftydol-
lars.
§3. That the county judge, policejudges, and jus-
ticesof the peace, in Casey county, are hereby au-
thorizedto issuea warrant of arrest,upon the affidavit
ofany respectablecitizenof saidcounty, againstany
onecharged with the offenseof violatingthe provis-
ions of the firstsection of this act without indictment
by a grand jury : Provided, The Commonwealth's at-
torneyor the defendant may demand and have a jury
to try the case.
§4 . This act to be in force from itspassage .
[Became a law without approval of Governor March 19,1888.]

bearing any rateof interestthat the county court by
itsorder may designate, not exceeding seven per cen-
tum per annum , and in such amounts as the court
may direct: Provided , That at no time shall the out-
standing bonds exceed eight thousand dollars, and be
made payable at any time the court may , by itsorder,
fix;but in no case shall the time exceed twenty years ;
and may fix the time of paying the interestannually
or semi-annually ; and , for that purpose,shallattach
coupons to said bonds ; said bonds and coupons shall
be signed by the county judge and countersigned by
the county court clerk; but before the same shallbe
issued, a majority of the justicesof said county shall
be present and concur in the same .
§2 . That the bonds mentioned in section1 may be
sold by any agent appointed by the county court :
Provided ,however, That in no event shallthey bring
less than ninety -fiveper cent.of the face of same ; and
the proceeds of said bonds shallbe used exclusively

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 203
t for the purpose of redeeming or paying offthe bonds
of said county now outstanding, building or repair-
ing the public buildings and bridgesof said county .
and to pay off any deficitthat now exists or may
hereafter exist by reason that per capita tax has been
or may hereafterbe insufficientto pay the current
expenses of said county .
§ 3. For the purpose of raisingmoney to pay the
principal and accruing intereston said bonds , the
county court of claims of said county is hereby em-
powered and authorized to levy an ad valorem tax on
allthe taxable property in said county, not exceeding
fiftycents on each one hundred dollars' worth, which
tax shall be placed in the hands of the sheriffof said
county, and shall by him be collectedin the same
way and manner as the Staterevenue iscollected;and
said sheriff and his sureties shall be liable for the col-
lectionof same as he is by existing laws for the
collectionof the county levy, and shallpay the same
over in like manner when required by the county
court .
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved March 19,1888 .

and after its passage .

CHAPTER 575 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Bellevue,in Campbell
county,and authorizingthe issueof schoolbonds of the town.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The board of trustees of the town of Bellevue ,Issuebonds.
in Campbell county, Kentucky , are hereby duly au-
thorized and empowered to issue the bonds of said
town , to be called" Bellevue School Bonds ," to any
amount not exceeding the sum of ten thousand ($10,-
000) dollars; and to pledge the faith, creditand rev-
enue of the town for the payment of the same ; said
bonds, or their proceeds, to be used exclusively, and
for no other purpose, than the buildingof a school-
house in said town .
bonds .§ 2. Said bonds shall be of the denominations of Denominationof
one hundred , five hundred or one thousand dollars,
as said board of trustees shall, by resolution or by
ordinance,determine. They shallbe numbered seri-
ally, commencing with the number (1) one. They
shall be made payable fifteenyears afterdate, with
the option on the part of the town to redeem them ,
or any of them , fiveyears after their date, bearing
interest from their date at the rate of six per cent .
per annum , payable annually, the interestas it ma-
tures, and the principal, when due ,to be made pay-
able at some place to be designated in said bonds .
Interestcoupons shall be attached, to be numbered
serially, from one (1) to fifteen(15), and every coupon
to bear the serial numbers of the bond to which it is
attached. Each of said bonds shallbe signed by the
presidentof the board of trusteesand countersigned
by the town clerk, and shallhave the sealof the town
affixedthereto. Each of said interestcoupons shall
be signed by the town clerk. Said bonds and coupons
shall be made payable to bearer, and shallpass by
delivery. Said bonds, when so issued, shallbe placed

in the hands of the president of the board of trus-
tees, the town clerk and the town treasurer, who shall
have the right to disposeof any of the said bonds at
no lessthan their par value, and with their proceeds
pay the costof the school-house to be erected in said
town . Said officersto reportin writing to said board
itsactionsin the premises, quarterly and oftener if
required by said board, the number and amount of
the bonds disposed of by them , and to whom . Said
bonds shallbe lithographedand issued in book form ,
with stub attached, showing the number , amount and
date of issue of each bond , and to whom sold, and
amount received therefor. Any person who shall use
or appropriate any of their proceeds otherwise than
in the manner hereindirectedshallbe deemed guilty
of embezzlement, and upon conviction punished as
provided by statutes in case of embezzlement .
§3 . The board of trustees of said town are hereby
authorized to levy an additional tax each year of ten
cents on the one hundred dollars, and to be levied
every year until said bonds are paid off,and to be
used exclusivelyto pay offsaidindebtedness. If any
sum should remain over after paying off saidbonds
and the interestthereon,such sum shallbe credited
to the school fund of said town .

Improvestreets.Kentucky,be,and the same ishereby,authorizedto

or avenue, by such petition, where the opposite side
of such street is not laid out in town lots or in the cor-
poration .
§5 . This act shalltake effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 19,1888.

§ 16. That said board of trustees and their success-
ors shallbe a body -politicand corporate, and shallbe
known and calledby the name and styleof the Board
of Trusteesof Campbellsville; and by that name shall
be capable in law, for and in behalf of said town , of
contractingand being contracted with, of suing and
being sued, of defending and being defended, of
pleading and being impleaded , in allcourts; and of
taking, holding, purchasing, leasing, and conveying
such real and personal estateas the purposes of the
town may require within or without the limits of said
town ; and in all courts and places may do allthings
which a body -corporate, with perpetual succession,
may lawfully and rightfullydo within the limits of
the powers herein granted .
§ 17. That allthe legislativepower and authority of
said town , together with the fiscal, prudential and
municipal concerns thereof, and the government and
control of same , shall be vested in said board of trus-
tees .
§ 18. That the officers of said town not hereinbefore
provided for shall be a clerk, treasurer, assessor, col-

ployes asthetrustees,from time to time,shallappoint.
Any vacancy occurringby death, removal,resignation
or otherwise,of any officerappointed by the board of

§19 . That the board of trustees shall hold stated
meetings, and the chairman , or, in his absence, the
town clerk, may call meetings by notice to each of
the members . A majority of the members elected
shallconstitutea quorum . At the firstregularmeet-

meetings,who shallbe styled“ Chairman of the Board
of Trustees of Campbellsville ." The said chairman
shallnot vote on any mattersbeforethe board, except
in cases of a tievote by the other members present.

Powers and Duties of Officers.

shallpresideover the meetings of the board, and take
carethat the laws of the Commonwealth and the by-
laws and ordinances of the town are enforced, and

Police Court .
§32 . That the judicialpower of said corporation
shallbe, and is hereby ,vested in and shall be exer-
cised by a court, to be styled "The Campbellsville
Police Court," which shall be held by one judge, to
be styledthe judge of the Campbellsvillepolicecourt,
who shall be elected as provided in this act.
§ 33. Said policecourt shallhave exclusiveoriginal
jurisdictionto hear, try and determine finallyall
actions, prosecutions or other proceeding for the vio-
lation of the ordinances and by -laws of said town ;
and shall have concurrent originaljurisdictionwith
justicesof the peace of Taylor county in all penal
and criminal caseswhich said justicesnow have or

232 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
may hereafterbe given them by general law ; and
shallhave concurrent originaljurisdictionwith the
Taylor circuit court to hear , try and determine
finallyall prosecutions, actions and proceeding for
misdemeanors against the laws of this Common-
wealth committed in Taylor county . The judge of
said policecourt shall have jurisdictionto hold ex-
amining courts in felony cases, and for his ser-
vices in holding examining courts shall receivethe
same compensation as is allowed by law to justices
of the peace for similarservices, to be paid as said
justices' fees are directedby law to be paid.
§ 34. Said police court shall have concurrent ori-
ginaljurisdictionwith justicesof the peace of Taylor
county of allmattersof law and equity in the limits
of said county, in allactionsor proceedings for the
recovery of money or personal property, when the
matter in controversy , exclusive of interestand costs,
does not exceed fiftydollars,and concurrentoriginal
jurisdictionwith the Taylor circuitcourt when the
matter in controversy, exclusiveof interestand costs,
does not exceed five hundred dollars in value ; and
said police court shall have the same power and au-
thority to enforce its orders and judgements as the
circuit courts of this Commonwealth have .
§ 35. If, in any action in the police court, except as
herein provided,itshallappear,during the pendency
thereof, that the titleof real estate is involved in the
controversy,it shallbe the duty of the court, on the
motion of eitherparty to the action,to make an order
transferringitto the Taylor circuitcourt; and itshall
be the duty of the clerkof the policecourt to deliver
the originalpapers, and certifiedcopies of allorders
made in saidaction, to the clerkof said circuitcourt ;
but if judgment be rendered by the policecourt, the

uary and October, and may continue from day to day

premises the board of trusteesshallorder and direct

Taxation .
§ 61. That the board of trusteesof Campbellsville
shallhave power,subjecttotheexceptionshereinafter
mentioned , toannuallylevyand collecttaxes,not ex-
ceeding fiftycentson each one hundred dollars' worth
of all the realestatein saidtown,includingtheim-
provements on suchrealestate, and upon allpersonal
estate, includingstores,groceriesand factories,shops,

as well as bonds, notes,stocks and other evidencesof

due from him , and also upon each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of railroadand railroadproperty within
the corporatelimitsof said town , the value thereofto
be assessedas provided forby the generallaws of this

§ 62. That there shall be exempt from taxation
under this act all lands within the limits of said town
used exclusivelyfor farming purposes, unless said
lands should be at any time divided into town lots,
then they are to be taxed as other property. There
shallalsobe exempt from taxation, under thisact, all
public property belonging to said town , the county of
Taylor, or the State of Kentucky , and all churches
and parsonages, and all grounds dedicated to such
uses, school property, and a cemetery belonging to
said town .
§ 63. That the board of trustees shall also have
power to annually levy and collecta tax per capita
upon each and every male inhabitantin said town over
the age of twenty-one years, not exceeding two dol-
lars in any one year , and a tax not exceeding one dol-
laron each dog over one that any person may keep ,
or sufferto be kept, on his premises.
Assessment and Collection of Taxes .
§ 64. The board of trusteesshall have power , by
ordinance , to prescribe the form of assessment rolls
and prescribethe duties and define the power of
assessors, and make such rules and give such direc-
tions in relationto attending, revisingor adding to
the rollsas they may deem proper and expedient,
not inconsistentwith general laws. That the annual
assessment rollsshall be returned by the assessor on
or beforethe firstMonday in April of each year,but
the time may be extended by the board of trustees.

On the returnthereof, the board may fix a day for
hearing objectionsthereto, and the clerkshallgive
notice of the time and place of such hearing ; and
any person feelingaggrieved by the assessment of
his or her property may appear at the time specified
and make objections. The board of trusteesshallhave
power to supply omissions in said assessment rolls;
and for the purpose of equalizingthe same , to alter,
to take from or add to,or otherwise correctand re-
vise the same . That when the assessment roll shall
have been corrected and revised, the same shall be
filed, and an order confirming the same shall be en-
teredby the clerk. The board of trustees shall there-
upon, by ordinanceor resolution, levy such sum or
sums of money as they deem proper, not exceeding
the amount authorizedby this act, and to directthe
collector to collect the same .
$65. That all taxes and assessments, general and
special, levied or assessedby the board of trustees
under this act,shall be a lien upon the realand per-
sonalestate upon which the same may be imposed
untilsuch tax shall be paid . No sale or transfer of
real estate shall affect the lien thereon for such taxes .
§ 66. The collector shall collect all taxes and assess-
ments which shall be levied and assessed by the board
of trustees, and forthat purpose he shallhave allthe
powers of a sheriffto make sales of personal and
realestate; he shall pay over to the treasurer all
taxes and other moneys by him collectedas soon as
collected. When the tax listisplaced in his hands ,
he shallproceed at once to collectthe same ; and to
enforcepayment , may levy on and sellpersonalprop-
erty,after advertisingthe same by writtenor printed
notices, posted at the court-house doors and three
otherpublic places in said town for at leastten days .
Within three months from the time he receives said
list, he shall make out and return to the board of
trusteesa report,showing the amount collected, the
names of those who have paid, and those who are

nor shall he be credited in his settlement with the

made . Upon the returnof the collector'sreport, as
above provided, the board of trusteesshall, by order
duly entered of record,directa warrant to be made
out,signedby the chairman and countersignedby the
clerk, commanding the collectorto levy upon and sell
so much of the realproperty of the delinquents, nam-
ing them ,situatedin the town , as may be necessary

auction,to the highestbidder,at the court-house door

turn of the manner in which he has executed said war-

clerk shallkeep a recordof such sale, which shallbe
open for public inspection at allreasonable times .

teesat alltimes to keep the streets, alleys,lanes and
highways in said town in good order and condition,
and for every failure to do so they shall be liable to
indictment and fine, of not less than five nor more
than twenty -fivedollars.
§ 82. All acts or parts of acts in relation to the
town of Campbellsvilleand its government and offi-
cers, heretoforeenacted by the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , which are incon-
sistentwith thisact, are hereby repealed.
§ 83. The saidboard of trusteesshallhave power to
authorize and direct any collector by them appointed
to collect all taxes which have been heretofore levied
by the board of trusteesof said town which are un-
paid,and saidcollector,when so required,shallcollect
said taxes, and shall have all the powers for enforc-
ing the collectionof the same that are herein given to
the collector of taxes in said town .
§ 84. The corporate limits and boundary of said
town shall extend one -half mile from front door of
the court-house in said town in every direction.
§ 85. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterits passage .
Approved March 19,1888.

CHAPTER 583 .
AN ACT to incorporateand establisha public schoolforcolored
people in the town of Hawesville, and within a radiusof two
miles of the court -house in said town .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That all of the territoryembraced within a
radius of two miles of the court -house , in the town of
Hawesville, shallbe, and ishereby, incorporatedas a
school district, which shall be under the controland
management of a board of trustees elected by the
colored voterswithin the limitsof said territoryas
hereinafter directed .
§ 2. The persons so elected shall constitute and be
styled the board of trustees of the Hawesville colored
public school, and by that name shallbe a body -poli-
ticand corporate,and may sue and be sued, contract
and be contractedwith; may buy and selland convey
real estate and personalestatenecessary for school

board of trustees and their successors .

annually,and hold theirofficesuntil theirsuccessors
are electedand qualified. A majority of said board
shall constitutea quorum for the transaction of busi-
ness ; and in case of the death or resignation of any
of said trustees, or if, from any cause, thereshall be
a vacancy , the other members of the board shall fill
said vacancy by the appointment of some suitable
and qualifiedperson ; said trusteesto qualifywithin
one week after their election .

charge of their duties, shall take an oath to faith-
fullyperform the dutiesrequiredof them under this
act,beforea justiceof the peace or county judge .

appointees, and forthe management , controland gov-
ernment of said public school; and shallcause to be
kept a correctjournalof theirproceedings, which
shall be open at all times to the inspectionof any
citizenof Hawesville and the territorymentioned .
§ 6. The said board of trusteesshall appoint all
teachersand other persons necessaryto carry on said
school, prescribeall rules for their government ,and
fix theircompensation or salaries; and may dismiss
or suspend any teacheror other person appointed by

sioner prescribe the branches of education to be
taught in the school, grade the school and classes,
and prescribethe necessaryqualificationsfor and the
mode of examination of pupils or persons applying

urer of said trustees all the money due to the said

deemed residents of said district, and be entitled to
