CHAPTER 587 .
AN ACT to charter the Corinth Deposit Bank .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby established a bank of de-
posit in Corinth, Kentucky , with a capitalof twenty-
five thousand ($25,000) dollars, in shares of one
hundred dollarseach, to be subscribed and paid for
as hereinafterprovided ; and the subscribers, their
associatesand assigns,shallbe a body -politicand cor-
porate,by.the name and styleof the "Corinth Deposit
Bank ," and shall so continue for thirty years from its
organiz tion; and shallbe capable of contractingand
being contractedwith, of suing and being sued, of
impleadingand being impleaded, of answeringand
defending , in allcourts and places whatsoever ; it may
have a common seal, and change and renew the same
at pleasure .
§ 2. Said bank shall be under the direction and
control of five directors, all of whom must be stock-
holders, and , afterthe firstelection,shall be elected
every two years, commencing on the firstMonday of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY. 281

January after the first election, and on the first Monday in January every two years thereafter, and hold office until their successors are elected and qualified; they shall elect one of their number president, and shall hold regular meetings at such times as may be fixed by the by-laws; and other meetings may be held whenever deemed necessary; and in case of death or resignation of any director, the vacancy shall be filled by the board of directors. The directors shall have power to receive subscriptions for so many of the shares of the stock of said company remaining unsold as they may at times choose to sell; to declare dividends of the profits arising out of the business, and to appoint such officers, agents, and servants as they may deem necessary to conduct the business of the bank, and pay them such sums as wages and salaries, and to take from them such bond to secure the faithful performance of their duties, as they shall deem proper and reasonable. The stock shall be deemed personal property, and shall be assignable according to such rules as the board of directors shall from time to time establish; but said corporation shall have a lien on the stock to secure any indebtedness by the stockholders for any unpaid balance due thereon.

§ 3. The private property of the stockholders shall be exempt from the corporate debts, save that each stockholder shall be liable for corporate debts to the amount of his or her unpaid subscriptions to the capital stock.

§ 4. W. H. Daugherty, G. W. Trimnell, R. C. Ford, T. H. Doughtery, A. Bean, J. M. Minor, W. W. Scott, J. M. Lancaster, W. H. Childers are hereby appointed commissioners, any one of whom may open books and receive subscriptions for capital stock; and when one hundred shares have been subscribed, it shall be his or their duty (as the case may be) to give notice by written or printed notices or otherwise, and appoint a day for the election of a board of directors, who shall hold their office until the ensuing election.

CHAPTER 590 .
AN ACT to authorize the trustees of the town of Bardwell to bor-
row money for the purposeof improvingand openingstreetsinsaid
town .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of trustees of the town of Bard-
well are hereby authorized to borrow a sum of money
not exceeding one thousand dollars,for the purpose
of improving and opening streetsin said town ; and
may agree to pay for same any rate of interestnot
exceeding ten per cent.per annum . But before same
shall be borrowed an order to that effect shall be en-
tered upon the record book of said board, specify-
ing the amount to be borrowed ,the rate of interest
to be paid ; and said order shall show that a ma-
jority of said board consented thereto.
§ 2. When said order has been made , the police
judge of said town shallbe, and he is hereby , ap-
pointed to carry same into effect; and to that end
may execute the necessary bonds or note for the
sum borrowed, bearing the rate of interestagreed
on , and payable at the time stipulated by the board ,
and for which said corporationshallbe responsible
in their officialcapacity, and which may be enforced
againstsaid town by appropriate proceeding in law
or equity .
§ 3. The board is hereby authorized to annually
lay a speciallevy upon the taxable property in said
town , not exceeding twenty-five cents on each one
hundred dollars' worth,to be devoted exclusivelyto
the payment of the principaland interestupon the
money borrowed .
§ 4. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[Became a law withoutapprovalofGovernor March 19, 1888.]

CHAPTER 594 .

GroveSundaySchoolUnion of Campbell county," approved Febru-
ary11,1880.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealthof Kentucky :
§1.That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons, other than the board of directors of the
BeechGroveSunday School Union, or such person
or personsas may be authorized by said board, to
sellany kind of refreshmentsor products of the
soil,eitheron the grounds of the Beech Grove Sun-
daySchoolUnion, or withinone mile of the same,
on thesame day or days on which the said Union
holdsitsSunday schools,Sunday schoolcelebrations,
picnics,etc.
§2.Any person violatingthisact shall,upon con-
victionthereof,be finedin any sum notlessthan ten
dollarsnormore than fiftydollarsfor each offense,
recoverableby warrant in any court of competent
jurisdiction.
§3.This act shall not apply to such person or
personsas shall be regularly engaged in the busi-
nessofkeepinga generalstore,at hisor theirusual
placeof business within said limits.
§4 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage.
[Becamea law withoutapprovalofGovernorMarch 21,1888.]

CHAPTER 614 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof G. W. Baker , of Clay county .
WHEREAS , Daniel Gray being charged with murder
in the Clay circuitcourt,at itsOctober term,1886,and
it having been shown in open court that two of his
most important witnesseswere sick and unable to tes-
tifyinsaidcaseat saidterm,as they liveda distanceof
fifteenmilesfrom the county seatof Clay county, but
by an agreement of the Commonwealth's attorney,and
the attorneysfor the defendent,the court appointed
George W. Baker to representthe Commonwealth in
taking the deposition of the defendant's witnesses in
said case. The defendant is now serving a life sen-
tence in the State penitentiary, and was , at the time
of his conviction, hoplessly insolvent, and had no
property out of which the attorney'sfee could be
collected. And whereas , the action of the court in
appointing said G. W. Baker to act in said case for
the Commonwealth enabled the court to try the case
at thatterm of the court,thereby saved the State the
costof continuingsaidcase for another term, and the
expenditure of a considerable sum of money to pay a
number of witnesses, who were summoned on behalf
of the Commonwealth ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be, and
he is hereby, directed to draw his warrant on the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 331
Treasuryin favor of said G. W. Baker , for twenty
dollars,payableout of any money in the Treasury not
otherwiseappropriated.
§2.This act shall take effect from and after its
passage.
[Becamea law withoutapprovalof Governor March 24,1888.]

CHAPTER 617 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Solomon King , of Marshallcounty.
WHEREAS , It appears to the satisfaction of the
General Assembly that Nimrod King was duly ad-
judged a pauper idiotby the Marshall circuitcourt
on the 16th day of June ,1882 ; that on the 21st day
of June , 1882,E. King was appointed by said court as
committee of said idiot, and duly qualifiedas such ;
that said committee drew out of the Treasury of this
State moneys for the maintenance of said idiot at the
rateof seventy-fivedollarsper annum until June 18,
1884 ; thatsaidcommittee,E. King , died in January ,
1885 ; that on or about the 1st day of June , 1884 , the
said committee, E. King , being poor, enfeebled by
disease, and incompetent to care for and maintain said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 333
idiot, placedsaid idiot in the care and custody of
SolomonKing ; and whereas,it further appears that
saidSolomon King kept and kindly cared for said
idiotfrom June 1, 1884, untilafterJune 28, 1885, for
whichsaidSolomon King has receivedno compensa-
tionwhatever, and that no money has been drawn
fromsaidTreasury for the support of said idiotfor
thetimeinterveningbetween June 18, 1884, and June
28,1885; therefore,
Beitenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1.That Solomon King , of Marshall county, be ,
and he hereby is, allowedthe sum of seventy-five
dollarsformaintaining Nimrod King , a pauper idiot,
fromJune 18, 1884, untilJune 28, 1885, and said
amountishereby appropriated to compensate him for
maintenanceof saidpauper idiot,and theAuditorof
PublicAccounts is directed to draw his warrant on
theTreasuryinhisfavorforthatamount.
§2 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[BecamealawwithoutapprovalofGovernorMarch 24,1888.]

CHAPTER 648 .
AN ACT to authorizethe court of levy and claims of Menefee county
to levy an ad valorem tax for county purposes.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Menefee county , a
majority of the justicesof the peace of said county
being present and concurring, shall have power to
levy an ad valorem tax annually , to be used for
county purposes,upon allthe property in said county
subjectto taxation:Provided ,however, That not more
than twenty-fivecents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of property shall be levied or collectedin any
one year .
§ 2. All tax levied under this act shall be collected
in the same manner , for the same commissions , and
by the same officers, under the same liabilitiesand
penalties, that the revenue tax is collected: Provided ,
The provisionsof thisact shallnot apply to the town
of Frenchburg .
§ 3. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 24,1888 .

intendentof Shelby county to organize, and cause to
be held , for the normal improvement and better quali-
ficationof the teachersof Shelby county, a teachers'

December in each year.
§ 2. The trusteesof any common school in said
county may extend the term to any time not exceed

fund shall be prorated for the time the common

addition to those prescribed by the general school

branches,and requiretheiruniform use by pupils pur-

sage .

CHAPTER 653 .
AN ACT respectingvagrantsin the cityof Louisville.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That thefollowingpersonsare vagrants:
1. A personwho , not having visiblemeans to main-
tainhimself,liveswithout employment .
2. A personwho ,being an habitualdrunkard ,aban-
dons,neglects,or refusesto aidin the supportof his
family.
3. A person who has contracted an infectiousor
otherdiseasein the practice of drunkenness or de

404 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
bauchery, requiring charitableaid to restorehim to
health .
4. A person wandering abroad and begging, or who
goes about from door to door, or placeshimselfin the
streets,highways , passages, or other public places, to
beg or receive alms .
5. A person wandering abroad and lodging in bar-
rooms ,ale-houses,watch or station-houses,out-houses,
market -places, sheds, stables, barns or uninhabited
buildings, or in the open air, and not giving good
account of himself .
§ 2. A policeofficeror constablemust (or any agent
or officerof an incorporatedcharityorganizationsoci-
ety) arrestand bring beforethe Louisvillecitycourt
a vagrant under the provisions of this act ; and if such
arrestedperson be found,aftertrial,to be a vagrant as
herein defined, he shall be committed to the city
work -house for a term not exceeding six months , or if
he be a proper objectof charity,he may be sent to the
cityhospitalor to the city alms-house, the time and
place of his commitment being leftto the discretion
of the judge of said court : Provided,however, That
saidjudge shallnot commit any person to said hos-
pitalor alms-house without the authorityor consent
of the proper manager or custodian of said hospital
or alms -house .
§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage, and shallapply only to the cityof Louisville.
Approved March 24 , 1888 .

CHAPTER 659 .
AN ACT for the benefit of James D. Blackwell, sheriffof Webster
county ,and to allow him furthertime in which to execute his county
levy and State revenue bonds foryear 1888 .
WHEREAS , James D. Blackwell, sheriffof Webster
county, has,through inadvertence, failedto execute
his bonds for collection of the State revenue and
county levy for year 1888 ; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That said sheriff is allowed the further time of
thirtydays,from and afterthe passage of thisact, in
which to execute his bonds for collection of the State
revenue and county levy for said year 1888 ; and the
county judge of Webster county is authorized to
accept and approve such bonds if executed within
saidtime ; and saidbonds , when so executed, are to
have the same effect, and stand as if executed within
the time prescribed by law ; and said sheriffisrelieved
of alldisability(ifany )consequent upon his failureto
execute said bonds or either of them .
§ 2. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved March 24,1888.

CHAPTER 664 .
AN ACT for the protectionof water-works companies , and to prevent
the pollutionof reservoirsin Fayette county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. Itshallbe unlawful to put or throw , or cause
or permit to flow, or run any liquid materialor sub-
stance of any kind into the reservoirof any water-
works company inFayettecounty,whereby thewater

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 427
in such reservoirshallbe or may be polluted, or ren-
dered in any way unfitfordrinking purposes.
§2 . Where any such reservoiris supplied by sur-
face drainage,it shall also be unlawful to carry on
or conduct any slaughter-house or manufacture of
any kind in such place, or in such manner that the
flowor drainage from the same may at any time run
or flowintosuch reservoir; and each day upon which
the same shall be so conducted shall constitute a
separateoffense.
§3 . Any violationof the provisions of this act
shallbe punished by a fineof not lessthan twenty-
fivenor more than five hundred dollars, and shall
also render the offender liable in a civil action to
the company injured for double the amount of dam-
ages sustained by his act.
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 24, 1888.

CHAPTER 668 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Boxville Male and Female Seminary ,
of Union county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That W. P. Hamner , as principal, and Dr. Joe.
Allen, J. D. Blue, Francis Sigler, Sam . Taylor and
W. D. McCarty , as trustees, and theirsuccessors, ap-
pointed by the principal, shall be a body -corporate,
under the name of the Principal and Board of Trus-
tees of the Boxville Male and Female Seminary .
§ 2. That the principaland corps of instructors
shall have power to determine the course of study
and disciplinein said institute, provided it be co-
extensive with that prescribedby the best institu-
tionsof the same order ; thatthe principaland corps
of instructors shall conduct semi -annual examina-
tions, and shall, with the advice of the board of
trustees, whose duty it shall be to attend the ex-
aminations, confer upon such young ladiesand gen-
tlemen as have completed the prescribedcourse of
study and passed a reputable examination, the lit-
erary honors of the institution.
§3 . That there shall be a course of science and of
modern literature, and a more extensive course of
scienceand ancient literature, taught in said insti-
tution; and upon those who shallhave pursued the
former, the degree of Maid of Science or Bachelor
of Science, and upon those who have pursued the
latter, Maid of Arts or Bachelor of Arts, shall be
conferred .
§ 4. That should this institutionat any time pass
out of the hands of the presentprincipal, his suc-
cessorshallhave allthe rightsand privilegesspeci-
fied in this act .
§5. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 24,1888.

CHAPTER 677 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Ohio , Cumberland and MississippiRiver
Railway Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Francis M. Green , John Hammack , Thos .
Blackwell and James Yates, of Webster county ; and
J. W. Blue, A. H. Cardin, Dr. W. J. Deboe , J. С.
Elder, Jr., and T. J. Nunn , of Crittenden county,
Kentucky , their associates, successors and assigns
who may become stockholders, be, and they are
hereby, created a body -politicand corporate, by the
name and styleof the Ohio, Cumberland and Missis-
sippiRiver Railway Company ;and by that name shall
have perpetual succession, and be capable of suing
and being sued, pleading and being impleaded , de .
fending and being defended, in law and equity, in
all courts and elsewhere as a natural person ; and
said corporation may have and use a common seal,
and set aside and renew or alter the same at pleas-
ure ; and by said corporate name may contract and
be contracted with ; and by that name shall be, and
are hereby , invested with all the powers and immu-
nitiesof natural persons , in acquiring and disposing
of all realand personal estatewhich it may be need-
ful to acquire or disposeof in order to facilitatethe
object and purpose of thisact and of this corpora-
tion .
§2 . The capital stock of said company may be
any amount not exceeding thirtythousand dollarsper

436 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
mile for each mile of said road which said company
shall build or own in this State, to be divided into
shares of one hundred dollars each ; and individu-
als, railroadcompanies and other corporations, either
in or out of this State, are authorized to subscribe
for and hold stock in said company .
§ 3. The corporatorsnamed in the firstsection, or
so much of them as may act, may open books for
subscriptionto the capital stock of said company ,
at such times and in such places as they may think
proper, and give such noticeof the times and places
of opening the same as they think proper ; and when
twenty thousand dollarsshall have been subscribed,
they may calla meeting of the subscribers, notify-
ing each subscriber of the time and place of meet-
ing, and proceed to organizesaid company by electing
from the subscribers a board of directors of seven
in number , who shall elect a president, vice-presi-
dent, secretaryand treasurerfrom the stockholders
of the company ; the president and directorsshall
have power to adopt such by -laws for the govern-
ment of the company , its officers, agents and em-
ployes, in the general conduct and management of
the affairsand business of the company , as may be
needful and proper .
§4 . That the board of directors shall provide in the
by -laws foran annual electionof the board of direct-
ors,consistingof seven in number ,who shallbe stock-
holders of saidcompany , and fixthe time and place,
and determine the manner of the firstand succeeding
election of directors; the directors shall severally
hold their officefor one year , and until their success-
ors are elected and qualified; it shall require a
majority to constitutea quorum of the board of di-
rectors for the transaction of business ; at the elec-
tion of directors,and allother meetings of stockholders
in said company , stockholdersshall be entitledto one
vote for each share of stock so held, which may be
cast in person or by written proxy ; provisionsshall

CHAPTER 678 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the Williamstown graded free school dis-
district .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the electionheld on the second day of
July,1887, forthe purposes of taking the sense of the

444 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .1
legalvotersresidingin the Williamstown graded free
school district, in Grant county, on the propositionto
vote an annual tax of fiftycentson the one hundred
dollarsof property within saiddistrict, for the organi-
zation, equipment and maintenance of a graded free
school in said district, and to elect trustees therefor,
ishereby ratifiedand confirmed , and allthe acts and
doings of the said board of trustees in reference to or-
ganizingthe said school and levying the said tax, not
contrary to the Constitution of this State or of the
United States, are also ratifiedand confirmed ,
§2 . For the more speedy and satisfactorywork of
providing a suitable house and gounds for the accom-
modation of said graded free school district, the
board of trustees thereof is hereby authorized and
empowered to issue and sellthe coupon bonds of the
saidgraded freeschool district, not exceeding in the
aggregatethe sum of eight thousand ($8,000) dollars.
The said bonds shall be issued in denominations of
not less than one hundred nor more than one thou-
sand dollarseach,inthe discretionof the saidtrustees;
shallrun for a period of not exceeding thirtyyears,
but be redeemable at the pleasure of the board ;
bear interest at a rate not exceeding six per centum
per annum , the saidinterestpayable semi-annually at
the office of the treasurer of said board . Said bonds
shallbe made payable to bearer, and pass by sale and
delivery; and shallbe signed by the presidentof said
board,and attestedby the secretarythereof, and may
be issued at such times and in such amounts as the
necessitiesof saidgraded freeschool districtmay re-
quire; but none of said bonds shall be sold for less
than the face par value, and the proceeds of all sales
of any of said bonds shallbe paid to the treasurerof
said board, and shall only be used for the purposes
contemplated in this act.
§ 3. After the issue and sale of so many of said
bonds as shall be necessary to purchase suitable
grounds,and build, furnishand thoroughlyequip a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 445
suitable house for the use and accommodation of said
graded freeschooldistrict,the annual tax to be levied
thereaftershallonly be for a sum sufficientto pay the
estimated legitimateexpenses of running saidgraded
free school, and pay the interest on said bonds ; and
any surplus revenue left on hand at the end of any
year shallgo to createand build up a sinking fund
for the purchase and retirement of said bonds .
§ 4. The county superintendent of common schools
for Grant county , and the trustees of the Williams-
town graded free school district,are hereby authorized
and empowered to make more definiteand certain the
boundary of said graded free school district, and
when so made certain and definite, the same shall be
recorded in the officeof the said county superintend-
ent, and also in the minute -book kept by said trus-
tees .
§ 5. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 26,1888 .

CHAPTER 680 .
AN ACT to amend an act incorporatingthe Lancaster City Hall Asso-
ciation .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1.That section10 of an act, entitled"An act to in-
corporatethe Lancaster City Hall Association," ap-
proved March 9,1867, and amended March 2,1880, be,
and the same is hereby , amended as follows : That the
trusteesof the town of Lancaster (Garrard county,
Kentucky ),a majorityofthem concurringtherein,may
issue a sufficientnumber of bonds , and no more , to
exchange for the outstanding bonds issued under said
amended act, which shall be in such amounts as said
trusteesmay desire, payable in fiveyears from the
date , and bearing interestfrom date until paid . Said
bonds shall not be sold for less than their par value ,
or they may be exchanged at par for the outstanding
bonds ; ifsold,the money realizedtherefrom shallbe
applied in paying off and cancelingthe outstanding
bonds . Said trusteesshallhave the right to pay off
all or any portion of said bonds at any time before
maturity .
§ 2. This act shall be in force from itspassage .
Approved March 26,1888..

CHAPTER 683 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof B. F. Reeks, of Daviesscounty.
WHEREAS , B. F. Reeks was , by the judgment of
the McLean county court,granted a licenseto keep a
tavernat Beech Grove,in McLean county,Kentucky,
with theprivilegeof sellingvinous,malt and spiritu-
ous liquorsby retail,on the sixthDecember ,1886,and
paid to the clerk of said court the tax required by
law, viz., one hundred and fiftydollars, and two dol-
larsand fiftycentsclerk'sfees; and on appeal by the
Commonwealth to the McLean circuit court said order
and judgment of the McLean county court granting.

-448 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said licensewas set aside and held for naught on the
twenty -ninth December , 1886 ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts draw his
warrant upon the Treasurer of this State in favor of
B. F. Reeks for one hundred and fiftydollars, which
shall be paid out of any funds in the Treasury not
otherwise appropriated .
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
[Became a law without approval of Governor March 24, 1888.]
CHAPTER 684 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof T. L. Rawlins, of Clay county.
WHEREAS ,Daniel Gray ,being charged with murder
in the Clay circuitcourt, at its October term, 1886,
and it having been shown in open court that two of
his most important witnesses were sick and unable to
testifyin said court, as they liveda distanceof fifteen
miles from the county seat of Clay county . But by
an agreement of the Commonwealth attorney and the
attorneysforthe defendant, the court appointed T. L.
Rawlins , an examiner of Clay county,to go,in com-
pany with an attorneyfor the defendant, and also an
attorneyappointed by the court,to act for the Com-
monwealth , viz: George W. Baker , and take the
depositionsof the defendant's witnesses, which were
read before the jury as testimony for the defendant .
The defendant is now serving a life-time sentence in
the Statepenitentiary,and was ,at the time of his con-
viction,hopelesslyinsolvent,and had no property out
of which the examiner could collect his fee for said
service; and whereas, the action of the court in ap-
pointingsaid Rawlins to take saiddepositionenabled
the court to try the case at that term, thereby saved
the State the cost of continuing said case for another

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 449
term,and the expenditure of a considerableamount
of money to pay a number of witnesseswho were
summoned on behalf of the Commonwealth ; there-
fore
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and
he is hereby, directed to draw his warrant on the
Treasury in favor of said T. L. Rawlins for eleven
dollarsand fiftycents, payable out of any money in
the Treasury not otherwise appropriated.
§ 2. And this act shall take effectfrom and after its
passage .
Approved March 26,1888.

three,in Graves county .

§ 1. That the county superintendent of Graves

tionof proposednew court-houseand jail,and toprovideforpayment
of cost of same .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the Bell county
court to order the sheriff of said county to open
a poll, at each of the voting precinctsof said county,
on Monday , the thirtiethday of April, 1888, or as
soon thereafteras county court may order, to take
the sense of the qualifiedvoters of said county as
to whether the proposed new court-house, clerk'sof-
fices, jailand jailer'sresidenceshallbe located upon
the present public square, or at some other place
within the present corporate limits of the town of
Pineville, to be determined upon as hereinafterpro-
vided ; and the said county court shall appoint the
officersto hold said election, and in making said
appointments shall have due regard to the general
laws on that subject.
§2 . That in making out the poll-books for the va-
rious precincts for the purpose of taking the vote on
said question, the clerk shall prepare one column
in which to record the votes of those who are in favor
of locatingthe proposed new public buildings upon
the present public square , and one column in which
to record the votes of those who are in favor of
the locationof said proposed new public buildings
at some place other than the present public square
within the corporate limits of Pineville; and the
poll-books shall be deliveredby the sheriffto the
several clerks of the election . The clerk of said
election shall propound to each individual voter vot .
ing at said electionthe following question: " Are
you forthe locationof the proposed new court-house,
jailand jailer'sresidence, upon the present public

square, or at some other place within the corporate
limits of Pineville, to be fixed upon hereafter ? ”

cationof said proposed new publicbuildingsupon
the present public square, his vote shall be so re-
corded , and if he answers that he is in favor of the
locationof said proposed new public buildings at
some other place than the present public square
within the corporate limits of Pineville, then his
vote shall be so recorded .
§3 . That the poll-books shall be kept sealed by

quired by law in the electionof county officers.

ings upon the present public square, the proposed

CHAPTER 705 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to incorporatethe town of
Helena,in Mason county," approvedMarch 8,1854.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act to incorporate the town of Helena ,
in Mason county,be, and is hereby, amended so that
the corporate limits of said town be extended to and
bounded on the south by the lands of W. Y. Wells
and Charles Minor ; on the east to the lands of W.
Y. Wells ; on the north and west to the lands of
Wm . Luttrell .
§2 . That on the firstSaturday in April, 1888, and
every year thereafter, there shall be elected, by the
qualifiedvoters resident in or owning real estate
within the corporate limits of said town , a police
judge and town marshal for said town for the term
of one year , whose term of office shall commence
on the second Monday aftertheir election, and con-
tinue for one year, and until their successors are
duly electedand qualified.
§3 . Said police judge shall have the same juris-
diction, concurrent, criminaland penal, with justices
of the peace of said county, and shallhave the same
fees,rights, duties, and be under the same liabilities,
as any justiceof the peace in said county . He shall
have exclusive jurisdictionin all cases arising under ,
or violationsof, the by -laws and ordinances of said
town .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 505
§4 . The marshal of said town shall give bond ,
with sufficientsecurity, to be approved by the trus-
tees of the town , for the faithfuldischarge of all
his duties, and payment of money collected, and
shallhave the same powers , duties, fees and liabili-
ties within the limits of said town and county as
any constable of said county .
§ 5. The policejudge shallkeep a record, and have
the same rightsand powers, and be governed by the
same laws in all other respects, not provided for in
thisact, as justicesof the peace.
§ 6. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved March 26, 1888.

CHAPTER 730 .

Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe lawful forsaidFrank Preston,
Mont . Preston and G. B. Preston, their lessees, as-
signees and grantees, to charge and collecta rea-
sonable wharfage upon all shipments of goods or
merchandise of any kind placed on said wharf -boat
while in course of shipment , said wharfage not to
exceed the usual ratescharged along said river upon
similar articles. They shall also have the right to
charge a fee,of not exceeding one dollarper day , for
any boat or craft anchored or tied at said wharf -boat :
Provided , That said fee shallnot be charged of any

freight. They shall have a lien for said wharfage

wharf -boat ; said lien to be enforced as other liens for

CHAPTER 731 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof G. T. Finn, of Simpson county.
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whereas Gerald Thomas Finn , of Simp-
son county, Kentucky, is deemed duly qualifiedto
practiceas an attorneyand counselorat law ; and
whereas, said Finn is not twenty-one years of age;

LAWS OF KENTUCKY. 549

now, therefore, be it enacted, that the legal disabilities of said Finn be, and they are hereby, removed, and the judges of the circuit and Superior courts are hereby authorized and empowered to grant unto said Finn license to practice as an attorney and counselor at law, upon like conditions and in such manner as to applicants or persons twenty-one years of age as provided by law; and all laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed.

§ 2. This act shall take effect at its passage.
[Became a law without approval of Governor March 27, 1888.]

CHAPTER 732.

AN ACT to prescribe the pay of justices of the peace in Edmonson and Hancock counties.

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That justices of the peace in and for the counties of Edmonson and Hancock shall be allowed, as compensation while serving as members of the county court and court of claims of said counties, the sum of three dollars per diem, to be paid out of the county levy of said counties.

§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in force from its passage.
[Became a law without approval of Governor March 27, 1888.]

CHAPTER 734 .
AN ACT to vacate " Park Place " in the cityof Louisville, and pro-
vide for the opening and dedication of Ormsby avenue through the
same .
WHEREAS , James M. Bryant and Theodore Harris
heretofore laid out and dedicated to public use as a
park a stripof land in the cityof Louisvilleknown
as " Park Place ," with streets on both sides thereof ;
and whereas,the owners of the property frontingon
said " Park Place " are desirous of having the same
vacated for public use as a park , and Ormsby avenue
extended through the same ; and whereas, the said
James M. Bryant and Theodore Harris have by their
deed duly executed , consented that said Park Place ,
and the streetslaidout by them on both sidesof it,
may be vacated,and have relinquishedallreversion-
ary interestswhich they have therein, and have con-
sented that Ormsby avenue may be extended through
saidPark Place, and that the streetsso vacated shall
become and form a part of the lots fronting thereon ;
therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That " Park Place " in the cityof Louisville,
and the streetsnow laid out and existing on the north
and south sides thereof,be, and the same are hereby,
vacatedfor public use as originallydedicated.
§2 . That Ormsby avenue in the cityof Louisville
be,and itishereby, extended and dedicatedto public
use, from the east side of First street to the east line
of said Park Place . Said avenue shall be of the uni-
form width of sixty feet, and the center line thereof
shall be the center line of said Park Place .
§ 3. So much of Park Place,and the existingstreets
on both sidesthereof, as shallnot be required in the
extension of Ormsby avenue as above provided,shall
become and remain a part of the lotsfrontingthereon,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 553 :
the same as ifsaidlotshad originallyextended out to
the line of Ormsby avenue as above dedicated .
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Became a law without approval of Governor March 27, 1888.]

572 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 740 .
AN ACT to authorizethe people of Casey county to vote on pro-
hibitingthesaleof liquors, and fixingthepenaltyforthesaleof
liquorsin said county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the officersappointed by the county court
of Casey county to hold electionsin said county in
the year 1888, are hereby authorized and directed to
open a pollat the variousvoting placesin said county
on the firstSaturday in the month of October, 1888,
to take the sense of legal voters on the question
of prohibiting the sale of liquor in said county .
The pollsshall be kept open by the officersof elec-
tion from 7 o'clock, A. M. , to 6 o'clock, P. M .; and
the question shall be put to each voter, "Are you
for or against the sale of liquor in Casey county ?"
§ 2. The poll-books of the electionordered by the
first section of this act shall be returned to the
clerk'sofficeof Casey county by the Wednesday
after the election, properly sealed and signed by
the officersholding said election, as other poll-books
are by law required to be signed and sealed. Said
books shall be opened by the county judge, clerk
and sheriff, and the votes by them counted ; and
if it shall be shown by said count that a majority
of those voting at said election have voted for pro-
hibiting the sale of liquor in said county , the re-
sult shall be so declared by being entered on the
order-books of said county court of Casey .
§3 . That if it be ascertained, by the electionor-
dered by the firstsection of this act, that a ma-
jorityof those voting at saidelectionvoted to prohibit
the saleof liquor in saidcounty, then it shallbe un-
lawful, from and afterthe entry of the resultof said
electionin the order-book of the clerk'soffice, for
any person or persons to sellspirituous, vinous or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 573
malt liquors in said county : Provided , That any
person or persons who may , at the time of said elec-
tion, have licenseto sellliquor as a beverage, may
continue to do so until the expiration of the time
for which the licensewas given.
§ 4. That afterthe entry of the resultof said elec-
tion shall have been made , the sale of spirituous,
vinous or malt liquors in said county shallbe con-
sidered a misdemeanor , and punished by a fine of
not less than ten nor more than fiftydollars for
each offense, and each sale shall be considered a
separate offense.
§5 . That the county judge,policejudge and magis-
tratesof said county are authorizedtoissuea warrant
of arrest, upon the oath of any respectablecitizenof
saidcounty, againstany one charged with the offense
of violatingthe third sectionof this law, without
indictmentby a grand jury : Provided , The Common-
wealth attorney or the defendant may demand and
have a jury to try the case.
§ 6. That should a majority of those voting at said
electionvote against prohibitingof the saleof liquor
in said county , authority is hereby given the county
judge of Casey county to callanother electionfor the
same purpose, at some other time not lessthan one
year from the time of holding the firstelection ; and
should a majority of the voters who voted at the
second electionvote against the sale of liquor in said
county, and the result thereof entered of record,
then the provisions of this act shall at once thereafter
be in fullforce and effectas though a majority had
voted for prohibition at the firstelection authorized
by this act.
§ 7. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved March 27, 1888 .

574 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 741 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Fayette Building and Loan
Association .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an act to incorporatethe Fayette Building
and Loan Association, approved February 29, 1888,
be, and the same is hereby, amended as follows: By
striking of the fifthsection of said act, and insert-
ing and enacting in lieu thereof as the fifthsection,
the following, to-wit : Section 5. The object of said.
associationbeing to enable itsmembers ,by theirearn-
ings and savings, to acquire homes and other prop-
erty,itsbusiness shallbe to loan itscapitalstock as
paid in, and the accumulations thereof, only upon
mortgages and loans upon real estate ample in amount
to securethe same , preferencebeing given in allcases
to members of said association; and said loans shall
be made at such rate of interest, not exceeding six
per cent., as may be agreed by the parties thereto,
together with such premiums therefor as may be
offered by the parties to whom the loans may be made .
§ 2. The board of directorsshall consist of any num-
ber fixed in the by -laws not exceeding eleven .
§3 . That said associationmay provide in its by-
laws upon what terms persons may enter and stock
subscribed in any particularclass, and upon what
terms members may withdraw from any particular
class; and so much of the act to which this is an
amendment as requirespersons enteringa classafter
itshallhave commenced operationsto pay up equal in
amount with those firstentering such class,be, and
the same is hereby , repealed .
§4 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 27, 1888.

CHAPTER 742 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to regulatethe saleof spirit-
uous,vinousor maltliquorsin thisCommonwealth ," sofarasitapplies
to the countiesof Menefee ,Powell and Wolfe .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled"An act to regulatethe
saleof spirituous, vinous or malt liquors in this Com-
monwealth ," approved January 26,1874, be, and the
same ishereby,amended by adding the word "county "
as follows, to- wit : Before the words "civildistrict"
in the fourth,eighth and tenth lines in sectionone ;
and before the words "civil district" in the seventh
linein section three ; and before the word "district"
in the sixth line of section four ; and before the word
"district" in the third line of section five ; and before
the word "district " in the fourth and sixth lines of
section six ; and before the word "town " in the second
line of section nine .
§ 2. That the election provided for in the act to
which this is an amendment to take the sense of the
people " as to whether they are for or against the sale
of spirituous, vinous or malt liquors," shall not be
held on the day of any regular State,town ,county,
city or districtelection, but the county court shall
name in the order the day on which itshallbe held.
3. This act shall only apply to Menefee ,Powell
and Wolfe counties .
§4 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afterits passage .
Approved March 27,1888 .

CHAPTER 743 .
AN ACT to empower Fayette county to make subscriptions to the
capitalof Jack's Creek Turnpike Road .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the county court of
Fayette county, a majority of the justicesof said
county concurring, to make subscriptionof stock for
macadamizing the Jack's Creek Road ,in said county :
Provided , Said subscriptionshall not exceed sixteen
hundred dollarsper mile, and a like proportion for
every fractionthereof: And provided, That no money
so subscribed by said court shall be paid except on
the terms and conditionsnow provided by law .
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 27,1888.

CHAPTER 752 .
AN ACT to authorize the city of Owensboro to aid the construction
of the Owensboro and Indiana bridge by subscriptionof stock or
donation .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the common councilof the cityof Owens-
boro, Kentucky , be, and they are, authorized and
empowered to aid and assistthe constructionand
building of the Owensboro and Indiana bridge ; and
in order to induce and to encourage itsspeedy build-
ing at or near the cityof Owensboro , acrossthe Ohio
river, said council may subscribe stock, or donate any
sum , not to exceed one hundred thousand dollars,
payable in the bonds of said city, which said bonds
shall bear a rate of interestnot exceeding fiveper
cent. per annum , payable semi-annually, and shall
mature at such time or times as the council may , by
ordinance , determine , not later than thirty years after
theirdate : Provided, That no such subscriptionof
stock or donation shall be authorized , nor bonds
issuedin payment thereof, untilafterthe questionof
such subscription or donation shall be submitted to
the qualifiedvotersof said city,and a majority of all
the qualifiedvoters of said city shall vote in favor
of such subscription or donation . The common coun-
cilshalldesignate the time when said electionshall
be held, and make such orders in relationto public
notice of the election, and all other matters in rela-
tionthereto, as shall,in theirjudgment , be necessary
to obtain a fairexpression of the popular will upon
the question . In the exercise of their own discretion,
the council may , at any time , order said election on
the proposition to subscribe stock or make a donation
to said bridge not exceeding one hundred thousand
dollars . This act shall not be construed to vest said
city with power or authority to both subscribe stock

and make a donation,the true intentof thisact being
to authorize said city to either subscribe stock or
donate any sum , not exceeding one hundred thousand
dollars, to said Owensboro and Indiana bridge, but
not to do both. For the purpose of determining the
number of qualifiedvotersof said city, recourseshall
be had to the number listed by the city assessor at
the last assessment prior to the taking of any vote
under thisact, or the councilmay cause a registration
or listof the voters to be made by the city assessor or
some other competent person for said purpose.
§ 2. That said bonds issued in payment of said sub- Bonds
scriptionor donation shallbe deliveredto the presi-
dent of the board of directorsof saidbridgecompany
only when said bridge shallhave been fullycompleted
and permanently built, and railroadtrainsrunning
over the same : Provided , That said bridge isso built
and completed within fiveyears from the date of said
election authorizing said subscription of stock or
donation .

CHAPTER 779 .
AN AUT to amend the charter of the town of Bardwell , in Carlisle
county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the board of trustees of the town of Bard-
well,in Carlislecounty, be,and they are hereby, au-
thorizedand empowered to lay out and establish,by
ordinance, such streetsand alleysin saidtown as may
be deemed necessary or convenient for the use and
enjoyment of the public; and for this purpose they
may , by theirofficersor agents, enter upon and ap-
propriatethe private property of any person or per-
sons, and to that end they are hereby authorized and
empowered to contractwith the owners of any such
property forsuch streetsand alleys, and to receivea
conveyance for same upon such conditionsas may be
agreed upon .
§ 2. If said board of trustees is unable to agree with
the owners of such property as may be laidoffby or-
dinance, into streetsor alleys, as to compensation
therefor, or if said owners, by reason of any disabil-
ity, can not contract, or if such owner be a non -resi-
dent of the county, then,in eitherof such cases, the
board of trusteesmay , upon applicationto the police
court of said town , procure a writof ad quod dam-
num to issue,directedto the sheriffor some constable
of Carlislecounty, commanding him , within a time
thereinnamed , to summon a good and lawful jury of
twelve men to meet upon the land, or convenient
thereto, which isso taken by saidstreetor alley, and ,
after having been sworn as in the condemnation of a
publichighway ,they shallproceed,afterhaving heard
the evidence, to assess the damages which the owner
has sustained by reason of his property having been
taken for such streetor alley.

§ 3. If such owner be a residentof the county , no
such condemnation shall be had until he shall have
been served with a copy of such writfor at leastten
days before the meeting of said jury, nor if such
owner be laboring under any disability, other than
thatof coverture, untildefensebe made by guardian
ad litem,nor if such owner be a non -resident, untila
defense be made by attorneyappointed for that pur-
pose .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the policejudge to at-
tend and preside at such trials, and to keep a record
of the proceedings, which he shallreturn to and file
in his office. Either party may except to the verdict
within three days afteritisreturned, and saidexceр-
tions shallbe triedby said policejudge, and he may ,
if he thinks proper, award a new trial.
§5. The board of trustees,or the defendant, may ,
within sixtydays afterthe verdict,prosecute an ap-
peal to the common pleas court of saidcounty . The
mode of taking such appeal shall be the same as an
appeal from a justice'scourt,except,when the party
taking the appeal is laboring under a disabilityother
than coverture, no bond shallbe required. The cost
of the appeal shallbe regulated as on other appeals
from inferior courts .
§ 6. In no case shall the said board of trustees open
any such street or alley until the compensation ad-
judged to the owner has been actuallypaid,or secured
in such manner as the court,upon finaltrial, shall

§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved March 29,1888.

act by selling or trading any spirituous, vinous,

unlawful , or fail or refuse to perform any duty im-

more than two hundred dollars, or imprisoned in the

CHAPTER 785 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Deposit Bank at Fulton , Fulton county.
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby establisheda Savings and
Deposit Bank in Fulton, Fulton county, Kentucky ,
with a capitalof two hundred thousand dollars, in
shares of one hundred dollars each , to be subscribed
and paid for as hereinafter provided ; and the sub-
scribers to said stock , their associates, successors and
assigns, shallbe a body -politicand corporate, by the
name and style of Deposit Bank , and shall so con-
tinue for thirtyyears from the date of itsorganiza-
tion, unlesssooner dissolvedby a majority in interest

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 659
of the stockholderstherein; and shallhave and may
exerciseallthe rightsand privilegesof a naturalper-
son in contractingand being contractedwith, and as
such may sue and be sued, and plead, answer and
defend ,in any court or place whatever ; and may have
and use a common seal, and change the same at
pleasure .
§ 2. Said bank shall be under the control and man-
agement of a board of nine directors, and shall hold
their offices until their successors are elected and have
qualified; and after the firstelection under this char-
terthey shallbe electedannually on the firstMonday
in January of each year , or as soon as possible there-
after; and in case of a vacancy for any cause, the
remaining directors may fillthe same with other
stockholders; they shall elect one of their number
president ; they shall have power to sellany of their
stock not subscribed for ; to declare dividends arising
out of the profitsof the business of said bank ; to
appoint such officers, agents and employes as may be
necessary to properly conduct the business of said
bank , and pay them for theirservices, and may take
such bonds from the president, cashier or other em
ployes of said bank as they may deem necessary to
insurea faithfulperformance of their respectivedu-
ties; and may make and enforcesuch by -laws as may
be necessary for the proper management and control
of the affairsof said bank : Provided , The same be
not inconsistent with the Constitution or laws of this
State or of the United States .
§3 . That James P. Tyler, J. W. Chambers , C. E.
Rice, D. J. Bard , W. T. Carr, W. W. Evans , J. М.
Freeman , L. Barry , J. A. Wyatt , T. D. Pierce and
J. W. Mott are hereby constitutedand appointed a
board of directors for said bank , to hold officeas such
until their successors are elected and have qualified;
and they may fillany vacancy occurringin theirnum-
ber, and shall provide for an electionof directorsby
the stockholders in one month after itsorganization.

660 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
The directorsaforesaid shall cause the cashier to give
due and proper noticeto allthe stockholders,by pub-
licationin some newspaper printed in Fulton , or by
written or printed noticesthrough the mails, of the
proposed time and manner of holding said firstelec-
tion,at which every stockholder shall be entitledto
cast one vote, in person or by proxy , for each share
of stock owned by him , and on which he has paid in
full all the called installments due at the date of said
election .
§ 4. The board of directors, as named in section
three of this act, may appoint one or more of their
number to open books, and take subscriptionsto
the capitalstock of said bank , and to receiveand
hold in trust, for the use and benefit of the sub-
scribers, any sums , not less than five dollars per
share, that may be paid in by any subscribersat or
afterthe time of making said subscriptionand before
the organization of the bank ; and as soon as five
hundred shares of said stock shall have been sub-
scribedfor, they may organizesaid bank by electing
a presidentand appointinga cashier; and may begin
business as soon as seventy -five hundred dollars of
the capitalhas been paid in : Provided , That the
cashier shall firstgo before the county judge, or
some justiceof the peace,clerk of a court of record,
or notary public, and make and subscribeto an oath
(or affirmation) that said seventy-fivehundred dollars
of capitalhas actuallybeen subscribedand paid in.
§ 5. After the organizationof said bank , the calls
for payments of installmentsor the subscriptions
of stock shall not at any one time exceed ten dollars
per share, nor be made oftener than one call per
month ; and at leastten days' noticeshall be given
each subscriber of the time such installment will be
due ; and any share of stock on which any called
installmentis at any time thirtydays over due , may
be declared forfeited by the board of directors after
ten days' notice in writing to the owner thereofof

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 661
their intention to forfeitsame , provided the same
stillremains unpaid . All shares of stock so for-
feitedby the board shallbe sold at public auction,
after notice to pay , first, all such due or overdue
installments to the bank and the expense of making
the sale, and the remainder, if any , shallbe placed
to the credit of the stockholder in the books of the
bank , and notice shallbe given him of the amount
thereof .
§ 6. The stock in said bank shall be deemed per-
sonal property, and shall be transferableonly on the
books of the bank by the stockholder,his agent or
attorney , under such rules and regulations as the
directors shall from time to time establish; and
every stockholder shallbe individuallyliableto the
creditorsof the bank for the full amount of the
unpaid stock subscribedforand owned by him .
§ 7. Said bank , when organized, may receivede-
positsof gold and silvercoin, bank notes, and all
other notes which may be lawfully circulatedas
money , and repay the same in such manner and at
such times as may be agreed on with the depositor
by general or specialcontract; may transact a gen-
eral banking business, loan money , discount paper,
buy and sell bills of exchange , promissory notes,
uncurrent money , stocks, bonds and other evidences
of debt ; take personal, collateral, or other security
for the payment of same , and dispose of such stocks,
bonds and securitiesin accordance with the pro-
visions of an act, entitled " An act for the benefit
of the incorporated banks of Kentucky ," approved
March 16, 1871.
§ 8. Said bank may acquire,hold and use such real
estate, goods and chattels,as may be necessaryforthe
transactionof itsbusiness, or which may be conveyed
to it as securityfor,or purchased in satisfactionof,
any debt, judgment or decree, and may selland con-
vey the same , and may buy itsown stock in satisfac-
tionof any such debt,judgment or decree, but must

662 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
resellthe same as soon as practicable; but no real
estate purchased by it in satisfactionof any debt,
judgment or decree shallbe held for a longer period
than fiveyears .
§ 9. Said bank may receivedeposits from minors
and married women , and the receiptsand acquittances
of such minors and married women shall be valid and
binding .
§10 The president, cashier, directors, and other
officersof said bank , before entering on the discharge
of theirrespectiveduties,shalleach take an oath (or
affirmation) before the county judge, or some justice
of the peace, clerk of a court of record, or notary
public, faithfully, and to the best of theirskilland
abilityand judgment ,to discharge all the duties of
their respective officesor stations under this charter,
or which may be required of them by the by -laws,
rulesand regulations, of the corporation, and that
they will not sanctionor permit the violationof any
of the provisionsof this charterduring theircontin-
uance in office .
§ 11. The Legislaturereservesthe right to amend ,
alteror repeal this charter at pleasure .
§ 12. This act shall take effectfrom and after its
passage .
Approved March 30, 1888.

CHAPTER 788 .
AN ACT to establisha Normal School at Augusta ,in Bracken county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an institutionof learningishereby estab-
lished in Augusta , in Bracken county, by the name
and style of " The Augusta Normal School," which
shallbe under the controlof George Doniphan , C. R.
McCormick , G. H. McKibben , T. S. Bradford, Geo .
W. Moneyhon , and J. R. Minor and their successors,
as trusteesof said institution. If at any time a va-
cancy shalloccur by death,removal, or resignation,
the other trusteesshall appoint a suitableperson to
fillthe vacancy. They shall have power to appoint
one of their number president of theirboard, and
such other officersas they may deem proper. They
shall have power to employ such professors of learn-
ing as they may desire to teach said normal school,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 665
and may co-operatewith the common schooltrustees,
and make such arrangements as may be agreeableand
advantageous to the institutionhereby created, and
the advancement of learning; they may hold annual
commencements and public examinations of the pu-
pils, and may confer such honorary degrees and di-
plomas as is usual in collegesand high schools in
this Commonwealth . They may adopt all needful
by -laws, rules and regulations, necessary for the
government of said institution, and shallbe a body-
corporate, under the name and style of " The Au-
gusta Normal School ;" and may contract and be
contracted with, sue and be sued, acquire and hold
real or personal property, sell and dispose of the
same in their corporate name .
§ 2. That said normal school shall receive students
of both sexes, and any degree of advancement .
§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 30, 1888.

CHAPTER 799 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Louisville Abstract and Loan
Associationby changingitsname to "Kentucky TitleCompany ," and
otherwise .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky :
That the charter of the Louisville Abstract and Loan
Association, as containedin an act to incorporatethe
LouisvilleAbstract and Loan Association, approved
March 20th,1876, and in an act,entitled"An act to

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 689
amend an act, entitled'An act to incorporate the
Louisville Abstract and Loan Association ,' approved
April 7,1886," be, and the same is hereby, amended
in the followingparticulars, to wit :
§ 1. The name of saidcorporationishereby changed
to that of "Kentucky TitleCompany ," under which
name it shall have and exercise all the franchises,
powers and rights now possessedby said Louisville
Abstract and Loan Association .
§ 2. In addition to the powers conferred by the acts
above mentioned , thiscorporationshallhave the right
to transact all business authorized by the acts above
named in respect to real estate situate anywhere in
the Stateof Kentucky , in the same manner and upon
the same terms as said associationisnow permitted to
transact such business in reference to lands in Jeffer-
son county . This corporation shall likewise have
power to undertake and contract for the making of
maps of cities, towns and counties,or any part thereof,
for individualsor corporations,privateor municipal,
upon such terms as may be agreed upon between the
parties .
§3 . The principalplace of business of saidcorpo-
ration shall be in Louisville, Kentucky , but it may
establishbranch officesor agenciesin other partsof
the State. The board of directorsmay electalso a
vice-president, and may appoint or employ such other
officers,agentsand employesas may be convenientin
the transactionof the businessof the corporation.
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .

CHAPTER 806 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof J.Soule Smith,of Fayettecounty.
WHEREAS , On the thirtiethday of September ,
1885, the Governor of Kentucky appointed J. Soule
Smith , of Fayette county,to representthisCommon-
wealth as Commissioner to the North , Central and
South American Exposition at New Orleans; and
whereas, said J. Soule Smith did prepare for and
place in said exposition an exhibit of Kentucky re-
sources, and did, at the conclusion of said exposi-
tion, on March 31, 1886, return the same to this State,
and to the owners thereof, and did,during the time of
saidexposition,representthisCommonwealth thereat,
for which services and expenses said Smith has re-
ceived no compensation whatever from the State ;
now , therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the present Governor of this Common-
wealth be hereby directedto investigatethe facts of
said employment , and the value of saidservicesand
expenses, and fix such sum as,in his judgment , will.
be a faircompensation and return therefor.

704 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§2 . That upon the written certificateof the Gov-
ernor, fixing said amount , being presented to the
Auditor , he shall draw his warrant on the Treasurer
for the amount so named in favor of said J. Soule
Smith , and the Treasurer shall pay the said sum to
the person so named out of any moneys in his hands
not otherwise appropriated .
§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 31, 1888.

Approved March 31,1888.

thecashpayment of allclaimsagainstsaidcounty.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims and levy of said
county shall, at any meeting after the passage of this
act, appropriate,in the hands of the treasurerof said

certifiedby the county clerk of said county, at any
time within ten days after the adjournment of said
court .
§ 2. Said court shall, at each term,directthe levy
and collectionof county levy and ad valorem tax,
sufficient to return into the hands of said treasurer the
amount he isdirectedto pay at said term, including

日
T
L
costs of collection and disbursement ; but nothing
herein shall be construed to increase the amount of
county levy or ad valorem tax as now fixed by law .
§ 3. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffor collectorof
said county to pay all county levy and ad valorem
taxes at the same time and in the same manner as now
required by law ; but he shall pay the same to the
treasurerof said county ; and for failureto so pay
the same , said treasurer shall have the same remedy
against him as is provided in favor of county credit-
ors by section7, article2, chapter 27, of the General
Statutes .
§ 4. Said treasurershallpay no claim until he has
knowledge of the fact that the person to whom said
claim was granted owes no part due ,taxes or county
levy; and the amount of any such taxes or county
levy shallbe deducted from said claims and paid by
the treasurerto the party holding said tax receipts:
Provided, Said claim is as large or larger than said
taxes ; the word taxes herein also includes the taxes
due the State. No assignment of any claim shallpre-
judice the rightof the treasurerto retainthe taxes
owing by the party to whom the claim was allowed,
and if the amount of said claim is more than sufficient
to pay offsaidtaxes,and the assigneeowes taxes,they
may in like manner be retained and paid by said
treasurer .
§5 . It shall be the duty of the sheriff of said
county, and of all other persons holding tax re-
ceipts, to furnish the said treasurerwith a listof all
persons owing taxes,to whom county claims have
been allowed, within three days after the adjourn-
ment of said court of claims ; and any such sheriff,
or otherpersons, failingso to do ,may , on indictment,
be fined in the sum of not less than twenty -fivedol-
lars nor more than one hundred dollars .
§ 6. Before actingunder thislaw saidcounty treas-
urer shallexecute, before the county judge of said
county, his bond , with at leastthree good sureties

that said treasurerwill well and truly discharge his

tled to receive the same according to law all sums to

the - day of—— , 1888.

court,and a minute of the execution approved and
filingof said bond shallbe made upon the book of
said court, and said bond shall be duly recorded in
said office .

shall in no way affect the bond of said treasurer ex-
ecuted pursuant to an act of the General Assembly of

act for the benefitof Hardin county ,and to dispose

of the sinking fund of said county ."

CHAPTER 815 .
AN ACT to empower the county court of Harrison county to borrow
the H. C. Moore School Fund .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That the Harrison county court, a majority
of the justicesconcurring, shall have power and
authority to borrow the fund known as the " H. С.
Moore School Fund ;" and they shall execute bond
for said sum to the trustee of said fund and his
successorsin office, due and payable not less than

716 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ten nor more than twenty years from the date of said
loan, bearing interest from date at a rate of not less
than six nor more than eightper centum per annum ,
interestpayable semi-annually on the firstday of
March and September of each year .
§ 2. That it shall be the duty of the Harrison
county court to levy an annual tax sufficientto pay
the interestupon said fund, which tax shall be col-
lectedby the sheriffof the county in the same way
and under the same responsibilitieswhich now apply
by law to the collection of revenue of the State,
which sum shall be paid over to the trustee of said
fund on the firstday of March and September in
each year .
§ 3. That at the expiration of the loan mentioned
herein, the county court shall have power to levy a
tax sufficientto pay the principalof saidloan. Said
tax shallbe collectedby the sheriffin the same way
and under the same responsibilitiesas described in
section 2 of this act .
§ 4. That in the event the county court refuses
to borrow said fund upon the terms prescribed in
section1 of this act,the trusteeof said fund shall
have the power to loan said fund upon the terms
prescribedin section1 of this act for any length of
time not exceeding three years, upon such security
as he shalldeem sufficient,being responsiblefor said
fund on his officialbond .
§5 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April3,1888.

pany , of Meade county .
WHEREAS , The Ekron Gas and Manufacturing
Company was , on the day of -, 1887 , duly
organized under the provisionsof chapter 56 of the
General Statutes. The object of the formation of
said corporationor company being, as stated in the
articlesof incorporation thereof, to develop , produce ,
utilize, convey, or any way dispose of natural gas,
petroleum, saltwater,buildingstone, etc.,etc.;there-
fore ,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Z. T. Herndon , A. J. Thompson , Gus . W.
Richardson and C. L. Cornwall , their successors and
assigns, be, and they are hereby, created a body -cor-
porate,under the name of the " Ekron Gas and Manu-
facturingCompany ," and by that name shall have
perpetual succession, and the power to contractand
be contractedwith,to sue and be sued,implead and
be impleaded ; to take, hold, own , and in anywise
alienproperty, real, personal and mixed ; to have a
common seal,and alterthe same at pleasure; and to
establishby -laws and make rules and regulationsfor
the management of the affairsof said company , not
inconsistent with the Constitution or laws of this
State or of the United States .
§2 . The affairs of said corporation shall be con-
ducted by a board of directorsof not lessthan five
nor more than nine directors, who shallbe electedfor
a term of one year, and untiltheir successors are
chosen . At a meeting of the stockholders of said
corporation,to be held in the town of Brandenburg
or the city of Louisville, as the directors from time
to time may agree, on the of each year ,
said directorsshallelecta presidentand vice-presi-
in

dent, who shall be, and a secretary and treasurer
who may be,members of saidboard. The meetings of

vice-president, or in case of his absence, inabilityor
refusalto act,by a written callof not lessthan one-
half of the directors. The powers, duties and com-
pensation of the president and other officersof the
company shallbe prescribedby the board of directors.
The directors, the incorporatorsnamed herein, shall
constitute the board of directors until the firstannual
electionprovided for herein. In all meetings of the
stockholdersthey shallbe entitledto one vote for
each share of stockheld by them . They may vote in
person or by proxy , written and subscribedby them .
Of all meetings of the stockholders fivedays' pre-
vious notice shall be given by the secretary of said
company .

million dollars , divided into shares of one hundred
dollarseach, which shallbe deemed personal propр-
erty, and subscribed for and paid in at the times
and upon the terms,and transferablein the manner to
be prescribedin the by -laws of said company . The
stockholders of said company may , by a two -thirds
vote of all the stock, at a meeting called for that
purpose, increasesaidstock not exceeding one million
dollars .

produce, utilize, convey, or in any otherwise dispose
of naturalgas, petroleum, saltwater,building stone,

which may be upon or come from any of the lands
which said company may own , lease, acquire or con-

do any of the acts in thissectionmentioned , itmay

and maintain wellsforthe purpose of producing such

ings, machinery , tanks, pipes, reservoirs, receivers,

CHAPTER 822 .
AN ACT to amend an act establishinga new charter for the city of
Louisville, approved March 3d, 1870,relatingto the remuneration of
the clerk and marshal of the citycourt of Louisville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That hereafterthe clerk of the city court of
Louisville shall receive no fees whatsoever in cases
triedin said court or for the taking of a bond ,whether
said bond be taken in said officer's office or out of the
same , by himselfor deputy ;but in lieuof allfeesand
salariesnow by law allowed to said officer, he shall
receivefrom the cityof Louisvillea salaryof three
thousand fivehundred dollarsfor his own services,
and the sum of threethousand dollarsshallbe paid

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 727
by the cityof Louisville,in equal proportions,to such
deputiesas saidofficermay appoint. The marshal of
the citycourt of Louisvilleshallnot receiveany fees
in any actionor proceedinghad in said court; but in
lieuof allfees or salariesnow by law allowed said
officer, he shall receivefrom the city of Louisvillea
salary of three thousand five hundred dollars for his
own services; and the sum of three thousand dollars
forthe payment of such deputiesas said officermay
appoint shallbe paid by the cityof Louisville,in equal
proportion , to such deputies .
§ 2. That in every case tried in said court,upon con-
viction,the defendant shallpay as costs the sum of
two dollars,which sum shallbe collectedby the mar-
shal and be paid, together with all finesand fees
collectedby him , into the treasury of the city of
Louisville .
§ 3. It shall be the duty of the clerk of the Louis-
villecitycourtto pay into the citytreasury allfees
collectedby him fortaking bonds, and the charge for
taking same shall be seventy -fivecents ; and under no
circumstances is he allowed to charge for such service
more than seventy -fivecents.
§ 4. All actsor parts of actsin conflictwith any of
the provisionsof thisact are hereby repealed.
§ 5. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[Became a law without approval of Governor April 4,1888.]

CHAPTER 829 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Arlington , in Carlisle
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of trustees of the town of Ar-
lington, in Carlislecounty, be, and they are hereby,
authorizedand empowered to lay out and establish,
by ordinance, such streetsand alleysin said town as
they may deem necessaryor convenient for the use
and enjoyment of the public; and for this purpose
they may , by their officersand agents, enter upon
and appropriatethe private property of any person
or persons, and to that end they are hereby author-
ized and empowered to contractwith the owners of
any such property for such streetsand alleys, and
to receivea conveyance for same upon such condi
tions as may be agreed upon .
§ 2. If said board of trustees is unable to agree
with the owner of such property as may be laid
out, by ordinance, into streetsand alleys, as to the
compensation therefor, or if, by reason of any disa
bility, the owner is incapable in law to contract,or
be a non-residentof the county, then, in eitherof
these cases, the trusteesmay , by applicationto the
policejudge of said town , procure a writ of ad quod
damnum ,directedto the sheriffor any constableof
Carlislecounty, commanding him , upon a day therein
named , to summon a good and lawful jury of twelve
men to meet upon the land or at a place convenient
thereto, who shall be sworn as in the case of con-
demnation for a public highway , and shall proceed,
afterhaving heard the evidence, to assessthe dam-
ages which the owner has sustained by reason of
his property having been taken for such streetor
alley; but no such condemnation shall be had if
the owner reside in the county until he has been

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 737
served with a copy of said writ for at least ten days
before the condemnation ; nor if the party be labor-
ing under any disabilityother than coverture, until
a guardian ad litem has made defense ; nor if the
party be a non -resident, until defense be made by
an attorney appointed for that purpose .
§3 . The police judge shall attend and preside at
such condemnations, and shallkeep a record of the
proceedings, filesame in his office; either party,
within three days, may fileexceptions to the ver-
dict, which shall be tried by said policecourt, and
he may confirm and render judgment upon the ver-
dict or award a new trial, as he may think isjust.
§ 4. Either party may , within sixty days from the
time the policejudge renders itsjudgment , prosecute
an appeal to the common pleas court of Carlisle
county ; said appeal shall be taken as appeals are
now taken from the judgments of inferiorcourts,
except if the party taking the appeal be under any
disabilityother than coverture, no bond shall be re-
quired ; and , upon appeal, the case shall be tried
de novo .
§5 . In no event shall any such streetor alley be
opened to the public untilthe compensation adjudged
to the owner shall be actually paid or secured in the
manner the court finallytrying the case may pre-
scribe ; nor shall any such streets or alley be laid
out so as to include or be nearer than ten feet of
any dwelling-house.
56. That said charterbe further amended by pro-
viding , that in allcase when a fine has been imposed
by any judicialofficerof said town for a violationof
any ordinance or by -law of thereof, an execution
may issue thereon forfineand cost, and no property
shall be exempt from such execution .
§ 7. Said town shallput and keep itsstreetsin good
repair, and if they failso to do, may be indictedand
fined not more than one hundred dollars ; but the

citizensshallnot be required to work the road out-
side of said town .
§ 8. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage .
Approved April5,1888.

CHAPTER 858 .

Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby establisheda bank in the town
of Tompkinsville,in Monroe county,Kentucky,tobe
styled the " Deposit Bank of Monroe County ," with
a capitalstock of thirtythousand dollars, in shares
of one hundred dollarseach par value, which may ,
by the consent of the owners of a majority of the
shares of stock aforesaid, be increasedto fiftythou-
sand dollars,in shares of one hundred dollarseach,
to be subscribedand paid for as hereinafterspecified;
and the subscribers, their associates, successorsand
assigns, shall be a body -politicand corporate, by
the name and style of the " Deposit Bank of Monroe
County ," and may so continue for thirtyyears from
its organization ; but the board of directors of said
bank are authorized and empowered to wind up its
affairs whenever the owners of two -thirds of the stock
determine that the interestof the company requires
its discontinuance ; and to effect this the board shall
have all powers necessaryto wind up and liquidate
the affairsof saidbank upon equitableprinciples.
§ 2. Said corporationshall have all the privileges
of a natural person in contracting and being con-
tractedwith,in suing and being sued,by the name
aforesaid, in allplaces and courts whatsoever ; and
may have and use a common seal, and change the
same at pleasure .
§3 . The said bank shall be under the control of the
Namesofdirect-following named persons as directors, viz: H. K.
Bedford, W. H. Botts, Turner Nelson, W. L. Porter,
James H. Bevell, and W. A. Scott, who shall hold
their officesuntil the second Tuesday in January ,
1889,and untiltheirsuccessorsare electedand quali-
fied. Each director hereinafter elected shall be a

stockholder and a majority of the directorsshall at
alltimes be residents of Monroe or Barren counties ;
and after the firstelection, which shall be held on
second Tuesday in January , 1889 ,they shall be chosen
on second Tuesday in January of each year, or if not
on that day , from any cause , then as soon thereafter
as convenient,and shallhold theirofficesforone year,
or until their successorsare elected and qualified.
They shall appoint one of their number president,
and may if they desire, and think bestand necessary,
at any time, appoint also from their number a vice-
president, who shallhold his positionat the pleasure
of the board of directors. The board shallhold regu-
larmeetings at such times as they may fixupon , and
such calledmeetings as they may deem necessary. In
case of the death, resignationor removal of any di-
rector or officer, the vacancy shall be filledby the
board of directors. The board shall have power to
receivesubscriptionsfor as many of the sharesof the
capitalstock remaining unsold,or increased as afore-
said and unsold , as said board may at any time de-
cide to sell; shall declare dividends, appoint such
officers, agents, or servantsas they think necessaryto
conduct the business of the bank , and pay them such
wages or salariesfor theirservices, and take from
them such bonds to secure the faithfulperformance
of their duties, as they may think proper ; shall pre-
scribe such rules as they may think best for the trans-
fer of the stock on the books of the bank , by the
stockholders or by an attorney; the stock shall be
personal property. The number of directorsto be
elected in January , 1889, and each year thereafter,
shall be not less than five nor more than nine , the
number to be hereafterdetermined by a vote of the
stockholders .
