CHAPTER 861 .
AN ACT to providefor a registrationof votersof the cityof Cov-
ington.
Officers.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county judge of Kenton county, the
sheriffof said county, and the clerk of the Kenton
county court, shall constitutea board, which shall,
between the firstand fifteenthdays of June in each
year, appoint four sober and discreetcitizens, in each
electionprecinctof the city of Covington, two to act
as judges of registration,one as clerkof registration,
and one as sheriffof registration, in the precincts for
which they are appointed. No person shall be eligi-
ble as judge,clerkor sheriffwho isnot a voterin the
precinctfor which he isappointed and a housekeeper,
and when there are two or more distinctpoliticalpar-
ties, said officersof registrationshall be so selected as
that one of the judges of each precinctshallbe of one
politicalparty, and the other of the opposing politi-
cal party, the clerk to be selectedfrom the third
politicalparty (provided that there should be more
than two distinctpoliticalpartiesat the time of regis-
tration) ; they shallserve for one year from the time
of theirappointment ,and each officerof registration,
before enteringupon the dischargeof hisduties, shall
take the oath prescribedby section1, article8, of the
Constitution, and shall, during the period of his term
of service,be excused from jury duty .
§ 2. The sheriffof the county shall, at leastseven
days before the general registrationin each year, as
provided for in this act,give each officerof registra-
tion written notice of his appointment . Should the
court, sheriffor clerk failto appoint, or should all
the officersappointed failto attend at the place of

792 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
registration for two hours after the time for com .
mencing the registration, or refuse to act, then the
sheriff, judge or clerk, or either of them that can
be found in the county , shall appoint officersto act
in their stead for that registration; but should two
officersbe in attendance, they may fillvacancies for
the purpose of conducting that registration, and may
administer the necessary oaths of office.

§ 3. Officers of registration shall have the same
power to preserve order at the placesof registration
as is exercised by officersof election at the polls.
the officersof registrationentertainany doubt as to

Forms .

ton county to prepare the proper forms , and cause to
be printed two registrationbooks foreach precinctin
the cityof Covington, and one of which shallbe kept
in his office,to be used as provided in section9 of this
act, and the other furnishedto the clerksof registra-
tionand election, before each registrationor election
day , as hereinafter provided . Said books shall be so
arranged as that the names of voters may be regis-
tered alphabetically, and shallbe ruled and headed
as follows: Name , residence, color,August election,
October election, November election,specialelection,
remarks .
General Registration .
$ 5. The officersof registrationshall attend at the
voting placesof theirrespectiveprecinctson the first
Monday in July in each year, and on the next suc.
ceeding day , from the hours of nine in the morning

until nine in the evening , and shall record in the reg-
istrationbook , which shall be furnished by the clerk
of the county court to each registrationclerk, a list
of such qualifiedvoters of the precinctas apply for
registration. If either of said days fall upon the
fourth day of the month , then said fourth day shall
be omitted as a day of registration, and the registra-

ment be not numbered , the locationthereof shallbe

cers,or ifany by-standershalldemand it,he shallbe

by such by -stander,touching his qualificationsas

registrationin July shall be known as the general

Qualifications.
§ 6. Every person shall be entitled to be registered
who would be entitled to vote at the next succeeding
August election; that is to say, every male citizen
who , on that day , would have attained the age of
twenty -one years, and shallhave residedin the State
two years, or in the county of Kenton one year, and

in the precinctsixty days . No person shall be reg-

known to one of the officers, or ifany by -standershall
demand it, he shall be sworn by one of the officers
and interrogatedby him ,or by such by -stander,touch-
ing his qualificationsas provided in this section.

ship, shall be entitled to be registered, but the clerk

and no person so removing , afterbeing registered,
shall be registeredin another precinct without the
production of such a certificate.
Striking off Names .
§ 13. Any voter may , by giving fivedays' notice in
writing to any person whose name has been registered,
move the county court to strike his name from the
register,and both partiesmay introducewitnesses, not
exceeding threein number on each side; said notice
must be served personally. If, at the hearing, the
court shallbe of the opinion that the person regis-
tered isnot lawfullyentitledto register,itshalldirect
the clerk to strikehis name from the registerby in-
serting opposite to it the words , " Stricken off by
order of the county court . — , clerk." If the per-
son upon whom noticeis attempted to be served can
not be found, the clerkshallwriteoppositesuch name
on the registrationbook the word " doubtful;" and
when at an electionsuch person whose name ismarked

798 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
doubtful shall offerto vote, he shall be sworn and his
rightto vote be investigatedfully.
§ 14. On the Saturday previous to the firstMonday
in August ,1888,and on the judicialday previous to
every electionto be held or vote of the people to be
taken thereafterin the cityof Covington, the clerk of
the county court shall deliver to the clerks of election
the registrationbooks for their severalprecincts, to-
getherwith the poll-books,which saidregistrationand
poll-books shallbe produced by said clerks at their
severalprecinctswhen the polls are opened on the
day of election. At said election no vote shall be re-
ceived unless the name of the person offeringto vote
is on the registryprovided in this act,or unless he
produce, before the officersof election, his written
affidavitstatingthat he was necessarilyabsent from
the city,or that he was illand unable to attend, or
that some named member of his family was so ill
as to require his constant attention, such absence
or illnesscovering the period allowed for registra-
tion, and statingfacts showing him to be a qualified
voter of the precinct, and alsothe facts in reference
tohis residencerequiredto be entered on the register,
and also prove by written affidavitof a registered
voter of the precinct, who is a householder, that he
knows such person to be a qualifiedvoter, and stating
the factsin referenceto such person's placeof resi-
dence required to be entered on the register. Said
affidavitsshallbe returnedby the officersof the elec-
tionto the county clerk, indorsed as follows: “Filed
before us this - day of , 18— . ——judges ;"
and shallbe kept on fileby the county clerk. Any
vote receivedby the officersof electionin contraven-
tion of this act shall be void, and shall be rejected
from the count . The officersof election shall, when
a vote is cast, mark opposite to the name of the per-
son voting, in the column of the registrationbook
provided for that election, the word " voted ;" and
at the close of the election, and before closing or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 799

leaving the voting place, shall mark opposite the
name of each person who has not voted at that elec-
tion the words " not voted ." The registration book
shallbe returned to the county clerk by the clerk of
the election the day next succeeding the election.
False Registration .
§ 15. Any person who shallcause himself to be reg-
istered in more than one election precinct other-
wise than is provided in section 12 of this act, or
more than once in the same precinct, or who shall
cause himself to be registeredknowing that he isnot
lawfully entitledto registration, and any person who
shall aid or abet in the commission of any of said
acts, shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and
shallbe imprisoned in the county jailnot lessthan
one nor more than twelve months .
False Registration Books .
§ 16. Any officerof registration or other person ,
who shall unlawfully alterany registrationbook , or
add any names thereto, or shall willfullysecrete,
suppress or destroy any such book , or who shall
make or aid in making any falseor fraudulent regis-
trationbook , shall be deemed guilty of felony, and
shall be confined in the penitentiarynot less than
one nor more than fiveyears, and shallforfeitany
officehe then holds, and shall forever be disquali-
fied from holding office.
Perjury .
§ 17. Any person who shallwillfullymake any false
statement, under an oath lawfullyadministeredunder
thisact, shallbe deemed guiltyof falseswearing, and
confined in the penitentiary not less than one nor more
than fiveyears .
Obstructing Registration.
§ 18. Any person who , by himself or in aid of
others,shallforciblybreak up or attempt to break up

800 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a registrationheld under this act, or shall forcibly
prevent or attempt to prevent any person from ap-
proaching or entering a place of registration, shall
be fined not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundred dollars, or imprisonment not less than six
nor more than twelve months in the county jailor
both .
§19 . No witness shall be excused from testifying
before any grand jury, or in any prosecution or in-
dictment under thisact, on the ground that his testi-
mony may criminatehimself; but no such testimony
shallbe used against him in any prosecution, except
for falseswearing in giving same .
Fees .
§ 20. For his servicesunder this act the county
clerk shall receive the following fees and no other :
For copying the registrylistsrequired to be kept in
his office, or to be used in supplying lost books , the
sum of two cents for each voter whose name is so
copied ; for his services under section 13 of this act
the sum of ten cents for the name of each voter
strickenoffthe lists; forreceiptingto clerksof regis-
trationand electionfor registrationbooks , the sum
of fivecents for each book receipted for ; the sheriff
shall receive for serving the notices provided for in
section 2 the sum of thirty cents for each actual ser-
vice ; officersof registration shall receive two dollars
and fiftycents per day for each day employed in
attending at the places of registration. But clerks
of registrationand electionshall not be entitledto
any pay , except upon the production of the county
clerk'sreceiptfor theirrespectiveregistrationbooks,
which receipt shall be the voucher of the officerpay-
ing them. The cityof Covingtonshallpay allfees
and expenses incurred under this act for registration.
The sheriffshallhave published in one daily paper,
published and circulatingin the city of Covington,
the names of all persons who have been registered at
leastthreedays aftereach registration.

§ 21. A copy of this act shall be printed in each
registrationbook made under itsprovisions.
§ 22. The eighteen precincts, as now laid off and
provided for by law for general electionsin the city
of Covington ,shall continue and remain as now estab-
lishedand laidoff, and shallbe regarded as precincts,
and treatedas such in allcity, as well as generalelec-

a registration shall be made of the voters of each of

duty of the officersof said city to cause a voting place
to be opened in each of said eighteen precincts, and

into effectand be observed at the next county election
to be held on the firstMonday in August , 1888 ; and
allactsor partsof actsin conflictherewith arehereby
repealed .
§ 23. The citycouncilof the cityof Covington shall
submit thisact to the legal votersof the cityof Cov-
ington, at a specialelectionto be ordered by itin the
usual manner , which shallbe held in the month of
April, 1888. At saidelectionthe officersthereofnow
provided by law shallopen a pollat each electionpre-
cinct, and to each legal voter offeringto vote the
question shallbe propounded , " Do you vote for or
against the registrationbill?" and his vote shall
thereupon be recorded for or against the same as
he shall direct . The officers of said election shall
make returns of said vote as officersof city elec-
tions are now required by law to make returns of
city elections; and if a majority of the legal votes
cast at said election are for or in favor of said bill,
the citycouncil shall so declare, and this act shall
take effectfrom that date ; but if a majority of the
legalvoters shallvote against thisbill, the citycoun-
cil shall so declare, and this act shall be of no force
or effect . Approved April 5, 1888.

802 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 862 .
AN ACT to amend an act approved March 7, 1876,entitled"An act to
amend an act approved the seventhof December,1850,entitled'An
actto incorporatethe Kentucky Agriculturaland MechanicalAsso-
ciationat Lexington,and to repealan act amending thesame, " ap-
proved January 30, 1888.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the stockholdersof the Kentucky Agri-
cultural and Mechanical Association at Lexington ,
Kentucky , shall hereafter meet in October of each
year instead of February , to receivereports of the
business of the year preceding, electdirectors, and
transact any other business they are competent to
transact .
§ 2. The presidentand vice-presidentelectedby the
stockholders lastFebruary shallhold office, in pur-
suance of saidelection, untilnext October, when their
successorsshallbe electedas provided by thisact.
§3 . Four of the directors elected at the last Febru-
ary meeting of the stockholdersshall hold office, in
pursuance of said election, until the stockholders'
meeting next October, and no longer; and the re-
maining six directors elected last February shall
hold officeuntil the meeting of the stockholders in
October , 1889 .
§ 4. Within one month afterthe passage of thisact
the board of directorsshalldetermine,by lot, among
themselves, the four directorswho shall go out in
October , 1888 , and the six who shall serve until the
October meeting in 1889 .
§5 . The stockholdersshall, at the October meeting
in 1888, elect six directors to serve for two years , and
they shall, at every regular October meeting there-
after,electin the placeof the sixdirectorswho then
go out, six others to serve untilthe October meeting
two years thereafter.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 803
!
1
2
ام
§ 6. Within one week after the election of directors
in October , 1888 , and within one week after each suc-
ceeding electionof directorsin October of each year
thereafter, the board of directors shall choose from
their own number a president and vice-president, to
serve for one year until their successors are chosen as
aforesaid .
§ 7. Ifa vacancy shall occur in the board of direct-
ors from resignationor other cause, the remaining
members shall, without unnecessary delay, fillthe
same until the next regular meeting of the stockhold-
ers the following October . In the event of a vacancy
from any cause in the officeof president or vice-
president, the remaining members of the board shall
proceed without unnecessarydelay to fillthe vacancy,
first, by electing another member of the board of di-
rectors, and then from the entire board of twelve
members to choose an incumbent for the vacant
office to serve the remainder of his predecessor's
term untilhis successoris duly electedby the stock-
holders .
§ 8. The president, vice-president and other officers
under this act,shallbe subject to the same restric
tions and have the same powers heretofore imposed
on said officers, unless otherwise provided herein.
§ 9. All of the officersmentioned herein shall be
eligible for re-election.
§ 10. That chapter 59 of the Acts of this session,
approved January the 30th, 1888, amending the char-
ter of the Kentucky Agriculturaland Mechanical As-
sociationat Lexington, not having been acceptedby
the stockholdersaccordingto itsprovisions,ishereby
repealed;otheractsincorporatingsaidassociation,and
allamendments thereto, are repealed or modified only
in so far as they conflictwith thisact.
§ 11. This act shall take effect and be in force from
its passage .
Approved April 5,1888 .

forcethe observancethereof by prescribingfinesand
penaltiesfor the violationthereof: Provided , The fine
shall not exceed fiftydollars, and the imprisonment
in the county jailshall not exceed fiftydays . The

of the town of Rowland , and to announce , by ordi-
nance,allvacanciesin office,and to fillthesame by

cial functions of all the officersof said town , and

§3 . The board of trustees shall have a lien on the
property assessed for all taxes under the charter,
which lien shall not be defeated by any cause what-
ever, and which taxes shallbe assessed,due and pay-
able , in such manner and at such time as the board of
trusteesmay by ordinance provide ; and allthe prop-
erty within the town limits, not exempt from taxation
under the general laws of the State,shallbe subject
to the tax imposed by the charterfor citypurposes ;
and the tax collector of the town of Rowland shall
have the same power to collectand enforce and coerce
the payment of the taxes provided for by the charter
that sheriffs now have under the State laws .
§ 4. In all civilcauses within the limits of said town ,
and in allcasesfor the violationsof the generallaws
within saidlimits, the policecourt shallhave concur-
rent jurisdictionwith justicesof the peace of Lincoln
county ; and in case any person shallfailto pay or

the ordinances of said town or the generallaws,the

person in the jailof Lincoln county, or may cause
them to work out such fine on the streets or other
place within the town ; said work shall be done under
the supervisionof the marshal or other person, as
the court may direct; and the imprisonment or labor
thus imposed shallnot exceed one day for each dollar
of the fine .
§5 . J. H. McPherson is hereby appointed police
judge of the town of Rowland untilthe firstelection
shallbe held as provided for by the charter, and until
his successor is elected and qualified.
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the marshal of Rowland
to serve and execute allnotices, warrants, processes,

806 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
writsand other things directedto him by the police
judge of Rowland , when they can be executed within
the town , and shall have the same power to serve
same as constables now have under the State laws ; and
he shall, within the limitsof said town , in allmatters
of a penal or criminal nature arisingunder the ordi-
nances of the cityor under the laws of thisCommon-
wealth,possessthe same powers and perform the same
duties as sheriffs and constables .
§ 7. All general laws relatingto town and cities,
and all general laws relatingto courts and officers,
not inconsistent with this amendment and the char-
ter,shallapply to the town of Rowland , to the police
court of said town , and allthe officersof said town .
§ 8. This act shall be in effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 5, 1888.

CHAPTER 865 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Michigan Lumber Company ,at Louisville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John Bartman , Sr., John Bartman , Jr.,
Charles Bartman , Jacob Bartman , George W. Bart-
man , William H. Bartman ,John Hoertz and John Ull-
rich,are hereby constitutedand created a body -cor-
porate,by the name of Michigan Lumber Company ;
and by that name they and theirsuccessorsand as-
signs to have perpetual existence as a corporation.
The following so constitute the firstboard of direct-
ors : John Bartman , Sr., John Hoertz, George W.
Bartman , William H. Bartman , with power to fill
vacancies as they occur from time to time ; to select
from their number a president, secretary and treas-
urer and general manager . The said corporation
shall have power to sue and be sued, to contract
and be contractedwith, to make and use a common
seal, and alter the same at pleasure, to purchase,
take and hold by purchase,gift or otherwise, and to
sellor mortgage any realor personalestate.
§2 . The objectand purpose of this corporationare
hereby declared to be the carrying on of a general
lumber business, with all the necessary powers in
such respects as private individualsnow enjoy ; to
buy and selllumber and logs,and provide means for
sawing logs.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 811
§ 3. The capitalstock of said corporation shall be
($25,000) twenty -fivethousand dollars, with shares of
($100) one hundred dollarseach ; and the said board
of directors herein named shall fix the time when and
the conditions upon which it shall be paid in, with
power to increasethe same as the board of directors
may direct ; but no such increase of stock shall be
valid unless ratifiedby the majority of stockholders
of thisbody .
§ 4. The board of directors of this corporation may
adopt by -laws or make any other rules and regula-
tions for the management of itsbusiness,and carry
out itsobjects, not inconsistentwith thisact or with
the Constitution or laws of the United States or of
this State .
§ 5. The said board of directors herein named shall
hold officeuntil the firstof June , at which time there
shall be an electionof a board of directors,and until
their successorsare duly elected and qualified, and
thereafterin each succeedingyear, on the firstday of
June , the board of directors shall be elected ; but a
failure to so electshall not invalidate any incorporate
act, or work a forfeitureof this charter.
§ 6. The highestamount of indebtednessor liability
to which the corporationis at any time to subject
itself shall be ten thousand dollars .
§ 7. The privateproperty of stockholders is to be
exempt from corporate debts .
§ 8. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April5,1888.

CHAPTER 866 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the city of Vanceburg , in Lewis-
county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charter of the cityof Vanceburg be,
and the same is so amended , that the cityassessor
shall enter upon his duties of assessingsaid cityon
the tenth day of September , or soon thereafter, in
each year ; that each person shall be assessed with
what property he owned or had in his possession on
saidtenthday of September . The cityassessorshall
complete his work and make his reportto the council
of said city at its regular meeting in November ; the
council shall, at said meeting, appoint three persons
to act as supervisors of said tax and as a board of
equalization ; before entering upon their duties they
shalltake an oath beforethe mayor of said city; they
will make theirreport at the regular meeting of the
council in December . The city clerk shall make out
the tax receiptsand place same in hands of city
collector, taking receipttherefor,by the fifteenthday
of January , or when the incoming collectorhas qual-
ifiedand been sworn in office. After the firstday of
May in each year the city collectorshall advertise
and sell all real estate listed for taxation on which
tax has not been paid ; he shall advertise same at
three or more public places in said city, at least
fifteendays before sale, which sale shall be at front
door of court-house in said city on some county
courtday . Any person buying said real estateshall
be given a certificateof purchase by the collector
making said sale; if no one bids on said property
the collectorshallbid same in for said city, giving
said city a certificateof purchase for each piece so
bought in ; the purchasers shall filetheir certificates
with the cityclerk,to be recorded by him in a book

kept for that purpose. Any time within two years
from day of salethe owner of said property, or some
one forhim ,may redeem same by paying to cityclerk
the tax,allcost,and thirtyper cent.interest. If same
isnot redeemed within two years,the collectormaking
said sale, or the collectorfor that year, shall make a
deed to said purchaser for said property. No real
estate, however ,shall be sold for taxes where there
is any personal property out of which to make said
tax .
§ 2. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 5,1888 .
CHAPTER 867 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Menelos and SilverCreek Station Turnpike
Road Company .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Menelos and Silver Creek Turnpike
Road Company be incorporated for the purpose of
constructingand operating a turnpike road from a
point at or near Menelos,in Madison county, Ken-
tucky , to a point at or near Silver Creek Station on
the Kentucky Central Railroad in said county ; or to
a point at or near Moran's scales on Paint Lick and
Walnut Meadow Turnpike Road in said county, or
to both of the two last-named points. The exact
locationof said road or roads shall be fixed by the
directors of said company in accordance with the
above description.

§3 . John Powers , John W. Maupin , W. B. Jones,
Robert Duerson , Thompson Burnam , B. F. Brown ,
James Adams and John Ballard ,are hereby appointed
commissioners, and they, or any three of them , may
open books and receivesubscriptionsto the capital
stock of said company at such times and places as
they may choose. The capitalstock of saidcompany
shall not exceed fifteenthousand dollars, and shall
be divided into shares of fiftydollars, and the pri-
vate property of the stockholders shall be exempt
from its corporate liabilities.
§ 4. When three thousand dollars of the capital
stock shall have been subscribed, a majority of the
commissioners named , who may act,shall, after ten
days' notice in writing of time and place, hold an
election of a board of five directors . The directors
shallhold officefor one year,and untiltheirsuccess-
ors are elected and qualified. Said directors shall an-
nually name one of theirnumber president, and also
appoint a secretaryand treasurerfrom among the
stockholders. A majority of the directorsshallbe a
quorum for the transactionof any business of the
company , and saidboard of directorsshallcontrolthe
affairsof the company .
§ 5. Similar elections for the election of directors
shallbe held annually, and ten days' previous notice
of same shallbe given by the board of directors; the
voting shall be in person or by written proxy , and
each share of stock shall be entitled to one vote .
§6 . The grade of said road-bed shall not be less
than sixteenfeetwide, and the metal upon same shall
not be lessthan twelve feet wide ; and said company
shall have all the rights and be subject to all the
liabilitiesnow granted and imposed by the General
Statutes of Kentucky .
§ 7. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 5, 1888 .

CHAPTER 869 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled "An act to consolidate common
school districtsNos .1 and 26,in Carroll county ,and to provide for
a school building and a better organization of the public schools in
saidconsolidateddistrict," approved May 7,1886; and providingfor
the levyingof an additionaltax in saidschooldistricttoextendthe
session of the school therein .
WHEREAS , The trustees of common school district
No. one (1), white, in Carrollcounty, are in debt for
a school building, and have levied a tax of twenty-
five(25) cents on each one hundred dollars' worth of
taxableproperty in said districtto pay said indebted-
ness,and the amount of such indebtednessbeing such
that said tax must be continued for at least three ·
years ; and whereas , the amount received from the
State common school fund by said districtwillonly
maintain the school for five(5) months ; and itbeing
greatly desirable to maintain the common school in
said districtfor a term of from eight to ten months .
in each schoolyear ; therefore,to provide forthe levy-
ing of an additionaltax to accomplish such object,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of trustees of common school
districtnumber one (1),white,in Carrollcounty, be,
and they are hereby , authorized to levy , from year to
year, a tax on alltaxable property within said dis-
trict, which is owned by white people,not exceeding
twenty (20) centson each one hundred dollars' worth
thereof, in any one school year , which tax shall be
collected as county and State taxes are collected; and
any officercollectingthe same ,or a collectorappointed
by the board of trustees, shall execute bond for the
faithfulperformance of his duties as such, and the
payment of said tax as collectedto the treasurerof
said board of trustees .
§ 2. The officeror collector making such collections-
shall have the same power to distrain for taxes, and

816 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
enforce the same in the same manner and to a similar
extent , as sheriffsin the collection of State taxes .
§ 3. The treasurer of said board shall be responsible
on his officialbond for allmoney paid to him under
the provisionsof this act, and shallpay out the same
on the order of the board of trustees .
§4 . Said board of trusteesshalluse said money so
collected to extend the sessions of the white common
school in the aforesaiddistrict, as the money so col-
lectedwilljustify, not exceeding ten months in any
one schoolyear ; and they shallreport the amount of
money collectedand how expended in their annual
report .
§ 5. Before said board shall levy the tax as pro-
vided herein, the question shall firstbe submitted to
a vote of the white voters of said district, qualified
to vote under the common school law, and the ques-
tion may be submitted at any time and as often as
said board may deem expedient,noticeof such elec-
tion being given as required by the common school
law ; and said election shall be conducted and the
result ascertained as in school district elections for
district taxation under the common shool law .
§6 . At the electionherein provided for, each voter
shallbe asked by the judge of the election: "Are you
in favorof the schooltax or againstthe school tax ?'”
and his vote shallbe recorded in favor of or against
the tax, according to hisanswer. If the resultis in
favor of " school tax ," the board of trustees shall
levy, from year to year, such tax as herein provided,
in any amount they may deem necessaryfor the year,
not exceeding the twenty (20) centsaforesaid.
§ 7. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after its passage .
Approved April5,1888.

CHAPTER 871 .
AN ACT to regulatethe operationand management of bridgesspan-
ning the Ohio riverat Covington,and to preventextortionand dis-
crimination upon the part of companies , corporationsor individuals
owning, maintainingor operatingthe same.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That no company , corporation or individual,
now or hereafter owning , maintaining , operating or
conducting a bridge or bridges,from any point within
52- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 2.

818 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the city of Covington over or across the Ohio river to
the oppositeshore thereof,shallexact, charge or re-
ceive, for passing over such bridge or bridges, in a
street car or other vehicle, a greater rate of toll or
charge or amount than is exacted, charged or re-
ceived for or from foot passengers crossing such
bridges. Nor shallsuch companies, corporationsor
individuals, charge , exact or receive from any street
railway company or corporation any toll in addition
to that paid for the passengers carriedin itscars.
Nor shall such companies otherwise discriminate
against any class of trafficover such bridges.
And street-car and transportationcompanies oper-
ating theirlinesand vehiclesforcarryingpassengers
over such bridges, and persons riding in such cars or
vehicles, shallbe accorded equal facilitiesfor the pur-
chase and use of commutation ticketsfor passengers
carriedby or ridingin such streetcars or other vehi-
clesas is accorded by such bridge companies to foot
passengers crossing the same .
§ 2. All such bridge companies shall at allhours,
both day and night, expose for sale such coupons or
commutation ticketsas shall be offeredfor sale by
them at any time .
§ 3. Each violationof the provisionsof thisact, or
of eitherof them ,shallbe punished by a fineof one
hundred dollars, recoverable in any court of compe-
tent jurisdiction.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom the day of its
passage .
Approved April5,1888.

CHAPTER 872 .

Company , Northern Division,in Mason county.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the charter of the Middle Trace Turnpike
Road Company ,Northern Division,in Mason county ,

ize said turnpike road company to construct a branch
as part of its road from the main line, near A. R.
Glascock's,to the Maysville and Lexington Turnpike ,
along what is known as the old Mefford dirt road.

and plank roads .

from and after itspassage .
Approved April 5,1888.

CHAPTER 876 .

CHAPTER 879 .
AN ACT to providefor keeping certainturnpikeroadsin repairin
Shelby county .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That upon the applicationto the county judge
of Shelby county by the presidentor any three di-
rectorsof any turnpike road in said county, to which
road said county may have given, or may hereafter
give, aid by subscriptionin the buildingthereof, and
settingforth in such application, under oath, that no
tollsare then being collected for travel on such road ,
or that the net receipts of same are insufficientto keep
itin good condition for public travel, and a majority
of the stockholdersin said turnpike road voting hav-
ing agreed, by vote taken under the supervisionof
the president and directorsof same , should agree to
forfeit their charter and all rights to said road ,
eitherindividuallyor collectively, said county judge

cause to be laid off into suitable and convenient dis-
trictsthe territory contiguous to such turnpike road ,
and he shall appoint a supervisor as in other county
roads; be ex officiothe surveyor of such turnpike
road ; and shallhave, in each and all the said dis-
trictsof his road, all the powers, and shall be sub-
ject to all the duties, penalties, and liabilitiesas

be subject to. Such persons as resideor own land
in said districts, and who may be by law subject to
work on roads, shall be liableto work on the said
turnpike roads upon two days' previous written no-
ticefrom the surveyor. Any person owning land in
saiddistricts,who are by law exempt from work on

theless furnish a hand in his or her stead to work on
said turnpikes. Also any person owning land or a

836 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
non -residentowning land in a district, for each fifty
acres, and each fraction over twenty -fiveacres, owned
by the said party in said district, shallfurnisheach
day of work ordered by the surveyor a wagon and
team of two horses, or a plow and team of two horses,
or two dollars($2) in money , to be used and applied
to the work on and improvement of such turnpikes.
Said wagons , plows, teams and money to be furnished
without compensation or charge to said surveyor, and
subject to his control and application ; and allherein
required to work or furnish wagons ,plows,teams or
money on said roads, and who shall failso to do when
required and notifiedby written notice by the sur-
veyor or forhim ,shall be subject to the same penal-
tiesfor failureas is provided by law for failureto
work on county roads . And all finesand forfeitures
of a districtshall be applied to the improvement of

ceivedone dollarper day for days of actual service.
And no work done , or wagons , plows, or teams fur-
nished , on any such turnpike roads , shall be a charge
against Shelby county or any one .
§ 2. If any such turnpike road upon which no tolls

CHAPTER 884 .
AN ACT tocreatea board of commissionersin Martincounty,topre-
scribetheirpowers and duties,and to authorizethem to issuebonds of
saidcounty .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That there shallbe, and is hereby , established
in Martin county a tribunal, to be styled " The Board
of Commissioners for Martin County ," which shall
consist of three members , to be elected on the first
Monday in August , 1889, by the qualifiedvoters of
Martin county. The comparison of the votes and
certificatesof electionshallbe by the same board of
officersappointed by law for that of county judge.
After the firstelection the persons firstelected shall,
on the firstMonday in October succeeding their elec-
tion, meet at the court -house in the town of Eden ,
when they shallqualifyand immediately enter upon
the duties of their officeas herein imposed upon
them .
§ 2. The term of officeof said commissioners shall
be three years, or until their successors are duly
electedand qualified.
§ 3. Said board of commissioners shall hold their
firstmeeting on the firstMonday in October, 1888.
The said commissioners , before acting as such , shall
be sworn (in addition to the constitutionaloath) to
faithfullyperform the duties of said officeas herein
set forth ; and each shall execute before the Martin
county court a bond to the county of Martin in a
sum not less than two thousand dollars each , with
solvent surety, for the faithfulperformance of the
duties as commissioners under this act . After the
expirationof the term of officeof the present treas-
urer of Martin county, the said commissioners, at
their next regular meeting, shall appoint a treasurer
for Martin county , who shall hold his officefor twelve

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 841
months from the firstMonday in October , in 1889, and
untilhis successor is duly electedand qualified, and
annually thereafter, on the firstMonday in October,
saidcommissioners shallelecta county treasurer.
§ 4. The treasurershallhave the custody and hold
allmoney belonging to said county subject to dis-
bursement as herein set forth, and shall execute
bond to the county of Martin in a sum not less
than five thousand dollars, with two or more sure-
ties, in the aggregate worth in real estate double
the amount of said bond over and above all indebt-
edness and exemptions from execution, conditioned
for the safe-keeping and disbursing of all money
coming to his hands , and for the faithful per-
formance of his duties as treasurer aforesaid ; and
further, he shall be sworn to a faithful performance
of his duties as such treasurer ; said bond to be ap-
proved by the county judge of said county, and attest-
ed by the county clerk, and by him recorded with the
proceedingsof said board of commissioners and safely
kept in the county clerk'soffice. County judge shall
require an additional bond and sureties when he may
deem it necessary , and shall do so when said treasurer
has or is about to have a greater amount of money
belonging to said county in his hands than is per.
fectlysecured by former bonds . The originalbond
as herein contemplated shall be filedin the county
clerk'soffice,and suitmay be brought on the same ,
from time to time , by and in the name of the county
of Martin, or any person injured by any breach of
said bond in any of the courts of Martin county hav-
ing jurisdictionof the sum claimed in said suit.
The treasurer shall receive a reasonable allowance
for his services as treasurer, to be fixed and allowed
by the board of commissioners, payable out of the
county levy .
§5 . The county judge shallbe ex officioa member
of the board of commissioners . He shall attend all
the meetings of saidboard, and by virtueof his office

842 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallpreside as chairman of said board, and as such
shall sign all of the orders and recordsof theirpro-
ceedings; he shallnot vote on any question pending
before said board, except in case of a tie vote ; he
shallthen give the castingvote; and in the absence
of the county judge, or upon his failurefrom any
cause to be present and so preside at any meeting of
said board of commissioners, they shall then select
one of their number to preside as chairman pro
tem., who shall perform the same servicesand ex-
ercisethe same power and authority to the same ex-
tent as the county judge would if he were present.
§ 6. The county clerk shallbe clerk of the board
of county commissioners , and under the orders there-
of shallprocure allrecord-books,in which he shall
keep a full and detail record of all proceedings of
any and all meetings of any or all said commission-
ers; and he shallkeep said books fullyand carefully
indexed and preserved among the records of Martin
county, at the clerk'sofficein said county . These
books shall be paid for on order of said board out of
the county levy of saidcounty .
§ 7. All meetings of said board shall be held at the
court-house in Eden , the regular meetings of which
shall be on the firstMondays in October , January ,
March , and June in each year. The county judge
shallpresideover the meetings of said commissioners;
and he may call an extra session of said board on his
own motion, or upon the applicationof two of said
commissioners . At all of such meetings at least two
of saidcommissioners,togetherwith the county judge,
shall be present to form a quorum to do business.
The said session of said commissioners shall not con-
tinue longer than fivedays at each quarterlysession.
§ 8. Contested election of said commissioners shall
be triedand decided in the same manner and by the
same board as is authorized by law for those of county
judge .
• § 9. The per diem of the board of commissioners

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 843
1
shall not exceed three dollars each per day , neces-
sarilyspent in the transactionof said business, to be
fixedby saidboard and paid out of the county fund .
§ 10. The sheriffshall, by himselfor deputy , attend
on said board, and execute all of itssummons and
orders required to be executed, and shall be allowed
fees thereforas allowed by law for such servicesin
the county court, which shall be paid out of the
county fund .
§ 11. The said board of commissioners shall have
exclusive originaljurisdictionin the same manner
and to the full extent and authority to exerciseall
of the powers and transactany and allbusiness here-
toforetransactedby the court of claims, or that may
hereafter be given by general enactment to the court
of claims in this Commonwealth , and shall be gov-
erned by and subjectto the same laws and penalties,
when not otherwise provided for in this act ; and all
actsor partsof acts creatinga court of claims appli-
cableto the county of Martin are hereby repealedand
held for naught , so faras they apply to the county of
Martin , to take effect as soon as said commissioners
are elected shall qualify.
§ 12. It shall be specialduty of the said board of
commissioners to take charge of the county buildings
and property of every description; also all of the
county roads and bridges in said county, and pre-
serve the same from waste ; and when it is necessary
to build or repair, or in any way improve any of said
buildingsor property belonging to said county, or to
build or repair any bridges and the approaches hereto ,
on any county road, which shall require a sum of
money to do the same , said commissioners shall im-
mediately cause the erectionof all such buildings,
bridges, approaches, or repairs thereon as may be
necessary , at the expense of the county .
13. For the purpose of carrying out the objects
set forth in this act, and for the purpose of paying
the present outstanding indebtedness of said county

844 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
that is now due , or is hereafter to become due , and
for the purpose of erecting, remodeling or repairing
any public buildingsor bridges, and other necessary
improvements for said county , the said commissioners
are hereby authorized and empowered to use the
bonds of said county in amount not exceeding ten
thousand dollars, and to borrow such sums of money
to accomplish such purpose. Said bonds so issued
shallhave coupons attached,and shallbear interestat
a rate not exceeding six per cent. per annum , such in-
terestto be paid annually or semi-annually, as agreed
upon and set forth in said bonds , the said commis-
sionersdrawing theirorder upon the treasurerof said
county for the same ; and the treasurer shall pay the
same out of any funds in his hands subjectto said
order; said bonds shallbe negotiableand payable to
bearer , and shall not be issued and sold at less than
par, and they shall not bear interestuntil they are
issuedand the county has value received.
§ 14. The bonds so issued shall be signed by the
county judge and commissioners, or any two of said
commissioners, and countersignedby the county clerk
under the sealof said county court, in sums not less
than fiftydollarsnor more than one thousand dollars
each, and to become due and payable at any time
within five,ten or fifteenyears from their date, the
coupons being signed by the county clerkalone ; and
each of the said bonds shall distinctlyspecify the
objectforwhich they were issued. All bonds issued
under this act shall be correctlynumbered in the
order which they are issued, and registeredby the
county clerk by him in a book provided and kept for
that purpose in the county clerk'sofficeat Eden ; and
allordersdrawn upon the treasurerfor the payment
of the principal or interestof said bonds shall specify
the fund on which they are drawn ,and shall also be re-
corded in said book ; and when the clerk shall deliver
to the holder of any such bond an order on the treas-
urer for the finalredemption of same ,he shallreceive

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 845
such bond , and forthwithwriteacrossthe faceof the
same , in red ink, the word " redeemed ," and date the
same and sign his name thereto. The treasurer, upon
receivingsuch order, which shallcontain the number
of the bond for the redemption of which the order was
drawn , shall, within threedays, proceed to the clerk's
office, and there, in the presence of said clerk, write
in red ink , across the face of the registryof such bond ,
" redeemed ," the proper date,sign his name thereto,
when the clerk shalldeliverto him the originalbond
duly canceled, for which , togetherwith allorders he
has paid forinterest,he shallbe creditedin his annual
or semi -annual settlement with said commissioners .
All orders drawn upon the treasurerby the commis-
sioners shall be registered by said clerk before he shall
countersign the same ; and it is especiallyprovided
that the outstanding indebtedness of Martin county
shall not, in any event, exceed the sum of twenty
thousand dollarsat any one time.
§ 15. The board shall annually, at the time they
make the annual appropriationsfor the current ex-
penses of the county,set apart and appropriatesuch
additional amount as will pay the intereston allbonds
issued, and at leastfivehundred dollarsof the prin-
cipal, which shallbe sacredlyheld and appliedto the
payment of the presentbonded indebtednessof Martin
county, and to the interest, and to no other purpose.
§ 16. And to provide for the payment of any bonds
that may hereafter be issued in pursuance of this act,
and intereston the same , the board of commissioners
of Martin county are hereby authorized and empow-
ered to levy an ad valorem tax on allrealand personal
property in Martin county which isassessedand sub-
ject to taxation for revenue purposes . The tax herein
authorized shall be levied at their October meeting,
from year to year , in any sum they deem necessary
forthe purpose named in this act, not to exceed fifty
cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of taxable
propertyin Martin county in any one year. The board

846 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of commissionersshall,annually,atthe time they shall
levy other taxes,impose or levy a capitationtax, in
any sum not exceeding three dollars, upon each male
citizenof Martin county over twenty -one years of age,
to be used for county purposes .
§ 17. That the sheriffof Martin county is hereby

CHAPTER 889 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the town of Franklin,Simpson county .
WHEREAS , In sinkingthe publicwellson the public
square of said town of Franklin, streams of excellent
mineral water was obtained, the use of which has be-
come so generalas torequirethe expenditure of money
on, and for the improvement of, said well; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of council of the town of Frank-
lin shallhave the right to appropriateof the funds
of said town a reasonable amount thereof to be ex-
pended upon , and for the public wellson the public
square of said town .
§ 2. That saidmoney shallbe appropriatedand paid
out only as other funds of said town are.
§ 3. That the board of council shall have power to
pass such ordinancesas they may deem necessaryfor
the government of said wells: Provided , however,
They do not affectin any way the rightor rightsthat
any citizenof Simpson county may have in said wells,
by reason of theirbeing upon the publicgrounds .
§ 4. That no ordinanceshallbe passed by saidboard
of council imposing a tax upon the mineral waters
from saidwellsthat may be shipped out of the county,
except it be passed subjectto the approval of the
county court .
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April6,1888.

WHEREAS , Alexander G. Gooch , a citizenand resi-
dent of Auburn , Logan county , Kentucky , was , on

officerof the Commonwealth ,duly authorizedfor that
purpose , to guard and protect a prisoner under arrest
for attempted rape , and in the hands of said officer,
and threatened with violence by a mob , and was ,
while in the dischargeof that duty and serviceto the
Commonwealth ,shot and wounded so as to paralyze
him and render him incapable of locomotion, or of
laboring to any extent for the rest of his life; and
whereas,said Gooch has spent the whole of his for-
tune in trying to restorehisformer health, and isnow
poor and needy , with a helplessfamily, consistingof
a wife and three young children; now ,therefore, as a
compensation for the public services rendered to the
Commonwealth by saidGooch ,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be the duty of the Auditor of
Public Accounts to draw a warrant on the Treasury in
favor of said Alexander G. Gooch for eight hundred
dollars, which sum shallbe paid saidGooch .
§ 2. For the purpose named in section 1 of this act,
the sum of eight hundred dollarsishereby appropri-
ated, out of any moneys in the Treasury,or that may
hereafterbe in the Treasury, not otherwise appropri-
ated .
§3 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
[Became a law withoutapprovalofGovernor April 7, 1888.]

CHAPTER 900 .
AN ACT to authorizeand empower the Marshall county court of claims-
toissuecountybonds and levyan ad valoremtaxforcountypurposes,
in Marshall county .
WHEREAS , It appears that the county court of
claims of Marshall county, which was composed of
the county judge and a majority of allthe justices
for said county concurring therein, at a special
term of said court, held on the thirty-firstday of
March , 1888, authorized by an act of the Legislature,
approved March 13, 1888, made an order on their
order-book , appropriating fourteen thousand dollars
($14,000) for the erectionand building a new court-
house and clerk's officescombined , in said county ;
and whereas, it appears that said court failed to
make any arrangements for the payment of said
sum ; therefore ,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Marshall county court of claims , com-
posed of the county judge and a majority of allthe
justicesof said county, be, and they are hereby, au-
thorizedand empowered to issue the bonds of said
county, with interestcoupons attached, not exceed-
ing fourteen thousand dollars ($14,000) in amount ,
and of the denomination of not less than one hun-
dred dollarsnor more than fivehundred dollars, pay-
able ten years from their date , to bear interest at a
rate not exceeding six per centum per annum , pay-
able annually on the firstday of January of each
year,beginning on the firstday of January , 1890, at
the officeof the county court clerk of the said county ,
on the presentation of the coupons .
§ 2. That the said county court shall prescribethe
form of said bonds . The said bonds and coupons
attached shall be signed by the county judge and
attestedby the county courtclerk, and stamped with
the seal of the court .

able at the pleasureof said court, afterone year from
date .
§ 4. To enablethe court to comply with and carry
out the provisionsof thisact, it is hereby authorized
to levy an ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty-
five cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of taxa-

collected in the same manner , and under the same

are collected .

to year until said bonds and interestthereon shall

shallbe held sacred,as hereinafterprovided, for the

and for no other purpose .
§ 7. Said court shallhave the power eitherto issue
the bonds , as set out in thisact, to pay for the build-
ing of said court-house and clerk'soffices, or to pay
for same out of the said ad valorem tax , when col-
lected, by annual installments.
§ 8. If said court shalladopt the bonded system , as

appoint a commissioner, who , under the directionof

bonds : Provided , however , They shall not be sold at

par or face value .

county treasurer, whose duty it shall be to receive
and collectfrom the sheriff, or collectorof said coun-
ty, said ad valorem tax , and pay it out on the order
of said court, and under no other circumstances .

monwealth of Kentucky :

at any point on the said route that they may see

constructed, and charge all travelers that may pass
over said road the full amount of toll that is now

debts,except fortheconstructionof the said firstthree

§ 8. Said company's officeor main place of business
shall be within the corporate limits of the town of
Murray ,and shallonly be removed from thence to any

of the originalincorporatorsherein named or their

CHAPTER 930 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the owners or lesseesof the ferry at the
town of Russell,in Greenup county,and providingpenaltiesfora vio-
lation of the same .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or
corporation, within one -half mile each way from the
established ferry at Russell over the Ohio river, to
keep or maintain a ferryacross said river, or keep a
boat , skiff or other water craft, with a hand or hands ,
to be used for the purpose of ferrying, or in which
they may , for compensation or otherwise , transport or
carry any horse, wagon , carriage, person or other
thing whatever , over or across said river within the
limitsaforesaid, nor shall any person or corporation
furnish, hire or loan to another any boat , skiff or
other craftto be used for ferrying any person or
thing over said river within the limits aforesaid,
with or without compensation : Provided , That the
provisionsof this act shall not apply to any resi-
dent owner of a skiffor other water craft, when
used by him or them in ferryingany member of his
family or employe over said river, within the limits
aforesaid, or for ferryingsupplies to or for them or
eitherof them ,or forusing the same in any way for
the use of himself or the use of his own family .
§2 . Any person or persons violatingthe provisions
of thisact shall,for each offense, be finedany sum not
lessthan ten nor more than twenty dollars,recoverable
before any justiceof the peace for Greenup county,
having jurisdictionthereof, upon a warrant in the
name of the owner or owners,lesseeor lesseesof said
establishedferry,which may be triedat any time.
§ 3. This act shall take effectand be in force from
itspassage .
[Became a law withoutapprovalof Governor April10,1888.]*

CHAPTER 940 .
AN ACT for the benefitof John W. Coffey,sheriffof Russellcounty .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That John W. Coffey,sheriffof Russellcounty,
shall have tillthe third Monday in April, 1888, to
make his revenue bond to the Commonwealth , the
bond to have the same effect as if executed within
the time now required by law.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 11, 1888 .

to employ ladiesas deputies.

son .

CHAPTER 946 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Northern Bank of Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Northern Bank of Kentucky , at Lex-
ington,in itscorporatecapacity,ishereby authorized
and empowered to construct and erect houses and
buildings for the use of said bank , or to rent, upon
any vacant property in the city of Lexington now
owned by said bank ,and adjoining itspresentbank-
ing house ; but such house or houses shallbe subject
to taxation, State, county, municipal and otherwise,
as other real estate .
§ 2. That at allmeetings of the stockholders, and at
all electionsby them , each and every stockholder
shall be entitled to cast one vote for each share of
stock held in their own right, either in person or by
proxy , as now provided in said charter; but any
stockholder who is not a citizen of the United States
shall not be entitled to vote on his stock .
§ 3. Any part of the charter of said bank in con-
flictwith,or coming withinthe purview of,thisamend-
ment , are hereby repealed.
§4 . That an act, entitled "An act to amend the
charter of the Northern Bank of Kentucky ," ap-
proved February 16, 1888, be, and the same ishereby ,
repealed .
§5 . This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April10,1888.

CHAPTER 947 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporatethe Bank of Allensville,
approved February 24, 1868 .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That so much of sectionsecond (2d) of saidact
as requires the directorsof said bank to hold at least
fifteen shares of stock, and to have held the same
at least six months before their election, is hereby
strickenout,and insertin lieu thereofthat they are
only required to be the owner of one share of stock at
the time of their election .
§2 . Amend the third (3d) section of said act by
strikingout the names of the incorporators,and in-
sert in lieu thereof the following names : C. E. Had-
dox , John Adams , Jas . B. Small , A. C. Hogan , B. D.
Johnson , Frank Walton and N. B. Riley, and strike
out of the seventh line of said section the following
words : " One -half or more ," and insertin lieu thereof
" ten thousand dollars," and add to said sectionthe
following : " That each stockholder shall be entitled
to one vote in all elections under said charter for each
share of stock owned by him , to be cast in personor
by proxy , by authority given in writing.
§ 3. This to take effectfrom and after itspassage.
Approved April 10,1888.
CHAPTER 948 .
AN ACT for the benefitof W. B. Foster, of Allen county.
WHEREAS , On the twenty-firstday of February,
1885, W. B. Foster, Charles Foster, and S. L. Foster,
of Allen county, distillers, having the rightto sell
spiritsof theirown manufacture, did pay to the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ,for tax on license, thesum

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1061
of one hundred dollarsthrough mistake and under
a misapprehensionof the law on the subject; and
whereas, they now claim and ask that said sum be
refunded to them ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor be,and he ishereby, directed
to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for the sum
of one hundred dollars, in favor of W. B. Foster, of
Allen county, in fullsatisfactionof said claim,to be
paid out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise
appropriated .
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April10, 1888.
CHAPTER 949 .
AN ACT for the benefitof W. A. Butt,of Simpson county .
WHEREAS , W. A. Butt, a resident of Simpson
county, owned lands in Allen county,and who had
a father living in Allen county also owning lands
therein ; and whereas , the sheriff of Allen county
made out tax receiptsagainst the said W. A. Butt
for revenue forvarious years to the amount of eight
dollarsand fiftycents; and whereas, in fact the re-
ceiptswere against the estateof said W. A. Butt's
father; and whereas, by mistake the said receipts
were made out againstsaidand paid by the said W.
A. Butt ; and whereas , the mistake was not discov-
ered in time to correct it before the county court ;
therefore,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be, and
he is hereby, directedto draw his warrant upon the
Treasurer for the sum of eight dollars and fiftycents
in favor of W. A. Butt ; and the Treasurer is directed

1062 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to pay the same out of any money in the Treasurynot
otherwise appropriated .
§2 . That this act shall take effect and be in force
from itspassage .
Approved April10,1888.

upon the applicationof the board of directorsof the
Crittenden Sulphur Springs Company , shall appoint
one or more persons to act as policemen at the Crit-
tenden Sulphur Springs , in Crittenden county .
§ 2. That said policemen shallhave power and au-
thorityto preserveorderand arrestany one fordrunk.
enness, disorderlyconduct, and allotherviolationsof
the laws of this Commonwealth while at said springs;
and shallbe clothed with allthe authorityof a peace
officerof this Commonwealth in regard to offenses
committed at said springs.
§ 3. All persons arrestedby the policemen at said
springs shall be turned over to the civilauthorities
of the county to be triedby them , in the same man-
ner as if they had been arrestedby any other peace
officer of this Commonwealth .
§ 4. Before said policemen shall act as such, they
are required to take the oath for the faithfulper-
formance of theirdutiesrequired by law of the con-
stables of this Commonwealth .
§ 5. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 10,1888.

CHAPTER 952 .
AN ACT to amend an act to reduce into one the severalacts in regard
to the town of Lancaster, approved March 9, 1867 .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That section 19 of said act be amended as fol-
lows : That all persons or persons failing to pay their
taxes due the town of Lancaster, Kentucky , by the
firstday of October, afterthe tax books have been
placed in the hands of the collector, shallpay six per
cent. additionalon the tax so due and unpaid , and
the collector shall account for same in his settle-
ment with the board of trustees of the town of Lan-
caster, and shall be responsibleupon his bond for
same . And that section twenty of said act be amend-
ed by strikingout the words two hundred dollarsand
inserting in their stead the words four hundred dol-
lars .
§ 2. That this act take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10,1888.

CHAPTER 956 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Upton .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the policejudge of the town of Upton , if
satisfiedthat a public offenseor a violationof an or-
dinance of said town has been committed , shall have
power to summon before him any persons he may
think proper for examination on oath concerning it,
to enable him to ascertain the offender, and to issue a
warrant for his arrest .
§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1888.

CHAPTER 958 .
AN ACT forthebenefitof Marion county.
WHEREAS , The Marion county court of levy and
claims made a levy of fiftyand one-half cents on the
one hundred dollarsof taxable propertyof saidcoun-
ty for the year 1886, and forty-fivecentson the one
hundred dollars of said taxable property for the year
1887, both for sundry purposes ; and whereas, some
doubt existsas to the legalityof said levy ; for rem-
edy ,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That said levy of fiftyand one-half cents on
the one hundred dollarsfor the year 1886, and that of
forty-five cents on the one hundred dollars for the
year 1887, on the taxableproperty of saidcounty for
saidyears, be, and the same ishereby, rendered valid
and in fullforce and effect, as fully as if said county
court of levy and claims had been empowered to levy
said amount for the purposes mentioned in said lev-
ies .
§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 10, 1888 .

CHAPTER 961 .
AN ACT to amend an act to provide for a stock law for Jefferson
and Oldham counties .
WHEREAS ,It appears that a poll was opened in
the Cane Run and Schardine'sprecinct, of Jefferson
county, on the firstSaturday in June in 1882, under
and in accordance with an act, entitled "An act to
provide for a stock law for Jefferson' and Oldham
counties," passed by the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky, and approved April 15, 1882, in order to take
the sense of the people by a majority vote of the
qualifiedvotersof the said precinct,in answer to the
question: "Are you foror againsta stock law ?" and
whereas, a vote of said precinctswas taken ; and
whereas, allthe requirements of said act and of the
law were complied with ; and whereas, the vote and
majorityof votescastwas and were in favorof enact-
ing said stock law in said precincts; but whereas , the
record of said votes and certificatesof the examining
board provided in said act were lost; now ,therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court of saidJeffersoncounty
shall, as soon afterthe passage of thisact as practica-
ble, cause to be posted up upon the door of the court-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1081
1
house of said county the followingnotice: “ (Giving
thereon the day upon which said notice was posted
up ) ten (10) days afterthe foregoing date upon which
this noticewas posted up the county court will hear
evidence as to whether or not the Cane run and Schar-
dine's precinctsof Jeffersoncounty did, at the elec-
tion held on the firstSaturday in June ,1882, adopt
the stock law for Jefferson and Oldham counties by a
majority of votes cast at said election. The members
of the examining board, and allpersons having infor-
mation on the subject, are hereby notifiedto appear
before said court ten (10) days afterthis notice,and
give evidenceas to said matter and matters pertinent
thereto ."
§2 . Whereupon itshallbe the duty of said county
court to hear evidence as to whether said act was
adopted by a majority of said votes cast at said poll
held on the firstSaturday in June , 1882, and allmat-
ters pertinent thereto. The court shall find in ac-
cordance with the facts, and shallorder itsjudgments
to be entered on record in lieu of said lost certificates
in the officeof the county clerkof Jeffersoncounty ,
togetherwith allpertinentmatters found or recitedin
saidjudgment .
§ 3. When said judgment is entered upon records
of the Jefferson county court, in the office of the
clerk of Jeffersoncounty, it shall be conclusiveof
the facts recited therein, and the requirements of
said act as to examining board and certificateare dis-
pensed with .
§ 4. This actisamendatory to said act, entitled"An
act to provide for a stock law for Jefferson and Old-
ham counties," approved April 15, 1882 ; and in case
that said Jeffersoncounty court shall find that said
stock law was adopted forsaid precinctsby a majority
of votes cast at aforesaidpoll, then said stock law
shall be and remain in full force and effect in said
precincts .
§ 5. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage . Approved April 10, 1888.

1082 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 963 .
AN ACT toempower the countycourtof Ballardcountyto levy a tax
forroad purposes, and to prescribethe manner of collectingand dis-
bursing same .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Ballard county , com-
posed of the county judge and a majority of the jus-
ticesof the peace in saidcounty,are hereby authorized
and empowered to annually levy an ad valorem tax
not exceeding fifteencentson each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of property in saidcounty subject to taxа-
tion for State revenue ,and a poll-tax not exceeding
one dollarupon each tithablein saidcounty, to be ap-
plied exclusivelyto the improvement of the public
highways therein.
§ 2. Said tax shall be certifiedto the sheriff or tax
collectorin the same manner that other county levies
are now certified; and it shallbe the duty of said
sheriffor tax collector, and he is hereby directed and
empowered to collectsaid tax as other county levies
are collected .
§ 3. The sheriffor tax collector is hereby directed
to pay saidtax,when collected,to the treasurerof said
county at the same times and in the same proportions
as other county leviesare now directedby law to be
collectedand paid, and the sheriffor collectorishere-
by made responsibleupon hiscounty levybond forsaid
tax as fullyas he isforany othercounty levy; and he
and his suretiesmay be proceeded againstupon the
same grounds and in the same way thatthey are liable
to for any defalcationin respectto any other county
levy .
§4 . The treasurerof Ballard county shall keep a
separate and accurate account of allmoney charge-
able to the sheriff or collector under this act, in the
manner that the account of other county levies are

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1083
required to be kept ; and he shall also keep an
accurate account of all money received by himself
on account of the levy herein provided for; his
duty in referenceto thislevy being similarin allres-
pects to other county levies; and he and his sureties
upon his officialbond shall be liablefor all money
receivedunder this act to the same extent, and may
be proceded againstin the same manner provided for
in case of defalcationrespecting any other county
levy .
§ 5. The county court,composed of the county judge
and a majorityof the justicesof the peace insaidcoun-
ty, shalllay the county off into road districtsand
number them ; they shallthen divide the roads in each
districtinto sections, and number them , allof which
shall be done and entered of record .
§6 . The court shallthen proceed, in the manner
most expedient and satisfactoryto itself, to ascer-
tain the condition of the public roads in the sev-
eral districts, and the sections of roads in said
districts; and shall set apart to the creditof each
districtsuch specificsum , to be expended therein,
as the courtshallthink fairand just; and in making
thisdistributionthe amount of travel upon the vari-
ous roads, as wellas the conditionof the roads, shall
be borne in mind ; and it shall be the duty of the
court to so expend this money as to promote the gen-
eral welfare of the county .
§ 7. After the court has determined upon the
amount of money to be expended in any district,
they shall next determine what road and what sec-
tion of road shallfirstbe improved ; and they shall
then ascertain specificallythe character and extent
of improvement to be done upon such sectionas they
have decided to improve ; and shall appoint a com-
missioner to let such improvement to the lowest and
best bidder ; and shall direct said commissioner as to
when and where said work shallbe let,and the mode
and manner of advertising same .

1084 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 8. Each sectionshall be let separately, but any
person may bid on and become the contractor for any
number of sections; but in all cases the contractor
shallenter intoa written contract, in which the work
to be done shallbe fullyand completely set out,and
the time in which said work is to be performed , and
the price to be paid therefor, shall be stated; and said
contract shall contain a covenant to the effect that the
contractor shall be responsible in damages in a sum at
leastequal to fiftyper cent.of the contractprice, for
any failureupon his part to comply with his under-
taking as thereinexpressed. Said contract shall be
signed by the contractorand his sureties, if any re-
quired, in the presence of the commissioner, and
attestedby him ; and he shall, within ten days after
the contracthas been executed, return same to the
county court clerk, who shall file the same in his
office,and of which he shall thereafter be the lawful
custodian .
§ 9. No money shallbe due or paid upon such con-
tractuntilthe work is fullycompleted, nor until the
commissioner with whom said contract was made shall
indorse upon the back of such contractthat he has
carefullyexamined the work , and thatthe same is,in
allrespects,completed accordingto the terms of said
contract; but when saidbond isso indorsed,the clerk
shall issue his order upon the treasurerfor the amount
of the contractprice, and the same shall be due and
payable upon presentation, and when paid, it shall
be receiptedby the person to whom itwas paid, and
retained by the treasurer as a voucher ; but said treas-
urer shall not pay out the road fund except upon such
order of the clerk, or upon order of the county court.
§ 10. It shall be the duty of the county attorney to
prepare all contractsfor work under this contract,
and no commissioner shallaccept any contract until
the same has been examined , and , as to form, ap-
proved by said attorney.
§ 11. No commissioner appointed under this act

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1085
15
shallhave or acquire any interestwhatever in any
contract which he or any other commissioner ap-
pointed hereunder shall make for work upon the
roads of said county ; and before any such commis-
sioner shall enter upon the duties of his office, he
shallappear in the county court and take an oath to
the effectthat he willfaithfullyobserve and perform
his duties as such commissioner , and the fact of his
taking said oath shallbe noted of record; and ifsuch
commissioner shall willfullyfailto observe and per-
form his duty ,he may be indictedfor falseswearing,
and , upon conviction,shallbe punished as now pro-
vided for such offense .
§ 12. The power to levy the tax herein provided for
is altogether discretionary with the county court ; but
if levied, this act shallin allother respectsbe com-
plied with, and in the event of a levy, all other laws
providing for the working of public roads shall be,
and they are hereby, repealed,in so faras they apply
to Ballard county .
§ 13. Nothing in this act shall be construed to pre-
vent the county court from using any other fund
under their control in repairing and improving
roads, but may , when they ascertainthat there is in
the hands of the treasurer an unexpended balance
arisingfrom any source,directhim to transferit to
the credit of the road fund , to be used as any other
fund raised under this act .
§ 14. If any commissioner under this act shall
move from the county, or become mentally or phys-
ically unable to examine any work , or make the
proper indorsement upon contractsmade with him ,
this fact shall be suggested of record in the county
court, who shall appoint some one to perform said
duties , who shall be sworn as other commissioners .
§ 15. The county court may make the firstlevy
under this act at any call term held not later than
the firstMonday in June , 1888, but all subsequent

1086 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
levies shall be made at the same time that other
county leviesare made .
§ 16. This act shall take effectand be inforcefrom
and afteritspassage.
Approved April10,1888.

F. Gillhan, Ben P. Easton, J. E. McCracken , Jame

mittee of three members , and such other officersas
shall from time to time seem necessary to the societv.
§ 3. The foregoing officersshall be chosen from

created for the use of the society.
§ 4. This societyshallnot, in itscorporatecapacity,
hold real estateexceeding in value at any one time
the sum of one hundred thousand dollars .
