sion of its members ,for government of the same , for
the election, changing the officersabove named , and
for the general regulation and management of its
69 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 2 .

1090 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
affairs, shallhave power to form a code of by -laws,
not inconsistent with the laws of this State or of the
United States,which code, when formed and adopted
at a regularmeeting,shall,untilmodifiedor rescinded,
be equally binding as this act upon the society, its
officersand members .
§ 6. The policeforceof the citiesof Covington and
Newport , as well as of allother placesin this State
where societiesfor the prevention and repressionof
actsof crueltyexist, or may hereafterbe established,
and where policeorganizationsexist, and allsheriffs,
constablesand justicesof the peace, shall, as occasion
may require,aid the society,itsmembers or agents,in
the enforcement of alllaws which are now or may
hereafter be enacted for the prevention of cruelty.
§ 7. The said society may appoint agents in any
county of thisState where no activesocietyexists,to
represent the society, and receive and account for all
funds coming to this societyfrom membership dues,
donationsor any other source,and may also appoint
agentsat large to prosecute the work of saidsociety
throughout the State.
§ 8. The agents of said society, and allothersocie-
tiesheretoforeor hereafterorganizedunder the pro-
visions of this act, shall have power to arrest any
person found violatingany law for the preventionor
repressionof cruelty; and upon making such arrest
shall forthwith convey the person arrested before
some court or magistratehaving jurisdictionof the
offense, and there make complaint againstthem ; but
said agents shall not be authorized to make such
arrestswithin any municipal coporation unless their
appointment has been approved by the mayor , coun-
cilor policecommissioners thereof, nor within any
county beyond the limitsof a municipal corporation,
unless their appointment has been approved by the
county judge of said county ; and the mayor , coun-
cil,policecommissioners or county judge shallkeep
a record of all such appointments .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1091
§ 9. Branches of the society, consisting of not less
than ten members ,may be organized in any part of
the State to prosecute the work of the society in their
severallocalities, under rulesand regulationspre-
scribed by the society.
§ 10. Such associationsmay electsuch officersand
make such rules, regulations and by -laws as may be
deemed necessary or expedient by their members for
theirown government and the proper management of
their affairs, said laws not conflictingwith the laws of
this State or of the United States .
§ 11. Such associationsmay appoint agents for the
purpose of prosecuting any person guiltyof any act
of cruelty to persons or animals within this State,
who shall have power to arrest any person found vio-
latingany of the provisionsof this act, or any other
law for the purpose of protectingpersonsor animals,
or preventing any act of cruelty thereto; and upon
making such arrest, such agent shallconvey the per-
son so arrestedbeforesome courtor magistratehaving
jurisdictionof theoffense,withinthe municipalcor-
porationor county wherein the offensewas committed ,
and thereforthwithmake complaint,on oath or affirm-
ation, of the offense; but allappointments by such
associationsunder thissectionmust have the approval
of the mayor , council or policecommissioners of the
cityor town in which the associationexists; and if it
existsoutside of any city or town , the appointment
must be approved by the county judge of the county ;
and the mayor ,council,policecommissionersor county
judge shallkeep a record of allsuch appointments.
§ 12. Any officer, agent or member of any such as-
sociationmay interfereto prevent the perpetrationof
any act of crueltyto childrenor animals in his pres-
ence,and may use such forceas may be necessaryto
prevent the same , and to that end may summons to
his aid any by -standers.
§ 13. Any sheriff, marshal, policeman, constable, or
agent of any societyfor the prevention of crueltyto

1092 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
animals, may killor cause to be killed any animal
found neglected or abandoned , and which , in the
opinionof threereputable citizens,is injured or dis-
eased past recovery , or by age has become useless.
§ 14. All finescollected under the laws for the pre-
ventionof crueltyto childrenor animals shallbe paid
to any duly incorporated society for the prevention
and repressionof cruelty. But any such societyinsti-
tuting any such prosecutionshall be entitledto re-
ceive allfinescollectedfrom such prosecution .
§ 15. This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved April 10,1888.

CHAPTER 972 .
AN ACT topreventtrespassingon realestatein the countyof Jeffer-
son .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . No one shallknowingly and unlawfully throw
down or leave open any bars, gate or fence upon the
land of another .
§ 2. No person shall enter upon the inclosed land of
another without permissionof the owner ,occupant or
person in charge thereof,for the purpose of hunting,
trapping, fishing, taking or destroying the nests or
eggs of birds, or gatheringnuts, fruitsor berries.
§ 3. Violation of any provisionsof the preceding
sectionsof thisactshallbe punished by a fineof not
lessthan one dollarnor more than fiftydollars; or in
default of payment of the fine, by imprisonment not
exceeding twenty days .
§ 4. The owners of any land,with such othersas he
may command to assisthim , may arreston such land
any person violatingthisact,and forthwith take him

1094 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
before any justiceof the peace or proper magistrate
for prosecution according to law .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage,but shallonly apply to the county of Jefferson.

CHAPTER 974 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Proctor , and reduce
into one the several acts thereto .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The boundary of Proctor shall be as follows :
Beginning at low water -mark on south side of the
Kentucky river, at the mouth of Knob branch, at the
lower end of the Samuel Johnson farm ,running with
the meanders of Knob branch to where the Booneville
road crosses said branch ; thence with said road to
where the said road forks to go to Proctor and Con-
gleton shoals; thence in the neareststraightline to
the South Fork of the Kentucky river; thence down
the South Fork of Kentucky riverwith the low water-
mark to the mouth ; thence with the Kentucky river
with the low water -mark to the beginning .
§ 2. The fiscal, prudential and municipal concerns
of said town shall be vested in five trustees, to be
styled the board of trusteesof Proctor, allof whom
shall be electedannually, on the firstSaturday of
April,by the qualifiedvotersof said town , who shall
have resided thereinat leastsixty days next before
said election . The trustees shall hold their office for
one year, and until their successorsare elected and
qualified; they shall, beforethey proceed to act, take
the officialoath prescribedby the Constitution and
laws of the State, which may be administeredby any
person authorized by the laws of the Stateto admin-
isteran oath . The officeradministering the oath shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1095
certifythe same to the board of trustees, and such
certificateshallbe spread on the recordsof the board
of trustees. They shall select one of their number a
chairman of the board , whose duty it shall be to pre-
side at all meetings of the board ; give the casting
vote in case of a tie; preserve order and decorum ;
callthe board togetherwhen emergency may demand ,
and see that allrulesand regulationsconcerningtheir
meetings are enforced. In case a vacancy shall occur
in the board of trustees, they may fillsuch vacancy
by appointment until theirnext election. No person
shallbe a trusteewho isnot,at the time,overtwenty-
one years of age, and who has not residedthereinfor
at leastsix months next beforehiselectionor appoint-
ment .
§ 3. The board of trusteesof said town , and their
successorsin office, shallbe a body -politicand corpo-
rate, and shall be known by the name and style of
the Board of Trustees of Proctor ; and by that name
may sue and be sued, contract and be contracted
with ; they may take and hold real estate or personal
property by purchase, devise, bequest or donation,
for the use and benefitof saidtown , and may leaseor
selland convey the same , and may selland convey
any property, real or personal, owned by them : Pro-
vided,however, They shallnot leaseor sellany such
property except by a vote of a majority of the board
of trustees; and the names of those voting for the
leaseor sale of such property shallbe entered upon
the records of said board of trustees .
§ 4. The board of trusteesmay fixby theirby -laws
the time and placeof theirregular meetings, and the
mode of specialmeetings. A majority of said trus-
tees shall constitute a quorum to transact business.
They may provide for punishing those who failto
attend the meetings, without a reasonable excuse, by
fines. In the absence of the chairman they may elect
one of their number chairman for the occasion . The
officerpresiding shallsign the minutes of the meet

1096 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ings,when approved by the board ; and shallhave all
the powers on that occasion as the regular chairman .
The board of trusteesshallhave power to provide by
by -laws for punishing any one guilty of disorderly
conduct in their presence while sitting.
§5 . That the titlesof the streets, alleys, and all
publicgrounds in said town ,shallbe,and ishereby ,
vested in the board of trustees of said town , and they
may maintain any appropriate action to prevent any
damage , obstructionor injuryto saidstreets, alleysor
otherpublicgrounds,shade trees, ornamental trees,or
other thing or fixturesplaced there by them ; and
may compel the removal of obstructionsin any of the
streets, alleys,or public grounds ,or to recover dam-
ages for any injury hereafterdone to any of the
streets, alleys, public grounds, shade or ornamental
trees, thing or fixture. Said board shall have power
to build cisterns,dig wells,and to regulatethe storage
of gunpowder or othercombustible material,or mate-
rialof an unhealthy character; and they may pro-
hibit the sale of fire-crackers, roman candles, sky-
rockets, or any other articleof the kind within the
limits of said town .
§ 6. The said board of trusteesmay require chim-
neys, fluesor stove-pipes, which may endanger the
safetyof property,to be repairedso as to make them
safe, and may provide penaltiesfor a disobedienceof
their orders, and provide for repairing or removing
such danger or defects, at the costof the owner of the
property. They may require owners or occupiers of
property frontingon the streetsor alleysto keep the
streetsand alleysin front of their property clean ;
and may prevent the exhibition of stud -horses or
jacks on the streetsof saidtown , or the exhibitionof
any obscene or immoral show or pictures.
§ 7. Said board of trusteesshallhave power to open
new streets or alleys in said town , or may extend ,
straighten,or widen any of the presentstreetsor alleys
in said town ; or may closeany of the streetsor alleys

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1097
in said town that they may deem useless, the owner
of any land giving his consent; and in case they can
not agree with the owner of the ground required for
such new streetor alley, or for widening or straight-
ening old ones,they may filetheirpetitionin the Lee
county court against the owner or owners of the land
required,settingforththe boundary and locationof the
land required, on which petitionsummons shall be
issued and served on the owner or owners of said land ,
ifresidingin Lee county ; and ifnot,then on a known
agent or attorney of such owner or owners ; but if
there be no known agent or attorney , then the sum-
mons may be served on such owner or owners in any
county of thisState; but should such owner or owners
be non -residentsof thisState, they shallbe warned to
appear, and be provided against, as provided in the
Civil Code of Practice . When such owner or owners
shall be properly before the court, the court shall
directa writ of ad quod damnum to issue, which writ
shall be executed by the sheriff, and the value of the
land required for any such new street or alley, or
alterationor extension of an old one , shall have been
ascertained, the court shallenter judgment condemn-
ing such land,and ordering the new streetor alleyto
be opened , or the old one extended or altered: Pro-
vided, however, That no such order shall be made
until the trustees of said town have paid into the
court the amount of damages assessed and all cost of
the proceedings : Provided further , That the trustees
shall not be compelled to pay the damage assessed,
after the damages are assessed, they elect to do so ;
and they may , if they see proper, abandon the pro-
ceeding, being responsiblefor cost only ; but upon
theirdoing so,the court shallenteran order annulling
the order condemning the ground : Provided, how-
ever, Eitherparty may appeal from the finaljudgment
of the court, condemning the land or assessingthe
damage to be paid,to the Court of Appeals , if the
Court ofAppeals have jurisdictionof the amount ; but
1-

1098 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
no such appeal shall be taken after six months from
the finaljudgment appealed from .
§ 8. The board of trustees of said town shall have
power to cause any of the streetsor alleys of said town
to be graded,the owner of lands giving his consent,
paved or graveled, or otherwise improved ; they shall
have power to order, at the cost of the owners of
property fronting the streets, the sidewalks to be
paved or graveled , or otherwise improved ; and they
shall fix in their order the grades , width , character of
material to be used in curbing ,paving or other improve-
ment , and shall notify the owner , agent,or attorney
of such property, if they residein said town , of the
intended improvement ,giving them a reasonabletime
to make the same ; but upon their failure to do so
within the time specifiedin such notice, itshall then
be the duty of said trusteesto have the improvement
made at the expense of said town , and shall enter the
costof the same in a book to be kept by them for that
purpose, together with a fair descriptionof the lot
or lotsso improved , which cost of improvement shall
stand as a tax assessment and lienagainst the whole of
the lot or lotsso improved ,togetherwith such build-
ings as may be upon it; the electionof said tax assess-
ment and lienshallbe enforced by a saleof such lot
or lotsso described, but not untilsuch tax and assess-
ment has been presentedfor payment to the owner ,his
or her agent or attorney,provided such owner ,agent
or attorney resides in said town . Should said owner
not reside in said down , or have no agent or attorney
residingtherein, it shallnot be necessaryto give the
notice requiredby thissectionnor make the demand
hereinnamed . A copy of the assessmentand descrip-
tion of the property herein named , attestedby the
clerk of the board of trustees, shallbe authority to
the marshal of said town to make the sale herein
named . Said sale shall be made as likeproperty is
required to be sold for State and county tax by the
laws of this State ; and the owner or owners shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1099
have the same rightsto redeem saidproperty,by pay-
ing the assessment, cost of sale, and per cent. now
allowed by law in the collectionof the revenue and
county levy of the State. Said marshal shall return
the originalassessment, under which he made the
sale, together with his return, to the board of trus-
tees, which shallbe copied in fullby the clerkof the
board on the records of said board, in a book to be
kept by them . Should the owner or owners failto
redeem said lot or lotsor land within the time pre-
scribed,it shallbe the duty of said marshal, or his
successorin office,to make deed of conveyance to the
same to the purchaser, which deed shallpass fulland
complete titleto the same to the purchaser.
§ 9. That the board of trustees of said town shall
have the exclusive right and power to license, and
provide by ordinance for licensing auctioneers, the
saleof goods,wares and merchandise,of any descrip-
tions whatever ; livery and sale stables; all vehicles
running forhire; and shallhave the right to tax any
show or exhibition exhibiting in said town ; that the
tax on auctioneers shall not be less than five nor more
than ten dollarsper year ; that the tax on each person
or firmforthe sale of goods, wares and merchandise
shallnot be lessthan five nor more than twenty -five
dollarsper year ; that the tax on each liveryor sale
stable shall not be less than five nor more than ten
dollarsper year ; that the tax on each vehicle shall
not be less than two nor more than fivedollars per
year ; that the tax on each show , exhibitionor per-
formance shall not be less than two nor more than
twenty-fivedollarsforeach performance.
§ 10. That the board of trustees of said town may ,
by general or specialby -laws or ordinances, require
all vendors of spirituous, vinous or malt liquorsin
said town , to close their bar-rooms , and keep them
closed, and not to sellor give to any person on the
Sabbath day;and may requirebar-rooms tobe closed
and prevent the saleof spirituousliquorson any pub-
*

1100 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
licday as they may deem proper ; and may require
allbar-rooms to be closedat a specifiedhour at night,
not earlier than nine o'clock .
§ 11. That when a licenseis required under the
charter to carry on a business, or do an act, the pro-
duction of the treasurer'sreceiptsfor the amounts of
said tax or license to the board of trustees shall be
evidence to that said amount has been paid into the
treasury; and upon which they may or not, in their
discretion, issue a licenseto the holder by his exe-
cuting the bond required by law, if a bond shall be
required in such case; the order of said board of
trustees,entered on theirrecords, specifyingthe busi-
nessto be done,signed by the chairman and attested
by the clerk,shall be authorityand a licenseto the
holder to carry on the business or perform the act
therein named ; but in case the license is refused by
said board, the treasurershall refund the money to
the applicantby his returning said receiptsand de-
manding the same .
§ 12. The board of trustees of said town shall have
power , by suitableby -laws or ordinances, with penal-
tiesadequate forthe purpose,not,however , to exceed
one hundred dollars' fineor fiftydays' imprisonment ,
or both, to suppress tippling-houses, gaming -houses,
and to punish any one guilty of rioting, disturbing
the public peace, disorderlyor indecent conduct or
language, disturbingreligiousworship, schools, pub-
licspeaking or lectures, or any lawful publicmeeting,
injury to streets, alleyscr public grounds , shade or
ornamental trees, cisterns, wells, pumps , churches,
school-houses or other public buildings or property
belonging to said town or the county of Lee,includ-
ing bridges which are now or may hereafter be
erected; and to pass allsuch by laws or ordinances
as shall be necessary or proper to carry out any
power given by this charter, or for the suppression
and punishment of allsuch disordersand irregulari-
tiesas shall be prejudicialto the good order and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1101
quiet of said town . The by -laws and ordinancesnow
in force in said town shall remain until changed by
the trustees. No by -law hereafterpassed shall go
into effectuntil ten days afterthe same is posted at
the most public place in the said town ; but it shall
not be necessary, upon the trialof any one charged
with a violationof said by -laws, that this provision
has been complied with, unless such person, before
the trialis commenced ,demand such proof.
§13 . The board of trusteesmay levy and collect,
annually, a tax, not exceeding forty cents, on each
one hundred dollars' worth of property in saidtown ,
taxable under the revenue laws of this State, includ-
ing the amount owned by any person residingin said
town , which ought to be given in under the equali-
zation law ; and on such personal property in said
town owned by persons not residingin said town , as
shallbe employed by such person in businessin said
town ; they may alsolevy a poll or capitationtax, of
not more than two dollarsin any one year, on allthe
male inhabitants of said town over the age of twenty-
one years ; and a special tax on all dogs , over one to
the family, of not more than three dollarsin any one
year ; and alldogs permitted to stay at or about the
house of a family shallbe construed as belonging to
and owned by such family .
§ 14. The board of trustees of said town shall pub-
lish annually, at the expiration of the term of each
board , an account of all the money received by them
and all the payments made out of the treasury during
the year,and the amount of the debt of the town , and
the amount due the town , which statement shall be
verifiedby the chairman of the board .
§ 15. Each board of trusteesshallappoint a treas-
urer, whose term of officeshallbe one year,who shall
be over the age of twenty-one years, and of good
moral standing. They shall take from him bond ,
with approved security, conditionedfor the faithful
disbursement of all moneys received by him as or

1102 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dered by the board ; and that he will settlehis ac-
counts, and pay over to his successorin officeany
balance in his hands at the expiration of his term ;
and the trusteesmay require him , at any time, to
renew his bond , and on his failureto do so in five
days , may remove him . The marshal or other col-
lectingofficercollectingfines, money due for licenses,
tax or assessments , or any money received in the
name of the trusteesor for their use, shall pay the
same over to the treasurerwithin ten days aftercol-
lectingthe same , and take the treasurer'sreceipt
therefor, and on his failure to do so, he shall be re-
sponsibleon his bond for the amount so collected,
and twenty per cent. thereon. The treasurer shall
pay out no money except on the order of the board
of trustees,signed by the chairman of the board and
certifiedand attestedby the clerk.
§ 16. Any person desiring to test the validityof
any by -law or ordinance passed by said board of
trusteesmay do so by petitionin the Lee circuit
court against the board of trustees, setting forth
the particularby -law or ordinance believed to be
illegal, and statingthat if enforced it will affectthe
rights of plaintiff; and on filingsuch petitionan
injunctionmay be granted the plaintiff, as provided
by the Civil Code of Practice, and the cause shall
be docketed , and stand for trial on the third day
of the firstterm of said court after filingsaid peti-
tion: Provided, Said petitionbe filedtwenty days
before the beginning of said court. Either party
may appeal from the decision of the circuit court
to the Court of Appeals , without any regard to the
amount in controversy; but no such appeal shall
be taken after six months from the rendition of the
judgment : Provided , however, That upon the disso.
lution of the injunction, the plaintiffand his secur-
itiesshallbe liableon their bond for alldamages ,
including the amount of any license, fines, tax , or
assessmentsthat may have been prevented from be-
ing collectedby said injunction.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1103 :
§ 17. There shall be elected, on the firstSaturday
in April, 1888, and every two years thereafter, by
the qualifiedvoters of said town , a police judge ,
who shall hold his officefor two years, and until
his successor is elected and qualified. No person
shall be policejudge who is under twenty -one years
of age , and who has not been a resident of said
town for six months next preceding his electionor
appointment , nor a vendor of spirituousliquors or
retailby license,keeper or owner of a billiardsaloon
or bowling alley; and any person holding the office
of policejudge,who shall become a vendor of spirit-
nous liquorsby license,or keeper or owner of a bil-
liard saloon or bowling-alley, shall ipsofacto vacate
his office; and should the officeof policejudge be-
come vacant at any time, the trusteesof said town
shall appoint one until the next electionfor trustees
of said town , or until the next regular electionfor
police judge, whichever may occur first, at which
electiona police judge shall be elected, who shall
hold his office until the next regular election for
police judge of said town , and untilhis successoris
elected and qualified.
§ 18. The policejudge of said town , or,in his ab-
sence, the chairman of the board of trustees, shall
have exclusivejurisdictionof alloffensesagainstthe
by -laws or ordinances of said town , and the police
judge shall have concurrent jurisdictionwith the
county judge and justicesof the peace in all penal
and criminal offensescommitted in the county of
Lee ; and the policejudge shallhave concurrentjuris-
diction in allcivilcases with the justicesof the peace
of saidcounty ; he shallbe a conservatorof the peace
in said county,and shallbe commissioned by the Gov
ernor, and take a similaroath to that requiredby jus-
ticesof the peace, and execute bond as now required
by the laws of this State. The policecourt shall be
styledthe policecourt at Proctor, and is declared to
be a court of record, and shallhave allsuch power of

1104 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
preserving order, punishing contempts of its author-
ity,and enforcingobedience to itsmandate , precept,
and authoritythat is possessed by the county judge.
All finesimposed by the policecourt foroffensescom-
mitted in saidtown againstthe by -laws thereof, shall
be for the benefitof saidtown , and shallbe paid over
by the officercollectingthe same to the treasurerof
the board of trusteeswithin ten days thereafter. All
warrants issued by the policejudge of said town for
offensescommitted in said town against its by -laws,
which are cognizable, shall be in the name of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the benefitof the
trusteesof said town , and shallbe executed by the
marshal,or any other peace officerof Lee county , to
whom they are deliveredand returned by said officer
to the policecourt fortrial. Said police judge shall
have power to take bail for the appearance of per-
sons charged with offenseswithin its jurisdiction
at a subsequent day for trial, or indorsed on war-
rants issued by him , the sum the defendants may
be admitted to bail in, by the officermaking the
arrest, for his appearance on a day to be fixed by
such officer; and in case any defendant shallfailto
appear in dischargeof hisor her bail, may declarethe
same forfeited, and summons the defendant and his
or her securitiesto show cause why judgment should
not be rendered against them for the amount of the
bond ; and if no sufficientreason is shown within ten
days after the service of the summons , may give
judgment for the amount ,on which judgments a cа-
pias pro finemay issue; and when collected,the same
shallbe paid to the town treasurer: Provided,how-
ever, That the bail required of the defendant shall
in no case exceed the amount of the finewhich might
be imposed . His court shall be open at alltimes for
the trialsof infractionof the by -laws and ordinances
of saidtown , or the trialof criminalor penal causes.
He shallhold a court on the second Saturday in each
month for the trialof civilcases, and may adjourn

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1105
the same until Monday , and from that to other days,
until the business of the term is completed : Pro-
vided, however, That his jurisdictionin civilcases
shall not be construed to go beyond the town lim-
its, except the defendant consents in writing, in-
dorsed on the summons or warrants, and signed by
himself: And provided further, That the process or
summons must be served fivedays before the com-
mencement of the term . Copies of his records, at-
tested by himself or his successor in office, shall be
received in evidence as copies of other records are
now admissible under the law .
§ 19. The policejudge may charge and collectthe
following fees, which shall be due and distrainable
as feesof justicesof the peace now are: For issuing
a warrant for an infractionof the by -laws or ordi-
nances of the town , or criminal or penal laws, one
dollar; forswearing a jury and presidingat the trial,
two dollars; for presidingat a trialwhere no jury is
required, one dollar; for each subpœna for one or
more witnesses, twenty-five cents; for recognizance
or bond to keep the peace, to be paid by the appli-
cant, one dollar; and for all other serviceshe shall
be allowed the same fees that are allowed to justices
of the peace for similar services.
§ 20. Either party may appeal from a judgment of
the policecourt to the Lee circuitcourt, in any case,
as now provided by law ; such party would have the
right to appeal from the judgment of the quarterly
court or justicesof the peace; also to the quar-
terly court as is now allowed from justices' court.
The appeal shall be taken in the same manner , with-
in the same time , and on the same conditions, as from
the quarterlycourt or justiceof the peace. The po-
licejudge shallhave power to enforce the laws of
the Stateagainst vagrants, gypsies and peddlers who
shall offend against the laws of this State; against
vagrants, gypsies and peddlers within the limits of
70- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 2.

1106 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said town , and to require bonds to keep the peace
and be of good behavior .
§ 21. There shallbe electedon the firstSaturday in
April, 1888, and on the firstSaturday in April every
two years thereafter, by the qualifiedvoters of said
town, a marshal, who shall hold his officefor two
years, and untilhissuccessoriselectedand qualified.
The marshal shall, within ten days after his election,
in addition to the oath required by the Constitution
and laws of thisState, take an oath faithfullyto dis-
charge allhisdutiesunder this act and the by -laws of
the board of trusteesof said town , and execute bond,
with security, approved by the board of trustees, con-
ditioned for the faithfulperformance of his duties;
and that he will in due time collectand pay over to
the proper person alltaxes, fines, assessments, forfeit.
ures or other dues of said town placed in his hands for
collection, and make finalsettlement with the board
at the expirationof hisoffice; and that he willin good
faith, to the best of his skilland judgment , execute
allsuch dutiesas may be lawfullyrequired of him by
the board of trustees, as are imposed by thisact. The
marshal shallexecute allprocessto him directedfrom
the policejudge or other officer, forbreaches of the
by -laws or ordinancesof saidboard of trustees, or for
any offensecommitted in said town ; and he may
execute process issued by any officerin the Common-
wealth of Kentucky for an infractionof the criminal
or penal laws of this State, and for these purposes
may go to any part of Lee county ; and said marshal
shallhave concurrent jurisdictionwith constablesof
said county in allcivilcases: Provided , however,He
shallhave no power to execute civilprocess untilhe
shallhave enteredintobond , with approved security,
in the county court, in the same manner that consta-
blesare required to do . He shallhave allthe powers
to levy, distrain, and sellfor all taxes,fines, assess-
ments, or forfeitures, or other dues of the board of
trustees, that sheriffshave in the collection of the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1107
revenue or county levy, and shallhave the same com-
pensation,or any additionalcompensation the board
of trusteesmay allow; and upon hisfailureto collect
and pay over,he and his securitiesshallbe liablein
the same manner ,and proceeded againstin the same
way ,thatconstablesareproceededagainstin thisCom-
monwealth . The marshal shallhave the power to sell
any real estate to pay any town tax or assessment
against its owner , in the same manner , and on the
same terms , that sheriffshave to sellfor State revenue
and county levy,when he shallbe unable to find per-
sonalproperty in saidtown to pay the same ,and shall
returna copy of the authorityunder which he made
the levy and sale, with his returnindorsed thereon, to
the clerk of the county court of Lee county, who
shall record the same in the same manner as sales for
taxes under the revenue laws of the State are required
to be recorded,and shallbe entitledto thesame fees
therefor. Such realestateshallbe subjectto redemp-
tion in the same manner , and in the same time , and
on the same terms as real estate sold for revenue
and county levy; and if not redeemed in one year,
shall be conveyed to the purchaser by the marshal,
in the same manner as real estateis conveyed by
the sheriff; and for making such deed the marshal
may charge and collecttwo dollars from the pur-
chaser. The originalauthority under which the sale
was made shall be returned to the clerk of the board
of trustees,and by him recorded in full,togetherwith
the marshal's return in a book to be kept by the
board for that purpose .
§ 22. The marshal of said town may appoint a dep-
uty , who shall residein the town of Proctor. The
marshal shall be responsibleon his officialbond for
the acts of his deputy ; but such deputy shall have
jurisdictiononly in cases of infractionsof the by-
laws and ordinances of the board of trustees . No
person shallbe eligibleto the officeof town marshal
unless he be at leasttwenty-one years old at the time

1108 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of his electionor appointment ; but this provision
shallnot apply to hisdeputy .
§ 23. It shallbe the duty of the marshal to arrest
allpersonswho may , at any time,be guiltyof a vio-
lationof any of the provisionsof the by -laws or ordi-
nances of saidtown , or of the charter, respectingthe
good order, peace and tranquilityof said town , or
any of itsinhabitants,and when necessary, he may
callto his aid any or allthe male inhabitantsof said
town , and all others who may be in the town , to
enable him to make arrestsor quell disturbances ; and
any inhabitant or person who shall, on being sum-
moned by the marshal to aid him in the execution of
his office, refuse to obey him and render assistance,
shall be fined not less than two nor more than ten dol-
lars for each offense, recoverable in the police court.
He may , at alltimes, arrestdrunk or disorderlyper-
sons; and he may commit to the jailof Lee county
any person who may be found in said town drunk or
disorderly, between dark in the evening and daylight
in the morning ; and the jailerof said county is au-
thorizedand requiredto receivesuch person into, and
confinehim or her in the jailuntilmorning ; but he
shall bring such person before the police judge or
chairman of the board of trustees, or, in theirab-
sence, before the county judge of Lee county, or a
justiceof the peace for said county, before twelve
o'clocknext morning ; and if no one appears against
such person,he or she shallbe discharged; or if none
of the aforesaid officersbe found , the marshal shall
discharge such person . The marshal shall attend all
the sittingsof the policecourt, and may be required
by the board of trusteesto attend any or allof their
meetings, and to perform other services, not incon-
sistentwith the nature of his office, as they may pre-
scribeby the by -laws .
§ 24. In casethe policejudge or marshal shallfail
to give bond , as requiredby thisact,within ten days
after the former receives his commission and the lat-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1109
=
ter his certificateof election, he so failingshall for-
feit his office, and the same shall be declared vacant
by a resolutionof the board of trustees; or if either
remove from saidtown his officeshall, in likemanner ,
be declaredvacant ; should the officeof marshal,from
any cause,become vacant,the board of trusteesshall
fillthe same by appointment , and the appointee shall
be required to take a similar oath and execute a simi-
larbond to that required in this act,and shall hold
his officeuntil the next electionof trustees, or regular
electionforpolicejudge and marshal, whichever may
occur first, and shall hold his officeuntil his successor
is elected and qualified.
§ 25. The board of trusteesshallannually appoint
one town assessor, who shall take a listof property
in said town , taxable under section fifteen of this
charter, and to fixa just and fairvaluationupon all

CHAPTER 990 .
AN ACT to authorizeand empower the Livingstoncounty courtto
levy annuallyan ad valorem tax in saidcountyforcountypurposes.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court of Livingston county,a
majority of the justicesof the peace of said county be-
ing presentand concurring therein, be, and ishereby,
authorizedand empowered to levy at each annual ses-
sion an ad valorem tax of not exceeding ten cents on
each one hundred dollars' worth of taxable property
in saidcounty in any one year ; and the money , when
so raised,to be applied to the generalexpenses ofsaid
county. Said tax shallbe collectedby the sheriffas
the other taxes are now collected ; and the sheriff of
saidcounty shallbe accountable for the same on the
bond that he executes annually for the collectionof
the county levy tax for each year .
§ 2. This act shall not be construed so as to prevent
the county court from levying an ad valorem tax, as
now authorizedby law,for the purpose of paying the
amount due W. W. Stewart by said county .
§ 3. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 12, 1888.

and after itspassage .

districtNo. 15, of Magoffin county, consistingof
sixty-fivescholarsin the schoolage, for the fullterm
of fivemonths , for the school year ending June 30,
1888, under the supervisionof three trustees, as re-
quired by law, which schoolwas regularlyreportedto
the superintendent of common schools for Magoffin

county . But through some informalityof the teach-
er'scertificatethe county superintendent refused and
stillrefusesto make the proper report to enable said
districtto draw the amount due it for said year ;
therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-

forthisCommonwealth be, and he is hereby, directed

amount that said school districtNo. 15, of Magoffin

Commonwealth under any law of the United States,
when discounted by this corporation shall be, and
are hereby,put upon the footing of foreign billsof
exchange ; and like remedy may be had thereon,
jointly and severally, against the makers , drawers
and indorsers .
§ 8. Said corporationmay make advances on,and
receivein pledge for money loaned or debts due it,
securitiesof any kind, goods, wares and merchan
dise, upon agricultural, mechanical or other products,
including growing crops and produce of the country,
and may sellthe same on the non -payment of the
debt or demand according to the terms agreed upon
between the parties by written contract at the time of
creation of the debt ; but before the sale or disposal
of any of the property, pledges or securitiesmen-
tioned in this charter, the party making the same or
depositingthe pledgeshallhave ten days'notice,in
writing, if a resident of this State, or if not a resi-
dent, the company , before proceeding to sellor dis-
pose of the property or pledge or security,shallcause
noticeto be insertedin some newspaper published in
the county for at leasttwenty days ; but nothing in
this act shall be construed to confer power or author-
ityon saidcorporationto sellor disposeof realestate,

or any interesttherein, so given in pledge or security

§ 9. No certificatesof stock shall be issued under Certificates.
this charter until the shares of stock subscribed for
shallbe paid in full; upon payment of callson stock
entriesshallbe made in a book kept for that purpose,
and receiptsexecuted to the subscriber for the amount
paid,signed by the presidentand cashier.
§ 10. Said corporationmay acquire, hold and use Realestate.
allsuch real estate, goods and chattels, as may be
necessaryforthe transactionof itsbusiness, or which
may be conveyed to it as securityfor any debt, or
purchased in satisfactionof any debt, judgment or
decree, and selland convey the same ; but when it
purchases real estatein satisfactionof any judgment
or payment of any debt due to it, it shallhold the
same not longerthan fiveyears ;and may buy itsown
stock in payment of a debt, judgment or decree,but
shall resellthe same as soon as practicallyconven-
ient. A conveyance, duly executed by the pres-
ident, shallpass the titleof said corporation to any
real estate it may want to dispose of.
§ 11. Said corporation may receive deposits from Deposits.
minors and married women ,and the receiptsand ac-
quittances of such minors and married women shall
be valid .
§ 12. Every stockholder shallbe individuallyliableLiability.
to the creditors of the corporation to the full amount
of the stock subscribed for and owned by him ; but
when the same shall have been paid for in full, then
no further individual liabilityattaches to the said
stockholders .
§ 13. It shall not be lawful for the president or di-
rectors to become surety or accommodation indorser
on any note or billdiscounted by said company or
corporation .
§ 14. No agreement, contract, obligation, deed or
other writing, shall be invalid for the want of the
sealherein provided for,if the same be regular and
legal in other respects.

Oath .
§ 15. The president, directorsand other officersof
said corporation, before entering on the discharge
of theirduties,shalltake an oath beforesome justice
of the peace , notary public , or other officerauthorized
by law to administeroaths,faithfully, honestly, and
to the best of theirskilland judgment to discharge
all their duties of their respective officesand stations,
and under this charter, or which may be required of
them by the by -laws. rules and regulations, of the
corporation; and that they will not, during their
continuance in office,sanction or permit any of the
provisionsof thischarterto be violated; and should
any occur without their concurrence, that they will
immediately report the same to the Attorney-General
of this Commonwealth .
§ 16. Said corporationmay be appointed and may
Guardian,&c. act as guardian for infants, as executors or adminis-
trators, or committee for idiotsor lunatics, and re-
ceivers, assigneeor other trustees, whether appointed
by deed, by last will and testament, by any court
within this Commonwealth , or in any other manner
not inconsistent with law . It may receive and hold
in trust estate, real and personal, including notes,
bonds, obligationsand accounts of estatesand indi-
viduals, of companies and corporations, private or
public; and may , in itsfiduciarycapacity, purchase,
collectand adjust and settle, selland dispose of the
same in this Stateor elsewhere. It may act as agent
or attorneyin the leasing,conveyingand managing
real and personal estate, receiving and collecting
rents and other moneys ,in issuing and countersign-
ing certificatesof stock, bonds or other obligations
of any corporationor municipality, State or public
authority, and may receive and manage any sink-
ing fund thereof; and may accept and execute and
guaranteeallsuch trusts, of every description, not
inconsistentwith the laws of this State, and may
be committed to it by any other person, or by any
corporation,or by any courtof recordof thisState.
